page_content:
FOREWORD

The subject “MAINTENANCE OF PUBLIC PEACE AND ORDER” is
essential for Police Officers to understand the challenges faced by the police
and to understand the social milieu and rights of the people with whom they
have to work. The reading material has been updated and freshly compiled
incorporating the concept and deeper understanding of the subject as
prevalence of peace is an essential pre-requisite for the overall development,
progress and prosperity of the nation.

This book consists of VII Modules covering the topics role of police in
preservation of public peace and maintenance of order, handling violent and
non-violent L&O situations, crowd control, religious processions, communal
riots, election bandobast, disaster management, traffic management and
enforcement of laws.

page_content:
I appreciate the dedicated work done by Smt. C. Anasuya, IPS, Deputy
Director, Induction, in bringing out this book. She has gone through the
syllabus and ensured that no topic is left out while compiling the book with
the assistance of Sri. Satish Kumar, DSP, TSPA.

I am sure this book will help the cadet trainees in understanding the
police work keeping the rights of the people in view. They will also get to
know the police organization and the service rules and conduct rules under
which they are expected to work while discharging their duties as police
officers.

page_content:
MAINTENANCE OF PUBLIC PEACE AND ORDER

MODULE - I
PUBLIC PEACE AND ORDER

1. CONCEPT AND IMPORTANCE OF PUBLIC ORDER AND
PEACE

Public order is synonymous with peace, safety and tranquility of the
community.

“Enforcing rule of law, and maintaining public order, are inseparable
and they form the bedrock of a civilized society and sound liberal
democracy”.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
“Enforcing rule of law, and maintaining public order, are inseparable
and they form the bedrock of a civilized society and sound liberal
democracy”.

In a democratic polity, which is founded on the bedrock of rule of law,
maintenance of peace and order assumes paramount importance. Public
order is synonymous with peace, safety and tranquility of the community.
Maintenance of public order is a core function of governance. The Indian
Constitution, while according a pre-eminent position for the fundamental
rights of citizens, recognizes the importance of public order, by providing for
legislation imposing reasonable restrictions in the interest of public order.
Under the Constitution of India, the Union and the States have well-defined
areas of responsibility. ‘Public Order' and 'Police' are essentially the
responsibilities of State Governments. However, the Central Government
assists them by providing Central Paramilitary Forces (CPMFs) as and when
required.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
How early symptoms of disorder should be detected and addressed well
in time? What should be the role of various stakeholders in maintenance of
public order? How the enforcement machinery should be made more effective
to deal with public disorder? What are the causes of conflicts and their
resolution, what is the role of civil administration, media, society, judiciary
and NGOs in maintaining public order, and thirdly the role of police, is the
subject for study here.

Importance of maintaining public order:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Importance of maintaining public order:

India today is poised to emerge as a global economic power with its high
growth rate of economy and all-round economic development. For realizing
our legitimate aspirations of economic development, it is essential that the
problems of peace and order are managed efficiently. No developmental
activity is possible in an environment of insecurity and disorder. Failure to
manage the multifarious problems arising out of violent conflicts based on
religious, caste, ethnic, regional or any other disputes, can lead to unstable
and chaotic conditions. Such conditions not only militate against realization

1| Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
of our economic dream, but also would jeopardize our survival as a vibrant
democracy. We have to look at the problem of public order management
and the role of law enforcement in that regard. Further, In this
perspective, we should not forget that it is the weaker sections which suffer
the most in any public disorder.

Whose responsibility is to maintain public order- Undoubtedly, it is the
role of the police, as the principal law enforcement agency to preserve public
order. The judiciary also has a vital role in preserving public order. But there
are other agencies within the governmental set up which have to contribute
towards preserving public order. It is the police which bear the brunt of
violations of the Laws and also the ensuing violence, but addressing the root
cause is beyond their purview.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
If one looks at the causes of public disorder, there are a host of reasons.
These may be broadly classified into socio-economic, political, historical and
administrative. Our response to public disorder should commence at the
very initial stage, and it is here that the role of entire civil administration
becomes important.

The period after independence has seen a tremendous increase in
spread of education and alongside has increased the awareness among the
people and so also the aspirations. And when these aspirations are not met,
tensions prevail within the society, which have the potential of erupting as a
problem of public disorder.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Role of the Police: The role of the police, as the principal law enforcement
agency is to preserve public order. The police work is aimed at ensuring
peaceful and orderly life in a community through faithful observance of the
laws with a view to upholding the quality of life for the citizens. Police
should always be impartial. Police administration needs interaction with
other governmental agencies, stakeholders and Public to maintain public
order.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Police must make a note of all the complaints given by different sections
involved in clash and ensure that the accused must be produced before the
Court, after proper investigation For the quick disposal of cases, the other
documents required, must be collected as soon as possible and charge sheet
filed in the concerned Court. If the accused gives counter complaints out of
retaliation, they should be enquired properly, to take necessary action. Police
should proceed to the place of incident immediately, without any negligence
or callousness and take up the investigation. The attitude of Police must
establish confidence and respect towards Police and Judiciary, in the minds
of the people.

2|Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Role of the Judiciary:

Access to justice is fundamental to the ‘Rule of Law”. If the citizens feel
hat access to justice is delayed or is not effective, they get alienated. If they
eel that punishment is meted out to the wrong doers only in a few cases,
hey have a tendency to disassociate them with the judicial process and stop
aking interest as a complainant, a witness or a mediator /pancha.

bok

tot

Sometimes a landlord looking to get a recalcitrant tenant out, a tenant
eeking protection from an avaricious landlord, families involved in property
isputes, start by thinking that gangsters (goondas) can settle their
problems which the courts are going to take ages to do. If this becomes
common, criminality will be a substitute for ‘Rule of Law’. We have to ensure
that we do not reach such a precarious situation. Criminals should be
intimidated by what Shakespeare called "The awful majesty of the law",
otherwise they will become a law unto themselves.

a

Role of the Media:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
a

Role of the Media:

The importance of a free press, for a healthy democracy cannot be
overemphasized. More importantly, it is a sort of check and balance that
keeps the authorities on their toes on the one hand and a type of mirror that
enables people in authority to get a continuous reality check, on the other.
This remains an important role for the media, despite the increasing
pressures of commercialization. Energetic media is in fact our best early
warning system.

But what is essential is that the media should play a responsible role. It
has been observed that at times, a part of the media has not been quite
objective in their reporting. Sometimes the media also plays a role in
spreading prejudices, as they are more interested in sensational news rather
than sensible constructive news. The potential of media can be tapped in
maintaining public order.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Role of the Society, NGOs: A democratic society cannot function properly if
everything in it is left only to the State or even to statutory bodies. Because
of the increasing complexities of life everything cannot be taken care of by
the Governmental institutions. The gap has to be filled up by the civil
society. Government intervention itself will be in fructuous if it is not
underpinned by voluntary action. Moreover the exercise of political power
through civil society opens the way for democracy in real sense of the
term. Civil society consists of open and secular institutions that mediate
between the citizen and the State. The modern idea of civil government
requires emergence of civil society which would make people self-reliant
rather than remain dependent on State institutions. Participation by the civil
society makes the citizens active agents instead of becoming merely passive
recipients of welfare.

3 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Conclusion:

Development and security are truly mutually inter-related. We need
therefore, to evolve a combined strategy to deal simultaneously with the twin
challenges of development and security within the framework of a
democratic polity committed to respect for all fundamental human freedoms
and also committed to upholding the rule of law. Internal conflict
management is the key to the success of participative democracy,
strengthening national solidarity. Violations of public order, given their
socio-economic, political and administrative causes demand a concerted
response from different wings of the civil administration. When this is done
at the nascent stage itself, minor discords can be prevented from turning
into major public disorders. The challenge lies in institutionalizing a
mechanism so that all wings of the civil administration as well as other
stakeholders work in a coordinated fashion.

A. REGULAR EVENTS

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
A. REGULAR EVENTS

Traffic jams/ strikes/ Dharnas/ Gheraos/ Fairs & Festivals/ Prayers/
Rallies/ Processions/ VIP visits.

TRAFFIC JAM is a condition on transport networks that occurs as use of
vehicles increases and is characterized by slower speeds, longer trip times
and increased vehicular queuing. When vehicles are fully stopped for period
of time, this is colloquially known as a traffic jam.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Traffic congestion can lead to drivers becoming frustrated and engaging
in road rage. Traffic congestion occurs when volume of traffic generates
demand for space greater than the available road capacity. This point is
commonly termed saturation. Most of the Traffic congestion is attributed to
traffic incidents, road work and weather events. Traffic research still cannot
fully predict under which conditions “traffic jam” may suddenly occur. It has
been found that individual incidents (such as accidents or even a single car
braking heavily in a previously smooth flow) may cause ripple effects which
then spread out and create a sustained traffic jam.

Traffic congestion has a number of negative effects:
“ Wasting time of motorists and passengers (“opportunity cost”).
“ Delays, which may result in late arrival for employment, meetings, etc.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
“ Inability to forecast travel time accurately, leading to drivers allocating
more time to travel "just in case", and less time on productive activities.

“ Wasted fuel increasing air pollution and carbon dioxide emissions
owing to increased idling, acceleration and braking.

“Frequent acceleration and braking, leading to more frequent repairs and
replacements.

4|Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
“ Stressed and frustrated motorists, leading to road rage.

“ Emergencies: blocked traffic may interfere with the passage of
emergency vehicles traveling to their destinations where they are
urgently needed.

“ Spillover effect from congested main arteries to secondary roads and
side streets as alternative routes are attempted (‘rat running’), which
may affect neighborhood peace & order.

STRIKES:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Strike is a collective, organized, cessation or slowdown of work
by employees, to force acceptance of their demands by _ the employer.
Refusing to discharge the duties, which they are bound to and also
boycotting duties, is called strike. Ex: The teachers boycott their duties, with
a demand, to increase their salaries. Government employees boycott election
duties, for their demand to increase salaries. The coal workers boycott
duties, for their demands. The role of Police during strikes is very limited.
When the strikes are organized peacefully, Police do not have any role. But,
the employees of emergency wings do not have a right to go on strike. This is
explained under The Essential Services Maintenance Act. Ex: Doctors and
nurses of Government hospitals, Officers and staff of Telegraph, Transport
and Police departments. Because, if these employees go on strikes,
Government machinery will come to a standstill and thereby, distorting the

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
and Police departments. Because, if these employees go on strikes,
Government machinery will come to a standstill and thereby, distorting the
normal life of public. So, strike by these people must be prevented at any
cost. Solution for strikes can be achieved by consultations and discussions,
with the Government and employers.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
HUNGER STRIKE:

Hunger strike may be conducted by one person or group of persons.
Hunger strike may be indefinite or fast unto death. If any person goes on
indefinite hunger strike and dies, it may lead to law and order problem. The
followers may resort to violence and eventually the image of the Government
gets tarnished.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
When the hunger strike is indefinite, the District Collector should
review the situation constantly. As per the advice of Medical Officer, if the
condition of person or persons who are continuing hunger strike and their
health deteriorates, necessary steps will be taken by shifting the person/s to
hospital, before the condition of that person goes out of control due to
hunger strike. Generally, leaders will address the gathering at hunger strike
place. They must be prevented from giving inciting and violence encouraging
speeches. Care should be taken and seen, that no such unsocial elements
join hands with them. At the time of shifting the persons to Hospital who are

5 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
on hunger strike to hospital the sympathizers may resort to clash with
Police. The situation has to be handled with care and caution, with tactics.

DHARNAS:

It is a cessation of work by workers in a factory/ industry etc. as a
protest against working conditions or low pay. An aggrieved person or party
sits down and refuses to move from a place believed to be the source of the
grievance, especially the entrance to a building or the door of a residence. It
is a traditional practice of demanding redress for an offense or payment of a
debt by fasting at the doorstep of the offender or debtor. A mode of
compelling payment or compliance, by sitting at the debtor’s door until the
demand is complied with.

GHERAO:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
GHERAO:

Gherao means "encirclement," Gherao is a word originally from
Hindi/Bengali/Marathi. It denotes a tactic used by labor activists and union
leaders in India. Usually, a group of people would surround a politician or a
government building until their demands are met, or answers given. It is
resorted to by people to show that they disagree with something by
standing around a person in authority and not letting them leave until
they agree to do what they want.

FAIRS AND FESTIVALS

The major religious celebrations in India include Id, Christmas, Diwali,
Pushkar Mela, Ganesh Chathurthi, Navaratri, Kumbh Mela, etc. These
festivals celebrated with large gatherings may lead to L & O situation hence
need close supervision and sufficient bandobust to prevent untoward
incidents.

RALLIES:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
RALLIES:

To call to assemble for a common purpose; assemble. The
demonstrators attend the protest rally in order to provide support or to make
a shared effort with their leaders in order to achieve their desired objectives/
demands especially on public roads by cars or motorcycle etc.

PROCESSION:

Procession is a group of people moving in an orderly state. The organizers of
processions have to obtain prior permission, from Sub-Divisional Police
Officer. Before granting the permission, Police must collect advance
information, about the starting place of the procession, route of the
procession, destination of the procession and presence of orchestra along
with the procession etc. As soon as the application is received from the
organizer, the SDPO should conduct enquiry in detail and accord
permission. It has to be taken into consideration, whether there are any

6| Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
mosques, temples on the route, whether any other party is taking out
procession in the same route, if so, its starting place and possibility of clash
of both processions, ending point of the procession.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The SDPO will fix the starting time, route and will issue license, by
imposing conditions, as per Sec.30 of Police Act. If the persons who are
taking out the procession violate the conditions imposed in the license, the
procession can be declared as unlawful and the persons who are
participating in the procession, may be declared as unlawful assembly and
can order for dispersal of the participants of the procession, by an Officer,
not below the rank of Station House Officer and can take action as per law.
Whenever large gathering in the procession is expected or one section of
people are taking out procession, before another one and taking active role,
mufti Constables have to be deployed, to collect information, whether
anybody is planning to cause obstruction to the procession, or preparing for
pelting stones, or planning for attack on each other. Such information is to
be collected and if such information is received, it must be informed to

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
pelting stones, or planning for attack on each other. Such information is to
be collected and if such information is received, it must be informed to
superior Officers, for taking necessary steps, to prevent untoward incidents
in the procession.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In general, when religious processions are taken out, the other religion
people may indulge in pelting stones on the procession, from the mosques or
temples. Advance intelligence has to be collected and sufficient strength of
bandobust must be posted, at the temples and mosques, to avoid untoward
incidents. Sufficient bandobust men and Officers should follow the
procession, from its starting point to end. The duty of bandobust party,
which is following the procession, is to see, whether conditions imposed in
the license are implemented and see that the timings are followed. Further,
the Officers and men at bandobust duty must control them, not to raise
slogans against other community people. The bandobust party must have
lathi section, Tear gas section and Rifle section. In case of emergency, these
parties must be used, for dispersal of unlawful assembly, as per law, by
following the procedure. While following the procession, the bandobust party

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
parties must be used, for dispersal of unlawful assembly, as per law, by
following the procedure. While following the procession, the bandobust party
has to regulate the traffic, till the procession reaches its destination point. If
two sections of the society desire to take out processions on the same day,
the SDPO must permit only one section invariably.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In case of unavoidable circumstances if both the processions are to be
allowed, the starting time and route have to be changed. Under no
circumstances they should clash with each other. As far as possible, the
superior Police Officers and Executive Magistrate have to follow the
processions. If the Executive Magistrate is present, it is easy to take
necessary steps in case of emergency.

7|Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
RASTA AND RAIL ROKOS:

The agitators in the process of their protest may resort to Rasta and
Rail Roko programmes. Their main aim may be to attract the attention of the
Government, by causing obstruction to traffic and disturb the life of public.
During the agitations, preventive steps have to be taken, as per law, to
restore the free flow of traffic. The rowdies and goondas have to be taken into
custody in advance, to meet any untoward incidents. Rasta Roko or Rail
Roko is an offence as per law. Agitators will form into unlawful assembly an
cause the breach of personal freedom to the public. Under these
circumstances, criminal cases can be booked u/s 147,148, 149 IPC and u/s
151 Cr.P.C,, against the agitators. Peaceful conducting of Rasta Roko an
Rail Roko is also an offence. Depending upon the gravity of crime, the senior
Officers have to decide, whether to book cases or not.

VIP VISITS

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
VIP VISITS

During the passage of a V.V.I.P. fleet/ convoy, the barricading shoul
be erected along areas where substantial crowds are expected to gather.
Constables on route lining must face away from the road as the trouble or
threat will come from the buildings and areas along the road. Constables
also must be posted in lanes and roads emerging on to the road, as groups
of people or demonstrators can gather on the road as the fleet approaches.

The Police have to provide required guard and bandobust at the place of
VIP visit, in their jurisdiction. The scale, strength of guard and bandobust
will depend upon the category of the VIP. If the VIP arrives by flight, the
route bandobust arrangements must be made from the airport to the place
of stay and from the guest house to places of meeting. The HCs and PCs
drafted for VVIP bando bust must report well in advance.

B. CIVIL DISTURBANCES
STUDENTS’ UNREST

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
B. CIVIL DISTURBANCES
STUDENTS’ UNREST

Students are a powerful force in the society. Students will support and
go on strike for solving their issues. Students’ agitations will be utilized by
political parties, extremists and terrorists for their selfish ends. Students in
a state of immaturity resort to violence.

Causes for students agitation

Non supply of text books, rise in tuition fees rise in accommodation
charges in hostels, SC, ST and BC scholarships, policies of the Govt.,
shortage of teachers, are the main reasons for students’ agitations. Other
reasons are when the students are suspended, outsiders attack the
students, restricted job opportunities, reservation policy of govt and when
government decisions are not acceptable to them.

8 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Agrarian Agitations:-

In India, about 59 percent of population is depending upon agriculture.
Telangana State is also an agriculture State. The owners of lands are called
‘farmers’ and those who are working on fields are called,’ agriculture labour,
without agriculture labour, there is no cultivation. Agriculture is the main
source of income and it is the back bone of our country. Country cannot
prosper without agriculture. The farmers and agriculture labour will play
major role in society, especially at the time of elections.

Reasons for Agitations

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Reasons for Agitations

Farmers may incur heavy losses, due to the policies of Government. Ex:
Increase in the prices of fertilizers Failure of Government in providing MSP
(Minimum Support Price) to agriculture products, short fall in supply of
electricity, water, seeds and fertilizes. Supply of spurious seeds,
international agreements, incurring loss due to natural calamities, getting
loss, due to fall in prices of agriculture products, disputes between farmers,
about distribution of water from canals, rivers, cheating farmers by the
business community, supplying spurious seeds and pesticides, are the
reasons for agitations by farmers.

Industrial labour agitations (APPM Order No.737):

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Industrial labour agitations (APPM Order No.737):

Peaceful running of industries leads to prosperity of the country. There
should not be strikes, lockouts in the industries. The Industrial Disputes Act
1947 come in to existence to settle the disputes/ issues between
management and labour. The disputes and issues will exist between the
management and labour but not between police and labour.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Caste-Related Violence has occurred and occurs in India in various forms.
According to a report by Human Rights Watch, "Dalits and indigenous
peoples (known as Scheduled Tribes or adivasis) continue to face
discrimination, exclusion, and acts of communal violence. Laws and policies
adopted by the Indian government provide a strong basis for protection.
Millions of people in India continue to be subject to discrimination,
exploitation and violence simply because of their caste. Untouchability
relegates Daliths throughout the country to a lifetime of segregation and
abuse. Caste-based divisions continue to dominate in housing, marriage,

employment and general social interaction-divisions that are reinforced
through economic boycotts and physical violence.

COMMUNAL AGITATORS

ey

“ Communalism has been described as differences on the basis of
religion, race, caste, language, place of birth, residence, etc. (see section
153(a) IPC).

9| Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
“Riots on the basis of communal difference have occurred both before
and after Independence. One of the first recorded incidents of a riot is
that of Ahmadabad in the 18th Century.

“ The resort to communal issue by the political parties has also resulted
in the rebirth of fundamentalism and militancy amongst various
communities.

“Issues which in the past remained local, have now started assuming
national proportions because of the communalization of politics.
(Rameeza Bee case, Ram Janambhoomi - Babri masjid issue)

ELECTIONS

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
ELECTIONS

Even though the system of voting by secret ballot is with the object of
protecting the voter, elections have been marred by violent incidents
affecting both the voters and the candidates. Elections to local bodies or
panchayats are more prone to violence than those to the Assembly or the
Parliament. This is because local issues come into play with greater force.
The Panchayat elections in recent years have left behind a legacy of enmities
which have been cause of many serious breaches of peace. In order to
ensure free and fair balloting, a climate of peace and absence of pressure is
necessary. The police play a major role in ensuring peaceful polling.

Natural calamities

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Natural calamities

Natural Calamities cause massive loss of life and property. Eg-
Droughts, flash floods, cyclones, avalanches, landslides brought on by
torrential rains, and snowstorms etc. A natural disaster might be caused by
earthquakes, flooding, volcanic eruption, landslides, hurricanes etc. To be
classified as a disaster it will have profound environmental effect and/or
human loss and frequently includes financial loss.

Manmade disasters, which result from human negligence, error or
intent, include nuclear warfare, biochemical warfare, explosions, toxic
emissions, global warming, fires, civil unrest and terrorism. Man-made
disasters can be catastrophic, causing extensive damage to the environment
and resulting in human loss and devastation. One of the most devastating
man-made disasters in history is the Bhopal, disaster, a gas leak, which
resulted in nearly 30,000 deaths.

EXTREMISTS :

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
EXTREMISTS :

Extremists are persons who advocate or resort to measures beyond the
norms, especially in politics. An extremist is a person who favors or resorts
to immoderate, uncompromising, or fanatical methods or behavior, in a
political or religious sense, for an ideology that is far outside the (acceptable)
mainstream attitudes of society. The term “extremism” is usually meant to

10 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
express strong disapproval. A person who holds extremists political or
religious views, especially one who advocates illegal, violent or other extreme
action is an extremist.

TERRORISTS

A common definition of terrorism is the systematic use or threatened
use of violence to intimidate a population or government for political,
religious, or ideological goals. Terrorism found in India includes ethno-
nationalist terrorism, religious terrorism and left wing terrorism. The regions
with long term terrorist activities have been Jammu and Kashmir, east-
central and south-central India (Naxalism) and the Seven Sister States.
Terrorist groups in india include Lashkar-e-Taiba, Jaish-e-Mohammed,
Hizbul Muzahiddin, ISIS etc. Acts of terrorism include kidnapping, hijacking,
bombing etc. The assassination of Prime Ministers Indira Gandhi & Rajiv
Gandhi, the Mumbai attack in 2008, the Parliament attack in 2001 by five
armed assailants are some of the major terrorist attacks that happened in
India.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
kek

11 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. ROLE OF POLICE IN PRESERVATION OF PUBLIC PEACE
AND MAINTENANCE OF ORDER

1. COLLECTION OF INTELLIGENCE PSO.NO.734-1.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
One of the most important duties of the police is the collection of
information about every movement, state of feeling, dispute or incident,
which may lead to a breach of peace, and the communication of such
information to the concerned authorities with a view to take action or to use
the information for the purpose of preventing or detecting crime or
preventing breach of peace. All police measures and actions, their efficacy
and use entirely depend on the intelligence gathered. It is absolutely
essential and should be gathered by the SHO, Inspector, SDPO and other
senior officers apart from the specialized units meant for the purpose. It is
pertinent to refer station records to identify the occasions when breach of
peace is likely, its nature, the places and potential troublemakers, visit the
place and assess public feeling and likely trouble. Also to the contact
persons who command respect and confidence of rival groups. It is

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
place and assess public feeling and likely trouble. Also to the contact
persons who command respect and confidence of rival groups. It is
important to gather information from all possible sources and to read and
follow thoroughly the communications and instructions from superior
officers. The SHO should ascertain full details from beat police and other
police staff. All HCs and PCs of the beat area should be instructed to take
help of village officials and keep vigil and report from time to time on the
meetings, both public and secret, handbills distributed and rumors set afloat
by mischievous local elements or outside agents.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. ASSESSING THE SITUATION

After the evaluation and assessment of the problem in all its aspects
with reference particularly to the persons, places and nature of trouble,
police officers shall decide on;

1) Surveillance,
2) Preventive arrests,

3) Promulgation of regulatory or prohibitory orders,

4) Arrangements to be made to contain and control trouble,

5) The specific briefing to all Police Officers to be deployed including
arrangements for their accommodation, food, etc.

It is the prime duty of the police to prevent the commission of
cognizable offences. In such endeavor they should enlist the assistance of
influential persons in communities in mitigating the situation and see that
they exercise their influence in controlling the situations. The help from
Maithri Committees is highly desirable in this regard.

12 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. WATCHING THE DEVELOPMENTS

A striking force of adequate police strength should be mobilized and
kept ready for attending to any sudden outbreak of mob violence. Besides
this all riot control equipment like water cannon, concertina coils, gas guns,
firearms and ammunition, helmets, shields, tear gas shells, shock batons,
baton rounds (rubber/plastic bullet), lathis etc. should be kept ready for
use.

4. PREVENTIVE ACTION

Precautionary measures for preventing or dealing with such disorders
depend upon a number of factors, including;

1 T
2) 7

he cause of friction,
he nature of the dispute,

3) Whether the danger is continuous or associated with a particular
festival or occasion, and;

4) T

Experience has shown that adequate precautions taken in proper time
tend to inspire confidence among several communities and check the
misguided zeal of irresponsible elements. Arrange frequent patrolling if
assessment reveals possible breach of peace.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
he influence of the leaders of the communities.

Such a precaution signals police preparedness and acts as a deterrent
to possible troublemakers and inspires confidence among public. Bestow
utmost attention to fasts, feasts and festivals of different communities
occurring at the same time. Work out mutually acceptable arrangements;
reconcile conflicting approaches or disputes with the help of rival local
leaders. But do not be misled by promises and assurances and make
necessary arrangements as a precaution. Keep the concerned Executive
Magistrate informed in writing to be available. Police Officers should remain

in close touch with the magistrates

luring situations of anticipation and

actual breach of peace. When tensions and feelings run high, a clash in a

large town develops into serious an

and stop clashes in other areas apart
times, disorders get a firm foothold be:

widespread trouble. Precautionary
sumption as such steps will prevent
rom containing spread of trouble. At
fore the necessary arrangements are

made, and forces arrive to control and bring about normalcy. In such an

event ordinarily, the trouble assumes t
tactics in localities.

Where one community is weak and the police are

inadequate to prevent it, widespread looting and arson will often be seen.

The following measures have proved to

be of value in such situations.

Promulgation of orders under Section 144 Cr.P.C/ Section 163 BNSS

13 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
= Curfew, prohibiting the holding of meetings, delivering of speeches,
assembling of persons,

= Carrying of weapons, taking out processions, or other acts from which
a breach of the peace can be apprehended.

= In cases of dispute that occasion the breach of the peace relating to
and or water or to boundaries of land or water action under sections
145 to 148 of Cr.P.C/ Sec 164 to 167 BNSS is appropriate and must
be taken.

= Copies of reports to Magistrates for action u/s 144 or 145 of Cr.P.C/
Sec 163 or 164 BNSS should be sent to the concerned superior
Police Officers.

= Ifthe prospect of averting trouble is not encouraging take the following
steps:

1) Deployment of police force in the vicinity of the scene of trouble. a
Show of strength, is sometimes sufficient to avoid untoward
developments.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
1) Deployment of police force in the vicinity of the scene of trouble. a
Show of strength, is sometimes sufficient to avoid untoward
developments.

2) Route marches by police columns through the affected areas help in
containing and the spread of incidence. Arrangements must be made
or patrols and pickets and for the establishment of communications
between the various police parties and the police headquarters.

ROLE OF POLICE IN CASE OF BREACH OF PEACE & ORDER

1. Use of Force.
2. Investigation of Cases
USE OF FORCE: -

For the dispersal of unlawful assemblies by use of force 3 important
conditions should be fulfilled;

(1) That there is unlawful assembly;
(2) Such an assembly is ordered to disperse; and
(3) In spite of the orders the assembly refused to disperse.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
(1) That there is unlawful assembly;
(2) Such an assembly is ordered to disperse; and
(3) In spite of the orders the assembly refused to disperse.

The force to be used should be the minimum required to disperse the
unlawful assembly. The quantum of the minimum force to be used differs
from situation to situation and depends on the nature of crowd and its
activities etc. A booklet on dealing with communal disturbances has been
circulated by the Government to all District Magistrates, Superintendents of
Police and other officers. The instructions given therein should be followed.
In appropriate cases, those who incite class hatred should be proceeded
against under section 153-A IPC/ Sec 196 BNSS.

14 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Tendency towards disorder should be nipped in the bud and the actual
outbreaks handled effectively with the least possible delay and the minimum
loss or damage to life and property. Senior Police Officer must assess the
situation promptly and leave the scene when they are fully satisfied that the
situation is brought under control. Cases reported during riots should be
promptly investigated and finalized.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
INVESTIGATION OF CASES: Cases must be registered promptly, investigated
impartially under the close supervision of Inspectors and SDPOs. If need be, a
separate investigation team should be formed during the period of riots. Whenever
there is a shortage of manpower, special police officers can be appointed under the
Police Acts. When public property is destroyed in the course of riots or agitations
action can be taken under the Prevention of Damage to Public Property Act 1984.
The Hyderabad City Police Act provides for externment of rowdies from the area.
Precautionary measures should include action u/s 151 Cr.P.C. against goondas,
rowdies, suspects, bad characters and the persons likely to create trouble. Since
the effect of an arrest u/s 151 Cr.P.C. is only for 24 hours. Even if remanded to
judicial custody, a specific offence must be made out in case he is to be kept in
custody for more than 24hours. In respect of breach of peace due to communal,

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
custody for more than 24hours. In respect of breach of peace due to communal,
caste or other problems, preventive detention can be resorted to. In such cases
recourse can be had to Telangana State Prevention of Dangerous Activities of
Communal Offenders Act, 1984, and other preventive detention laws.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
kek

15 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. GENERAL PRINCIPLES OF HANDLING VIOLENT
L & O SITUATIONS

A. SITUATION ASSESSMENT AND MANAGEMENT:
Collection Of Information

One of the most important duties of the police is the collection of
information about every movement, state of feeling, dispute or incident,
which may lead to a breach of peace, and communication of such
information to the concerned authorities with a view to take action or to use
the information for the purpose of preventing or detecting crime or
preventing breach of peace. Station records became handy to identify the
occasions when breach of peace is likely, its nature, the places and potential
troublemakers. (To know about past incidents)

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Visit the place and assess public feeling and likely trouble. Contact
persons who command respect and confidence of rival groups. Gather
information from all possible sources. Read and follow thoroughly the
communications and instructions from superior officers. Confer and
ascertain full details from beat police and other police staff

All HCs and PCs of the beat area should be instructed to take help of
village officials and Keep vigil and report from time to time on the meetings,
both public and secret, handbills distributed and Rumors set afloat by
mischievous local elements or outside agents.

Conduct of Peace Committee/ Mohalla Committee Meetings:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Conduct of Peace Committee/ Mohalla Committee Meetings:

“Policing in a fast changing socio-economic environment is a complex
task. In a democracy it is an accepted view that policing cannot be
accomplished without people’s participation. This is more so in a democracy
such as ours with its pulls and pressures due to various religions, castes
and the sheer numbers involved. Mohalla committees are an integral part of
the community policing initiative.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
As the name suggests, Mohalla committees are formed at the local area
level and consist of a cross-section of society. The local Police station acts as
facilitator for the formation and functioning of the Mohalla committee. The
main task of the Mohalla committee is to assist the Police in maintenance of
law and order and prevention of crime in the area. The Mohalla committee
members act as eyes and ears and pass on relevant information pertaining
to communal, social, law and order related issues to the local beat officer
with whom they are constantly in touch. Besides this, they play an
important role in prevention and management of law and order situations.
The Mohalla committees meet at regular intervals throughout the year. They

16 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
discuss and solve issues relating to local crime and disputes through active
involvement of the beat staff. During major festivals, special meetings are
convened in order to ensure that peace prevails in the area.

Special care is taken to nominate suitable and respectable members of
the community to the Mohalla committee. Measures and decisions taken
during the meetings are disseminated to the community through its
members. Suggestions of the Mohalla committees members are given due
weightage by the police station in identifying and implementing crime
prevention measures.

People’s participation and democratic approach of the Mohalla
Committee movement has been widely appreciated over the years. Mohalla
committees have proved their usefulness time and time again and have now
become an integral part of the policing initiatives.

Counseling & Mediation:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The role of counsellor and mediator has often to be played by the police
officer. In most cases people approach the police in the first instance, even
though the police may have no authority to take action as it may be purely
civil in nature. But as the implications often have a bearing on law and
order; it is in the interest of the police that such cases are amicably sorted
out failing which the parties are quite likely to resort to extra legal methods
which would result in breach of peace. This is where the role of the police
officer as a counselor and mediator comes into effect even though the result
may have no legal validity in the court of law. Often mediation between two
parties which could develop into a law and order situation and breach of law
can lead to a lasting compromise; more importantly as the police are aware
of a developing situation and it is able to take the necessary preventive
measure in time. Press and Radio/ T.V can be effectively used to verify and

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
of a developing situation and it is able to take the necessary preventive
measure in time. Press and Radio/ T.V can be effectively used to verify and
control rumors. This was done during the 1969 Ahmadabad riots and during
the 1965 Indo-Pak war in Delhi.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Often tension builds up over certain incidents such as the discovery of a
dead body. The police must in such cases immediately verify the report and
if untrue take the reputed leaders of the community to the alleged spot and
convince them if true immediate measures to counter the situation should
be taken.

Flag Marches and Show of Force - Flag marches are usually used before
commencement of elections and during the communal or law and order
situations to instill fear in the bad elements and instigators to the incidents.

17 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In flag marches
1. Civil
2. Armed Reserve and

3. Other security forces will participates to show large numbers. The
police shall appeal to the public to help maintain law and order, and
announce any kind of undesirable act, which threatens peace, would
not be tolerated.

Cordon and Search Operations

In law and order situations police should conduct cordon and search of
operations in

1. Houses of the instigators,
2. Rowdies and

3. Other important persons for their preventive arrest and to seize
weapons if any from them while conducting cordon and search
operations procedure should be followed to safe guard the police
personal to avoid untoward incidents. Prepare plan for cordon and
search operations, in advance.

Collect the information about
1. Whether the area is open or closed.

2. What type of buildings, temporary sheds etc

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

Collect the information about
1. Whether the area is open or closed.

2. What type of buildings, temporary sheds etc

3. How many entries and exits to the area. Cover all entries and exists,
take odds to cover from firing. After cordoning the houses and
suspected places announce the (suspects) to come out of the place.

Preventive Actions and Arrests

Section 107 Cr.P.C/ Sec 126 BNSS: security for keeping peace for one year
where a person is likely to commit breach of peace or disturb public
tranquility.

Section 108 Cr.P.C/ Sec 127 BNSS security for good behavior from

Section 124-A IPC/ Sec 152 BNS Publication of seditious matter, which is

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

Section 124-A IPC/ Sec 152 BNS Publication of seditious matter, which is
punishable under this section.

Section 295-A IPC/ Sec 299 BNS maliciously insulting religion or religious
beliefs of any class Action under this is possible only by the order of state.
Executive magistrate empowered to execute the bond. Court of the J.M. Ist
class is empowered to execute the bond.
