page_content:
Section 151 Cr.P.C/ Sec 170 BNSS Arrest to prevent commission of
cognizable offence.

Section 153-A, I.P.C/ Sec 196 BNS

Promoting enmity between classes (groups) on grounds of religion, race,
place of birth, etc. by words spoken or written or visible presentation or by
any act prejudicial to the maintenance of harmony between different groups.

Section 505(I) ( c ) I.P.C/ Sec 353 BNS Statements conductive to public
mischief inciting by rumor or statement made, published or circulated by
one community against another.

Section 505 (2) I.P.C/ Sec 353 BNS

Statements creating or promoting enmity, hatred, or ill-will between classes
in form of reports or statements containing rumor or alarming news.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Statements creating or promoting enmity, hatred, or ill-will between classes
in form of reports or statements containing rumor or alarming news.

Section 144 Cr.P.C/ Sec 163 BNSS empowers a Magistrate to direct any
person to abstain from any act, which is likely to promote disturbance of
public tranquility or a riot or lead to an affray. Violations are punishable
under section 188 I.P.C. which is cognizable. Under this section individuals
can be prohibited from forming an assembly or a procession if it is felt that it
may lead to a disturbance of public tranquility.

Curfew

A regulation or rule requiring certain or all people to leave the streets and

be at home at a prescribed hour.

The time at which such a restriction begins should be signalled
through a bell, announcing the beginning of this restriction. An official
regulation setting restrictions on movement, especially after a specific time a
at night, the time set as a deadline by such a regulation.

Patrolling:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Patrolling:

e The patrolling party in communally sensitive and disturbed areas
must prevent offences and maintain law and order in that area.

e During patrolling, a watch should be kept, over the movements of
suspicious persons, strangers and checking of vehicles to unearth
unlicensed weapons, country made bombs and other deadly weapons.

e In the patrolling area, if there is any information about commission of
an offence, the information has to be passed on to higher Officers and
see that no offence takes place, and to maintain law and order.

e If any incident takes place and information is received for Police
presence, the patrolling party should react and rush to the spot
immediately.

19 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The patrolling party has to keep a watch and conduct surprise checks
on the houses of ex-convicts, anti social elements and if any slightest
suspicion arises, they should be taken into custody and necessary
enquiries must be caused.

The patrolling party must inform the control room about their
locations promptly, to the control room.

Picketing:

Pickets posted at communally sensitive areas, have to keep close
watch over the persons encouraging communal hatred feelings and
also on communal leaders.

They have to create confidence in the minds of the public.

Prior information has to be collected. They have to prevent offences.
Information collected, has to be passed on to superior Officers and to
act as per the instructions of Officers.

One should not leave the picket and proceed on his personal work.
They have to maintain good relations with Mytri members, peace
community members and _ voluntary organizations.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Any incident, even trivial in nature, should not be neglected and
should be solved immediately.

Pickets posted at Temples, Mosques have to take precautions to see,
that the feelings of public are not hurt.

Care should be taken to see, that anti social elements, rowdies,
goondas, communal offenders, do not take shelter in temples and
mosques.

Control Room:

Police Control Rooms function in large towns where two or more Town Police
Stations exist, for a District as a whole, for cities having commissionerate
system and at the police headquarters of the State.

Control rooms are also set up on special occasions, for specific operations
and large-scale bandobusts.

Special units like CID, Intelligence, Traffic Police, TSSP, District Reserve
Police, Special Groups, and Railway Police etc. have their own control rooms.

These constitute an important part of the coordination, control and response
mechanism.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
These constitute an important part of the coordination, control and response
mechanism.

It is a vital instrument for the convenience and benefit of public, a
source of immediate information to the Police and a nerve centre of
varied police operations.

20 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e They are intended to enable public to contact the Police in urgent
cases and obtain assistance immediately.

The functions of the Police Control Room are:

1. To be an information centre to receive calls and rush help or to send
appropriate police assistance;

2. To keep the concerned police officers informed of all-important
incidents /information.

3. To dispatch, L&O Police or Detective teams to deal with crimes like
theft, burglary, murder, dacoity, rioting, arson, accident, kidnapping
or abduction, missing children or persons, terrorist crimes, and new
cases etc.

4. To aid the public during fire accidents, floods and other natural
calamities;

5. To coordinate the operations for maintenance of peace and public
order in times of large-scale riots, strikes, processions and other
situations when the peace of the city or town or of the district or
State is likely to be disturbed;

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
6. Record all incidents, information in a given format and intimate the
concerned officers so as to enable them to take decision;

7. Maintain prescribed records;

8. To alert concerned police stations for interception, seizure or search or
arrest;

9. To provide necessary information to public.

10. Any other function to save life and property or rescue persons in
danger.

11.A Police Control Room should generally be located in a central place in
the town and city and in premises other than a police station.

12. The control room communications on the lines indicated above should
be organized in all district and major cities. Wireless-fitted cars,
vehicles or motorbikes are sanctioned for the Police Control Room, the
number of each being fixed with reference to the needs of each town
or city or district.

13.They should carry first-aid kit so that the Police during traffic
accidents or other cases could render first aid to the injured
persons.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
13.They should carry first-aid kit so that the Police during traffic
accidents or other cases could render first aid to the injured
persons.

14. Effective public address system and searchlight should be installed to
all mobiles, besides siren and lights. Siren should be used only when

21|Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
proceeding to meet an emergency only to have precedence in traffic. If
traffic conditions do not warrant there is no need to use the siren.

15. Striking force contingents of such strength and number as
earmarked for emergency should be available at the Police Control
Room with the required wireless fitted vehicles.

The following records should be maintained in the Police Control Room
by the Sub-Inspector or, in his absence by whoever is in-charge:

a) The Police Control Room General Diary.

b) Incoming Telephone Message Register.

c) Out-going Telephone Message Register.

d) Wireless fitted Cars working Evaluation Register.

e) Data entry, storage, retrieval in the computers provided.

Use of social media to control rumours/ to send factual information to
the public

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e) Data entry, storage, retrieval in the computers provided.

Use of social media to control rumours/ to send factual information to
the public

Social media consisting of WhatsApp, face book, twitter and other blogs are
the popular communication methods to society. Police may use social media
in case of breach of the law order situation to control the rumors by sending
the factual information to public by following steps - like uploading the
videos of the actual incident, suggesting, cautioning, educating & explaining
about the rules / Do’s and Don'ts.

Dos/ Don’ts while handling agitations:

DO’S: Agitation to be handled always in full uniform. Follow the rules and
regulations keep informing the minute to minute situation to the higher
officials. Draw sufficient number of force in advance. Deploy the force in
time. Collect advance information Discuss with leaders of agitators. Ensure
the presence of magistrate.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
DON’TS: Don’t use vile language don’t act discourteously don’t get
intoxicated on duty, don’t make yourself a public nuisance. Don’t get
provoked don’t fall in to argument. Don’t undermine public. Don’t express
personal opinion. Don’t allow to congregate in crowds.

22 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
MODULE - II
HANDLING DAY TO DAY L & O SITUATIONS

1. PSYCHOLOGY AND BEHAVIOR OF CROWDS AND MOBS

1. Common Intention and Joint Liability.

2. Understanding Group Behaviour.

3. Unlawful Assembly.

1. COMMON INTENTION AND JOINT LIABILITY
(a) Common Intention:

To invoke Section 34 IPC/ Section 3(5) BNS it must be established that
the criminal act was done by more than one person in furtherance of
common intention of all. It must, therefore, be proved that:

(i) There was common intention on the part of several persons to commit
a particular crime and

(ii) The crime was actually committed by them in furtherance of that
common intention.

The essence of liability under Section 34 IPC/ Section 3(5) BNS is
simultaneous conscious mind of persons participating in the criminal action
to bring about a particular result. The sharing of common intention gets
satisfied when an overt act is established qua each of the accused.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Common intention implies pre-arranged plan and acting in concert
pursuant to the pre-arranged plan. Common intention is an intention to
commit the crime actually committed and each accused person can be
convicted of that crime, only if he has participated in that common intention.
The classic case on the subject is the judgment of the Privy Council
in Mahbub Shah v. Emperor, AIR 1945 PC 118 wherein it was held as
under:- “...Section 34 lays down a principle of joint liability in the doing of a
criminal act. The section does not say “the common intentions of all” nor
does it say “an intention common to all”. Under the section, the essence of
that liability is to be found in the existence of a common intention animating
the accused leading to the doing of a criminal act in furtherance of such
intention.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
To invoke the aid of Section 34 successfully, It must be shown that the
criminal act complained against was done by one of the accused persons in
the furtherance of the common intention of all; If this is shown, then liability
for the crime may be imposed on any one of the persons In the same manner
as if the act were done by him alone.

23 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Common intention essentially being a state of mind it is very difficult to
procure direct evidence to prove such intention. Therefore, in most cases it
has to be inferred from the act like, the conduct of the accused or other
relevant circumstances of the case. The inference can be gathered from the
manner in which the accused arrived at the scene and mounted the attack,
the determination and concert with which the attack was made, and from
the nature of injury caused by one or some of them. The contributory acts of
the persons who are not responsible for the injury can further be inferred
from the subsequent conduct after the attack. In this regard even an illegal
omission on the part of such accused can indicate the sharing of common
intention. In other words, the totality of circumstances must be taken into
consideration in arriving at the conclusion. Whether the accused had the
common intention to commit an offence of which they could be convicted.

2. UNDERSTANDING GROUP BEHAVIOUR

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. UNDERSTANDING GROUP BEHAVIOUR

A crowd has been described as a "more or less gathering of individuals
in one place in close physical proximity to one another with a tendency to
develop psychological interaction” which the psychology of a crowd differs
from and interacts with that of the individuals within it. Crowd behavior is
heavily influenced by the loss of responsibility of the individual and the
impression of universality of behavior, both of which increase with the size of
the crowd. Crowd psychology is the study of the collective behavior of human
beings when gathered in a mass.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
It is important for a police man to understand a crowd’s psychology
since it enhances his effectiveness in handling crowds of different kinds.
Crowd behavior is different from that of an individual’s behaviour because it
erodes the inhibitions in the individual who forms a part of the crowd. This
is due to the sense of anonymity that develops because the individual’s
identity is submerged in the crowd. Fear of disapproval by the community is
a strong inhibiting factor which governs an individual’s action, refraining
him from committing or indulging in socially unacceptable acts for fear of
being ostracized by the community. He will, however, go along with the
group to commit socially unacceptable acts because in a group his social
inhibitions are loosened. In the cities these restraints are further eroded
because the individual is able to merge himself more completely with a
crowd. The crowd, in its attempt to seek relief from its pent up emotions, will

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
crowd. The crowd, in its attempt to seek relief from its pent up emotions, will
follow any person, or idea, which promises to give relief. In seeking an outlet
for its emotions and excitement it will even attack persons or objects. At this
stage the crowd is not at all susceptible to reason. Moreover, at this stage
the leadership of the mob usually passes into the hands of hooligans or
violent minded person.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content: 24 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Crowds are of two types:-
a) Assembly with common intention to participate in dharnas.
b) Accidental crowd Ex: At the time of fire accidents, road accident.
Crowd will become mob, when it goes out of control, and indulges in
unlawful activities. Generally, the mob will have a leader and there is every

possibility of mob indulging in unlawful activities. Ex: communal/ caste
conflicts, political clashes rioting etc.

3. UNLAWFUL ASSEMBLY:

According to section 141 of the I.P.C/ Section 189 BNS an assembly of
5 or more persons is designated as an “unlawful assembly” if the common
object of the persons composing that assembly is;

First — to overawe by criminal force, or show of criminal force, the central or
any state government or Parliament or the legislature of any state , or any
public servant in the exercise of the lawful power of such servant: or
Second- to resist the execution of any law, or any legal process, or

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Third — to commit any mischief or criminal trespass, or any other offence: or

Fourth- By means of criminal force, or show of criminal force, to any person
to take or obtain possession of any property, or to deprive any person of the
enjoyment of a right way, or the use of water or other incorporeal right of
which he is in possession or enjoyment, or to enforce any right or supposed
right: or

Fifth- by means of criminal force, or show of criminal force, to compel any
person to do what he is not legally bound to do, or to omit to do what he is
legally entitled to do.

Explanation- An assembly which was not unlawful when it assembled may
subsequently become an unlawful assembly.
> The essential ingredients of an unlawful assembly have to be fulfilled:
i. Assembly of five or more persons;
ii. There must be a common objective;

iii. Common objective must be one of the five specifically mentioned in
the section.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
iii. Common objective must be one of the five specifically mentioned in
the section.

Where seven accused were charge- sheeted of whom two were acquitted
by the sessions court and one by the High Court the number became less
than five and the unlawful assembly became extinct.

The offence of rioting as defined under section 146 I.P.C/ Section 191
BNS has the ingredients of an unlawful assembly.

Every unlawful assembly may not turn into a riot, but every riot at its
inception will be an unlawful assembly.

25 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. PLANNING AND HANDLING REGULAR/ NON-VIOLENT
L & O SITUATIONS

Traffic diversions and regulations for VVIP route.
Traffic jams/traffic accidents.

Peaceful rallies and processions.

Village sandy day/ festivals.

Prayers and gatherings.

oooooo

Conventions and conferences.
Q Cinema shootings.
1. TRAFFIC DIVERSIONS AND REGUALTIONS FOR VVIP ROUTE

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The time of departure, arrival route, destination, temples, hotels,
cinema halls and places of frequent visit, by the VIP, should be kept secret.
Whenever suspicious incidents or persons are noticed, assistance of the local
Police must be sought immediately. The protected routes where there is
illumination should be selected. The unexpected or sudden programmes
should be kept secret and it should be informed to only those who are
concerned. The unexpected or sudden programmes should be kept secret
and it should be informed to only those who are concerned. When the places
of visit are not safe from security point of view, the local Police must be
informed, to provide necessary assistance. When the VIP attends special
programmes by road, the traffic should be cleared for VIP convoy, without
any obstruction and bandobust by Police in uniform, should be posted at all
important junctions. The Officers and men, who are drafted for bandobust

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
any obstruction and bandobust by Police in uniform, should be posted at all
important junctions. The Officers and men, who are drafted for bandobust
duty, shall be informed that they have to be in place till the VIP passes
through route. Before the visit and journey of the VIP, roof top bandobust
should be posted, with binoculars, on the top of high rise buildings, water
tanks, heaps and hillocks, to watch the movements of suspects and anti
social elements. Additional link roads for diversion should be available, slow
moving and fast moving traffic should be segregated, cross traffic should not
be allowed; different parking lots to handle regulated dispersal should be
ensured; availability of break down vans for removing vehicles that have
broken down; proper policing with communication and proper selection of
site for congregation should be ensured.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. TRAFFIC JAMS/ TRAFFIC ACCIDENTS:

Handling traffic jams during accidents includes alerting hospitals,
TRAUMA centers, Police Officers and other departments: On receipt of any
information, about an accident, PCR staff must immediately inform the
patrolling vehicles to rush up to the place of incident and regulate the traffic.

26 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Information should be given to the concerned P.S, Government hospitals,
trauma centers and superiors, to be in readiness, for extending co operation
and help. Information should be given to other relevant Officers of revenue
(Tehsildar) Transport and R&B departments also. If the injured persons are
more in number, information must be given to 108 (for ambulance), blood
banks and voluntary organizations.

Receiving and disseminating the information about suspected vehicle,
alerting and diverting the Rakshak vehicle to chase or block the suspect
vehicle:-

If information is received by the staff on duty in P.C.R, that a vehicle is
involved in a crime or road accident and fled away, particulars about that
vehicle, like type of the vehicle, colour, registration number and the direction
in which it sped away etc., must be collected. Information must be passed on
to all Police stations located in the direction. Patrol and Interceptor vehicles
must be alerted, to chase the suspect vehicle.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. PEACEFUL RALLIES ARE:

Q A mass meeting of people making a political protest or showing
support for a cause.

Q A long distance race for motor vehicles over public roads or rough
terrain, typically in several stages.

PROCESSION:

Procession is a line of people who are walking or travelling in the same
direction. Processionis a group of people moving inan orderly state
a regular series sequence or succession of things resembling a procession.
Prior permission, from Sub-Divisional Police Officer is compulsory. Starting
place, route, destination of the procession and existence of places of worship
shall be born in mind while granting permission. Separate route should be
ear marked for each party to avoid confrontation. The SDPO will fix the
starting time, route and will issue license, by imposing conditions, as per
Sec.30 of Police Act. Officer not below the rank of SHO can declare any
procession as unlawful and take action as per law in case of violation of
conditions.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
4. VILLAGE SHANDY DAY/ FESTIVALS:

The volume of moving crowds is much less at fairs and exhibition hence
barriers only at the entrance and exit may be required. It is preferable to
have the fair laid out on rectangular line with static posts at intersections
and mobile patrolling in the lanes. Towers at vantage points should be set up
if the volume of crowd is expected to be large. This will allow for better

27 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
visibility and control by the police. The anticipated volume of crowd will
dictate the extent of arrangements.

5. PRAYERS AND GATHERINGS:

Bandobust must be arranged for prayers and large gathering. Police
must contact the important cadre of religious leaders and ascertain the
expected crowd, well in advance and required bandobust arrangements have
to be made. If the selected place of congregation is insufficient to the
expected quantum of gathering, it should be informed to the organizers
about it and ask them to select another convenient place.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
At the place of congregation, if any situation arises or untoward
incident takes place, it has to be taken into account and ensure that there
are number of ways for safe exit of public from the place. If sufficient
number of exits is not available, arrangements for more exits must be made
by consulting organisers. Strong sufficient barricading must be arranged, to
avoid stampede during the congregation. Police Constables on bandobust
duty should not unnecessarily wield lathis. The public must be convinced
tactfully, to sit within the sectors. At times, unnecessary wielding of lathis,
may lead to stampede.

6. CONVENTIONS AND CONFERENCES:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
6. CONVENTIONS AND CONFERENCES:

Most of the conventions/ conferences are arranged indoor and VIPs may
visit. Police have to contract the organisers for time and place of conventions
and conferences. Ascertain the expected crowd, well in advance. Security
arrangements must be made accordingly. Proper access control, anti
sabotage check, frisking and checking must be taken up. Roof top bundo
busth shall be made if necessary.

7. CINEMA SHOOTINGS:

People are normally crazy about the film stars. They try to come
close/touch the film stars which interrupts their activity. Police shall ensure
no wide publicity is made. Police should make security arrangements at the
location and place of accommodation. Crowd shall be kept at a distance from
the location. Steps should be taken to prevent people attempting to get close
to the film stars. Rope parties are usually effective in controlling unorganized
crowds.

kek

28 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. HANDLING OF NON VIOLENT SITUATIONS LIKELY TO
TURN INTO VIOLENT L & O SITUATIONS

1. REMOVAL OF ENCROACHMENTS

Types of encroachment:-

Encroachment on public land and construction;

Occupying public land by people organized by political parties;
Construction of the structure of worship;

Construction or expansion of shops or shutters;

Occupying footpaths by shopkeepers and petty venders;

Erection of sign boards/advertisement boards by shop keepers.

Action by Police:

The best remedy is to prevent encroachments.

Prompt legal action.

Assessing the number of persons involved and their affiliation.
Preparation of action plan, drawing of force.

Coordination with revenue and other departments.

Presence of executive magistrate.

Pooling up resources, material and man power required.
Pooling up vehicles required for evacuation.

Mobilizing sufficient number of WPCs to deal with women folks.
Deployment of pickup teams separately.

Fire engine, fire extinguishers and blankets.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Mobilizing sufficient number of WPCs to deal with women folks.
Deployment of pickup teams separately.

Fire engine, fire extinguishers and blankets.

Riot control equipment, VHF sets, ropes, mega phones and other
material.

Convincing the encroachers to remove encroachments on their own by
explaining legal consequences.

Ensuring evacuation of encroachers before removal of encroachments.
Minimum use of force.

Posting of pickets following removal of encroachments to prevent re
occupation.

29 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. BANDH CALLS BY POLITICAL PARTIES:

Abstaining from duties and obstructing those going to attend is called

Bandh. Bandh is an act or agitation, to show their resentment, against the
government or against other political parties. Apart from abstaining from
their duties, they also insist or force the business establishments and other
offices to close down which is an offence, Sometimes, small incidents turn
into major violent incidents and cause danger to the lives and property of
people.

Police should ---

Collect advance information about political parties and understand the
issues,

About strength of parties, founders, trouble making leaders and the
likely incidents.

Careful decision when to interfere

Deployment of adequate number of force at strategic points
Protection to soft targets like transportation, govt. offices etc.,
Intensified patrolling by supervisory officers.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Firm action in case of violence by invoking substantive sections.
Preventive arrest of leaders when violence is apprehended.

Preventive arrest of goondas rowdies and trouble mongers.

3. AGITATIONS BY STUDENT UNIONS

Students are powerful force in the society.

They go on strike for solving their issues.

They are exploited by political parties and others for selfish ends .
In a state of immaturity they resort to violence.

They do not have criminal back ground in general.

Sometimes they are also used by extremists and terrorists to carry out
their agenda.

Police duties during students’ unrest/ campus violence (PSO.743)

The nature and its previous history has to be known.

Political party workers may encourage students to indulge in violence
and they will instigate police to resort to lathi charge. At times, it may
lead to police firing also. Police must have patience, to the maximum
extent possible and identify the trouble mongers.

30 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Students, who are trying to indulge in violent activities, must be
identified and separated from the crowd.

If senior police officers talk to the student leaders in advance, violence
can be prevented.

The issue is between the students and the college management or the
government. So, police must be liberal to some extent.

If there is information about the likely hood of serious agitation,
preventive arrests have to be made.

If anybody is behaving like a rowdy, by taking support of political
parties, the activities of such student, have to be recorded.

Counseling of parents of the students has to be conducted. Take
assistance of student leaders or neutral teachers to persuade against
violence.

Repercussions and bad effects of involving in cases, has to be
highlighted.

Make prompt arrest if a serious incident like grievous hurt or homicide
has taken place.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Make prompt arrest if a serious incident like grievous hurt or homicide
has taken place.

While proceeding to deal with an outbreak of violence, strong police
orce must be taken. All policemen should be doubly briefed on
restraint and conduct. Use of force should be avoided as far as
possible, unless there is no other way to stop further __ violence.

While making arrests avoid doing so in the full view of the students.
Unless it is in the course of dealing with an unlawful assembly.

Watch should be kept at hostels.

See that the concerned principal takes action against errant students.
Keep special watch on extremists influence.

Ensure that only genuine and legitimate inmates stay in hostels.
Visit the campus in normal times.

Initiate action u/s 107 Cr.P.C/ 126 BNSS, 2023 in cases of group
clashes.

Unlawful activities of agitators must be photographed.

Original issue will be diverted and agitation will be focused against the
government.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Unlawful activities of agitators must be photographed.

Original issue will be diverted and agitation will be focused against the
government.

Do not express personal opinions, but only act according to law.

Students are not enemies of police. But, Police should take firm action
and avert violent incidents.

31 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf

page_content:
AGITATIONS BY EMPLOYEES UNIONS AND TRADE UNIONS

Forms of agitations-

1.

Police Action (refer PSO no. 737)

Police should not take sides of either the management or labour. Police
have to act impartially. They must understand the issue.

[The SHO should assess the situation correctly and make necessary

properly.

During agitations, the Constables should keep a watch on the

[ake action against persons in possession of deadly weapons.

[he persons indulging in violence to be identified and removed.

Ensure presence of a Executive Magistrate.

Security to houses of management personnel prone for attacks.
Protection to loyal workers and officers.

Covering agitation with photography and videography .

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Covering agitation with photography and videography .

Police to exhibit patience if violence prevails.

Take steps to prevent the agitators targeting/ approaching
sophisticated machinery and chemical substances.

Seek cooperation and assistance of security persons.
Maintain good relations with labour and their leaders.

Collect statistics of labour, their leaders, their unions and their

Take cooperation of labour leaders and responsible workers.

page_content:
Deal with destruction of public property /physical harm/violent
demonstrations in accordance with law with speed and expedition. Create
confidence in labour unions and ensure conducting elections to labour
unions in a fair manner.

4. PICKETINGS/ GHERAOS/ DEMONSTRATIONS/ RASTHA ROKOS, CHALO
ASSEMBLY/ STRIKES

Picketings: are organized at the gates of the institutions by its employees or
public in general. Picket should not be allowed near to the gates and should
be some distance away.

e Sec 163 BNSS 2023/ 144 Cr.P.C prohibitory orders may be
promulgated to keep pickets away.

e The area of possible confrontation should be kept clear of persons and
vehicles.

e Place cordons to create sterile area, to reduce chances of conflict.

e Police shall negotiate with the agitators to maintain order.
Gheraos:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e Place cordons to create sterile area, to reduce chances of conflict.

e Police shall negotiate with the agitators to maintain order.
Gheraos:

It denotes a tactic used by labour activists and union leaders in India.
Usually, a group of people would surround a politician or a government
building until their demands are met, or answers given.

e Police shall exhibit patience

e They should withhold action as long as possible to facilitate & to clear
people gathered through talks.

e Police should be ready to intervene as situation demands.

e Use of force is only when necessary.

e Eviction shall be done firmly when people try to barge in to.
Demonstrations:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e Use of force is only when necessary.

e Eviction shall be done firmly when people try to barge in to.
Demonstrations:

Political parties often express their disapproval of certain govt. measures by
demonstrations. Demonstrations are well advertised to get public support
and increase the gathering. Demonstrations are also organized by traders
and other associations to highlight their problems. Peaceful demonstrations
are allowed under article 19(1) (b) of Constitution of India. Improper escort to
some demonstrations may lead to violence. Proper bundo bust under
responsible officer may be provided to all demonstrations.

Rastha rokos: is a form of protest by causing obstruction to traffic and
disturb the public life.

e The main aim may be to get the attention of the government.

33 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Preventive steps shall be taken as per law to restore free flow of traffic.

Preventive arrests of rowdies and goondas shall be done in advance to
meet any untoward incidents.

Criminal cases can be booked under 147, 341, 149 IPC/ 191, 190,
BNSS depending on gravity.

Agitators can be taken into custody under section 151 Cr.P.C./ 170
BNSS.

Chalo Assembly: Is a form of protest popularized in the recent past.

Political parties give a call for chalo assembly while the assembly
session is on.

Elaborate bundo busth arrangements are necessarily made to avert
any attempt to disrupt the proceedings of assembly.

Check posts shall be erected in all possible routes to prevent the
agitators to reach assembly.

Preventive arrests of probable leaders and agitators shall be made well
in advance.

Collection of information about the movements and changing tactics of
the political party leaders.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Collection of information about the movements and changing tactics of
the political party leaders.

Strikes: refusing to discharge duties which they are bound to do, and also
boycotting duties is called strike.

Qa
Qa
Qa
Qa

Qa

Strikes are generally organized peacefully.
The role of police during strikes is very limited.
Emergency wings do not have a right to go on strike.

Strike by emergency wings must be prevented as explained under
Essential services maintenance Act.

Solution for strikes can be achieved by consultations and discussions.

5. AGITATION BY UNEMPLOYED YOUTH/ WOMEN

Both women and unemployed youth also express their protest in the form of
rallies, demonstrations, Rastarokos, chalo assembly and strikes to solve
their problems.

Bandobust at public meetings at large gathering (APPM Order No.735)
procession (APPM Order NO.736-1) Dharnas ,Bandhs,Picketing and
strikes:

1.

Bandobast must be arranged.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. Contact important person or leader of group to ascertain all the details
required for bandobusts arrangements.
3. Ensure selection of suitable venue for expected crowd.
4. Ensure multiple exit points.
5. Ensure proper barricading to avoid stampede.
6. Police constables shall not unnecessarily wield lathis.
Processions:

Prior permission must be obtained from the SDPO.
Specify starting time, route and ending time by imposing conditions as
per sec-30 Police Act.

Ensure declaring unlawful assembly in case of violation of conditions
and dispersal of participants.

Deploy mufti constables to collect information about causing
obstruction / preparing for pelting stones/attack by opponents.

Ensure posting of sufficient bandobust at vulnerable places.

Sufficient strength of men and officers should follow the procession
having lathi section, tear gas section and rifle section, if needed

Ensure regulation of traffic.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Ensure regulation of traffic.

No permission shall be granted for two processions at one point of time
and route.

Presence of superior officers and executive magistrate, is necessary.

The Role of Police in Controlling Mobs:

Collection of advance information and preparing plan of action.

Intelligence branch to collect information about organizers and their
activities.

Keeping watch on the organizers, method of assembly and persons
assisting them.

Ensuring sufficient police force to face any sort of situation.

Effective communication arrangements between officers and men and
with head quarters.

Arrangements for photography, video grapahy and public address
system.

Identifying leaders and important persons among the mob.

6. ROLE OF WOMEN POLICE IN HANDLING OF L&O SITUATION

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Woman is considered to be symbol of dignity in India. Any kind of disrespect
to women will lead to lot of criticism from all sections of society. Keeping this
in mind, agitators normally keep their women cadres in front line to
embarrass police forces. Handling of women gatherings by male police
personnel led to serious remarks by the courts and commissions on many
occasions in the past. Women gatherings shall be invariably handled by
women police without any deviation. Proper strength of women force shall be
drawn to deal with women agitators by collecting advance information and
assessing the situation. Avoid rough handling and dragging of woman
agitators. Care must be taken about dislocation of clothes of women
agitators while handling them. Make arrangements for video graphics and
photography. The police should exhibit restraint and patience to maximum
extent. Minimum force should be used. Ropes should be used to contain

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
photography. The police should exhibit restraint and patience to maximum
extent. Minimum force should be used. Ropes should be used to contain
women agitators. Care should be taken to avoid photography and
videography by media.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf

page_content:
4. COORDINATION WITH VARIOUS DEPARTMENTS

= Revenue.

" Health.

* Municipal.
= R&B.

= 1&PR.

« Fire.

" Railway.
= Electricity.

Revenue: Revenue Magistrates: In every Mandal, Tahsildar is the Executive
Magistrate. The maintenance of law and order in their jurisdiction is the
primary duty. Police will co-operate for implementation of Govt. welfare
schemes and maintenance of law and order. The Executive Magistrates will
exercise their powers as Magistrates, in cases of maintaining public peace
and tranquility. They will conduct enquiries and inquests in custodial deaths
(lockup deaths). They will conduct exhumation of dead bodies, if they are
buried, without proper investigation, in case of unnatural deaths. The
judiciary is the competent authority, to punish the accused. When the
offenders are punished by the Courts, then only it is possible for Police to
maintain law and order. Thus, the judiciary has to play a major role, in
maintaining law and order.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Police should take necessary steps, to assist judiciary, in its function,
from registration of F.I.R to filing of charge sheet in Courts. Police must work
hard. Mere filing of charge sheet in the Court will not end the duties of
Police. Police have to produce witnesses before Courts and see that the
witness depose their evidence correctly and thereby the accused is convicted
by Courts, for the offences, committed by them. Police should follow the
orders of Courts, in service of summons and execution of warrants.

Health- Medical Department: The designated Medical Officer is an ,expert*
as per law. The evidence of Medical Officer is very important in the Courts.
Medical Officer will examine and treat the injured persons, sent by Police, in
medico legal cases. He will conduct autopsy over the dead bodies, sent by
Police and issue wound certificates and post mortem reports. The evidence of
doctor is very essential for all bodily offences.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Forest Department: The rainfall of the country depends upon the area of
forest. The protection of forests is very important. Protection of wild life in

37 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
the forest, along with other species is an important issue for the
maintenance of ecological balance of the environment. The protection of
forest is the duty of forest department. Police will assist the forest
department, in checking unauthorized persons, prevention of unauthorized
cutting of trees and hunting of wild animals. Police will also assist the forest
department in the service of summons and execution of warrants, as and
when required, by the forest department.

The State Excise Department: The State excise department is one of the
major income departments. It is earning large portion of the income, to the
State Government. The collection of taxes, through excise and prevention of
illicit liquor and related offences, are the duties of excise department. As the
civil Police are also given powers to prevent the offences under the excise
Act, it will extend co-operation to the excise department in its functioning.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Transport Department: The transport department is also an important
source of earning for the State Govt. Registration of all the public vehicles
plying in the State and collection of taxes, are the important duties of this
department. Persons, who drive vehicles, must possess a driving license and
it should be issued by the transport department, after conducting prescribed
tests to the applicant. The transport department should ensure that all the
owners of vehicles possess their valid documents. As Police department is
also vested with powers under the Motor Vehicle Act, curbing of illegal
practices, while using the vehicles, is also the responsibility of Police. Police
must extend co-operation to the transport department, whenever it seeks
assistance. Proper security must be provided to the transport department, if
the illicit vehicles are handed over for safe custody.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Municipal: The Municipal department is also an important department. The
epartment maintains sanitation and collects the waste material. It provides
rinking water to the towns. It regulates the new constructions of buildings
and roads. During the regulation sometimes L&O problem may occurs Police
will assist the Municipal authorities when ever needed. Municipal authorities
will provide the basic infrastructure like traffic signals, road dividers,
painting the roads etc., for better traffic management.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Roads and Buildings Department: The R & B department is normally
responsible for preparation of Helipad, barricading and stage arrangements
in the event of visit of VIPs, official meetings and functions. The scenes of
collapse of stages/barricading are seen many times which are considered to
be severe security lapse on part of police. Laying of roads during VIP visit is
sometimes taken up in the last hour ignoring the quality norms. It is also
responsible for the arrangements for the accommodation of VIPs. Police shall
have coordination with R & B officials in above issues according to the
security norms.

I & PR: This department is responsible for passing of information to press
and media and circulation of official press notes. The department maintains
co-ordination with press and media It also provides transportation and other

38 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
amenities to enable press and media to cover VIP tours and functions. Police
require co-ordination with this department for better media management.

Fire services: Co-ordination with fire service is required in:
= Fire accidents
= VIP visits and tours
= Helipad
= Fairs and Festivals
= Rescue operations during natural calamities.

Railway: Co-ordination with railways is required during the mass agitations
like rail roko, VIP visits and tours, train accidents, thefts and burglaries,
surveillance, anti sabotage measures, mischief and crime, illegal
transportation and smuggling, human trafficking.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Electricity Department: Electricity Department plays a key role in the
society. This department is catering to the needs of public. Sufficient power
is required to be supplied to the Industries, agriculture and for domestic
needs, basing on demand. The prosperity of the State is depending upon the
supply of power. Police department must give proper and required assistance
to the electricity department, while checking power thefts and misuse of
electricity, by the people who are not covered under the scheme of free power
supply.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Education Department: The education department is also an important
wing of the State. The increase in the literacy will improve the standards of
public. Higher education, vocational courses, will improve the standard of
living, among the public. Poverty and unemployment are the main reasons
for the law and order problems. Police must properly assist the education
department, in conducting examinations. Police must maintain rapport with
education department, to prevent the offences relating to examinations,
ragging and eve-teasing etc.

Endowments Department:

= This department is established to safe guard the temples, ancient
culture, monuments and traditions of the society.

= During important festivals, processions and Annual Rathothsavams,
Police must assist this department, for the smooth and incident free
completion of the events.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
= It is the duty of Police, to provide proper security to the valuable
ornaments of temples. It is also the duty of Police, to give protection to
the huge public, who visit temples, like Yadadri, Bhadrachalam,
Vemulawada, Basara, Kaleswaram etc. Police should also guide the
pilgrims, when they are in need.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
MODULE - Ill
HANDLING OF SPECIFIC L & O SITUATIONS/ CASE
STUDIES

1. LARGE SCALE RELIGIOUS GATHERINGS

A. PUSHKARAMS

Pushkaram is an Indian festival dedicated to worshiping of rivers. It is also
known as Pushkaralu (in Telugu), Pushkara or Pushkar. It is celebrated at
shrines along the banks of 12 major sacred rivers in India, in the form
of ancestor worship, spiritual discourses, devotional music and cultural
programmes. The celebration happens once in 12 years along each river.

Preventing stampede at Pushkarams

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Preventing stampede at Pushkarams

Wide publicity about pushkar Ghats and regarding their capacity,
arrangements of queue lines and barricades, barriers for controlling surge of
crowd in queue lines shall be made Inflow and outflow of crowd at regular
intervals shall be monitored and pilgrims shall be diverted to other Ghats.
Stagnation of pilgrims shall be cleared. Separate entry and exit for children,
women and the physically challenged pilgrims shall be arranged. Sufficient
number of entries and exits shall be ensured. Instructions shall be passed to
the pilgrims to regulate crowds through public address system.CC TV
cameras and integrated control room with all government departments shall
be arranged. Presence of senior officer in control room to assess situation
from time to time and pass instructions shall be ensured .Effective
communication plan must be drawn and implemented. Proper briefing to
force and coordination among the force shall be ensured.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
There shall be proper coordination with other departments. There
should be arrangements for separate timings for VIPs. Emergency exits and
rope parties shall be effectively used for crowd management. Contingency
plan for emergency and evacuation shall be prepared. Medical service,
rescue teams, Ambulance and fire services shall be kept ready.

Preventing Drowning - There shall be red flags and danger lights to show
danger zones. Cautions and warnings through PA system shall be passed
from time to time. Chain link fencing and barricading shall be arranged to
prevent people from entering deep water. Riverine police with boats and
proper equipment shall be deployed. Deployment of expert swimmers should
be ensured. Fire and rescue teams shall be in place.

40 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
B. STAMPEDE DURING GODAVARI PUSHKARAMS IN RAJAMUNDRY

Reasons for stampede:

Kotagummam and kotilingala Ghats were considered as sacred.
These Ghats were over crowded when other Ghats were empty.
Wide publicity was given that the starting time was a sacred muhurat.

People gathered huge number and eager to take holy dip at muhurat
time.

People were stopped to facilitate the CM and other VIPs to take holy
dip.

People were kept waiting for a long period and crowd went on
increasing.

When gates opened they were eager to rush in which led to stampede.
Entire focus was on VIP security ignoring crowd management

Poor assessment about gathering of crowd.

No proper mechanism to divert the crowd to other Ghats.

No proper briefing of duties to be performed and lack of preparedness.
Poor coordination between the ranks.

Nobody knows who opened the gates.

Insufficient number of entry and exits.

No proper maintenance of single line queue system.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Nobody knows who opened the gates.

Insufficient number of entry and exits.

No proper maintenance of single line queue system.

No senior officer was in charge for crowd management.

Lack of contingency plan.

41 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. GANESH IMMERSION PROCESSION & VIDEO FILM ON
GANESH PROCESSION IN HYDERABAD

In each Police station, a list of places of Ganesh idol installations
alongwith the names of organizers date and place of immersion has to be
prepared well in advance and accordingly the required bandobust
scheme/arrangement has to be ensured, in connection with “Ganesh
Festival” within the jurisdiction. In case, any additional civil force and AR
force is required, it must be drawn by making a requisition to S.P. of the
District and Commissioner in Commissionerates. The entire force drawn for
bandobust must be briefed about their duties. The men drafted for
bandobust duties must be issued a duty ticket with clear instructions of the
duty to be performed. Police has to liaise with other stake holders - R & B
Department, Health Department, Sanitary Department, Water Works
Department, Fire Services and Peace Committee/ Mythri Committee etc.

42 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. CASTE/ COMMUNAL VIOLENCE - ARSONS

Communalism has been described as differences on the basis of
religion, race, caste, language, place of birth, residence, etc. (see section
153(a)IPC)196 BNSS. Riots on the basis of communal difference have
occurred both before and after independence. One of the first recorded
incidents of a riot is that of Ahmadabad in the 18th century which resulted
in the birth of fundamentalism and militancy amongst various communities.
Issues which in the past were local, have now started assuming national
proportions because of the communalization of politics. (Rameeza Bee case,
Ram Janma bhoomi Babri Masid issue).

Causes of Communal Violence

1. Personal disputes between individuals or parties which may be of a
civil or criminal nature.

Encroachment on places of worship or on land adjoining such places.

Playing devotional music in front of mosques.

Aa

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Encroachment on places of worship or on land adjoining such places.

Playing devotional music in front of mosques.

Aa

Processions by one community passing through the majority area of
other community or by place of worship.

Conversions.
Teasing of girls.
Inter community marriages.

Cow slaughter.

pop aonran

Throwing of color during Holi.

10. Extraneous factors such as issues pertaining to other districts, states
or even countries.

Nature of disturbances

Communal disturbances may take the following shape;
Assaults and murders.

Arsons.

Looting.

Rw Nb

Defilement of places of worship.
5. Rape and abduction of women.
Causes of caste violence
1. Social/financial inequalities.

2. Land disputes.

43 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. Inter caste marriages.
4. Eve teasing.
Handling of communal trouble

Prevention is always better than cure. Here preventive measures would
entail all those steps which the district administration should adopt to
maintain a healthy communal atmosphere.

1. The police must adopt a secular and unbiased approach and a will for
effective action.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
1. The police must adopt a secular and unbiased approach and a will for
effective action.

2. It should respond immediately to all situations which have a communal
implication, in criminal cases proper legal action such as the prompt
arrest of the accused and investigation should take place. In civil
disputes, though the police cannot legally settle the disputes they must
counsel both the parties to refrain from taking the law into their own
hands and seek adjudication from the proper court of law .The police has
to enforce status quo and if necessary action under sections
107/116/151 Cr.P.C./ sec. 126, 135, 170 of BNSS, 2023 can be taken.
Similarly ,in any dispute regarding possession of immoveable property
,action should be taken u/s 151 Cr.P.C./ 170 BNSS in cases where the

persons have been forcibly dispossessed of their property, action is

possible u/s 145(iv)Cr.P.C./ 164 BNSS, 2023 for restoration of status
quo.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
persons have been forcibly dispossessed of their property, action is

possible u/s 145(iv)Cr.P.C./ 164 BNSS, 2023 for restoration of status
quo.

3. Healthy interaction between all the communities should be maintained
by frequent peace committee meetings and functions on various
occasions.

4. Rumors, like incidents, have to be immediately attended to and nipped in
the bud.

5. The Intelligence Wing has to maintain a continuous watch to assess and
analyze the incidents and their reactions so that the police is in a
position to respond immediately and effectively. This pertains to
happenings not only within the district but also outside. A record of all
those persons who indulge in communal utterances and publications
should be maintained for action under available laws.

6. Frequent press briefings by the district administration should be
organised to prevent publication of distorted news.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
6. Frequent press briefings by the district administration should be
organised to prevent publication of distorted news.

7. Routes of processions should be surveyed well in advance and necessary
steps take to ensure smooth passage. All differences between the
communities should be smoothed out before any major festival. Licenses
under section 30 Police Act should explicitly spell out the routes, timings
and other aspects.

e Administrative arrangements;
e Welfare and catering;

e Strategy such as action against criminal, anti social and communal
elements, protective arrangements for sensitive and vulnerable areas
and points, should be part of the contingency plan.

Case study: Bhainsa communal riots - 2008, Shamshamshabad/
Rajendranagar P.S. communal riots -2014.

Communal violence is a form of violence that is perpetrated across
ethnic or communal lines, the violent parties feel solidarity for their
respective groups, and victims are chosen based upon group membership

BHAINSA COMMUNAL RIOTS - 2008

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
BHAINSA COMMUNAL RIOTS - 2008

On October 10, 2008, three people were killed and fifteen others were
injured in communal riots and subsequent police firing in the communally
sensitive Bhainsa town of northern Telangana’s Nirmal district. The violence
erupted during the immersion procession of Durga idols, which coincided
with the time when the Muslims in the area gathered to offer Friday Namaz.
As per reports, the Hindu procession was passing through a road next to the
Panjeshah mosque in the town and the people inside the shrine objected to
music being played as their prayers were on. That led to a heated argument
between the members of the two communities which led to stone pelting and
the situation went out of control. During the clashes, miscreants plundered
and set fire to 150 shops and 20 vehicles. To control the situation, the police
used brute force but when that didn’t help the authorities opened fire in the

45 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
air. The situation was brought under control after 35 bullets were fired into
the air. A week-long curfew was also imposed to restore normalcy in the
area.

The police had filed the case under Sections 147 (rioting), 148 (violence
using weapons) and 149 (unlawful assembly to commit an offence), besides
302 (murder) of the Indian Penal Code against the accused and all the
accused were granted bail merely 3-months after the incident. Subsequently
charge sheet was filed in the year 2012, four years after the incident
occurred.

The local court in Adilabad district dismissed the case stating that the
prosecution had failed to provide adequate evidence to substantiate the
accusations and acquitted all the accused involved in the violence that broke

out between Hindus and Muslims in Bhainsa town in Nirmal district,
Telangana, in the year 2008.

SHAMSHABAD/ RAJENDRA NAGAR P.S. COMMUNAL RIOTS -2014

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
SHAMSHABAD/ RAJENDRA NAGAR P.S. COMMUNAL RIOTS -2014

On the I/N of 13/14.5.2014 due to the burning of “Nishan Sahen” (Religious
flag) of Sikh community by some unknown miscreants, communal violence
erupted between Sikh and Muslim communities at Arsh Mahal, Attapur,
Rajendranagar P.S. limits, erstwhile Shamshabad Zone. The communal
clashes resulted in vandalism and arson, wherein intensive damage was
caused to the properties of both the communities by the warring groups.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
On receipt of information, the local police rushed to the scene of crime
and tried to bring the situation under control. Additional forces were
deployed and all possible efforts were made to bring the tense situation
under control, but the angry mob attempted to snatch weapons and
attacked the police personnel. As such, in the larger public interest to
protect the lives and properties of the general public, the police resorted to
fire in self-defense. Due to the police firing, several persons among the
rioting mob sustained injuries and three persons succumbed to death. A
week-long curfew was also imposed to restore normalcy in the area.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In connection with the above incident, three cases were registered; 1)
FIR No. 386/2014 U/s 147, 148, 332, 307, 153, 153(A) R/w 149 IPC &
Sec.25 of I.A. Act, 1959, 2) FIR No.387/2014 U/s 176 of Cr.P.C. and 3) FIR
No. 388/2014 U/s 176 of Cr.P.C. at Rajendranagar PS. erstwhile
Shamshabad Zone. After due investigation and Magisterial enquiry, the
above three cases were referred as “Action Abated”.

Case Study of Massacre of Daliths In Tsunduru in -1991

Chundur Massacre/ Tsundur Massacre is an event in the history
of Andhra Pradesh, India. It happened in 1991, in Chundur village
in Tenali mandal of Guntur district. In Chunduru, a village a few kilometers

46 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
from Tenali in Andhra Pradesh, 08 dalits was massacred by the upper-caste
Reddy men in the fields in 1991. Conflicting atmosphere prevailed among
dalits and Reddy communities on some occasions prior to this massacre.
Inauguration of statue of Ambedkar in centre of village along with Subhash
Chandra Bose and Potti Sreeramulu questioned the traditional hegemony of
upper caste Reddy community in symbolic way.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In another occasion, Dalit youth made an attempt to watch a play
Chintamani on the occasion of Dussera festival along with the upper caste.
This claim irritated the supremacy of the Reddy and Telaga community and
become intolerant towards Dalit claim of equality in public place. The
incident of touching the leg of Dalit youth to the upper caste reddy fuelled
the situation. This incident followed the fight between two communities in
the cinema hall itself. Later, Reddy community people beat the Dalit youth
involved in the cinema hall episode and beat up his father, a school teacher.
The teacher decided to leave the village for this insult. Dalits of the Tsundur
united together and consoled the teacher not to leave the village and even
fined Rs.25 for not complaining against Reddy community to the police.
Later on for complaining to the police against Reddy community on this
incident, the upper caste under the leadership of Reddy village surpanch

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Later on for complaining to the police against Reddy community on this
incident, the upper caste under the leadership of Reddy village surpanch
took a decision to socially boycott the dalits on July 9, 1991.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Many Dalits lost their livelihood as they depended on the daily wages by
working in the paddy fields of the Reddys. In all these incidents the
assertion of Dalits was felt by the upper caste as threatening the dominant
caste hegemony The conflict between the Dalits and Reddys reached to the
end point by August 6,1991 known as Tsundur massacre, in which eight
Dalits were killed and many wounded in the well organized caste raid of
Reddys of Tsundur and nearby villages, with support of Telaga
community. The police forced dalits to run away from Dalitwada, in an
understanding with Reddys. Around 400 upper caste people ganged together
and divided into two groups, the killed dalits with traditional arms whomever
they met on their way. The bodies were packed in a gunny bags and thrown
into the nearby Tungabhadra drain. The bodies were recovered after three
days from the incident in a swollen condition.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The media reported these incidents are due to the earlier conflicts
between two communities, rather than one of the ongoing incidents of caste
violence against dalits. One more youth, Anil Kumar, was killed in police
firing in the village in the following incident. The significance of this atrocity
was Dalits collectively fought to gain legal justice by invoking SC/ST
Prevention of Atrocities Act 1989.

There has been an active Dalit movement in the following years of the
atrocity. Police filed charge-sheets against 219 people belonging to Tsunduru

47 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
and neighboring villages. Under pressure from Dalit associations, the State
government established a special court under the provisions of SC & ST
(Prevention of Atrocities) Act in Tsunduru to try the accused. On a tense day
on August 1, 2007, special court judge Anis convicted 56 accused, out of
which 21 were awarded life imprisonment and 35 simple imprisonments. It
let off 115 others citing lack of evidence. But later the High Court took off
the charges citing lack of evidence.

Failures on Part of Police

= There was a criticism that police had been partial in dealing the
incidents prior to massacre.

= They were supporting upper caste community.

= No senior officer handled the cases.

= Poor anticipation of incidents leading to massacre.
= No attempt of counselling of both communities.

= No preventive action to reduce the tensions.

= No deployment of force/picket/patrol after occurrence of stray
incidents prior to the mass killing.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
= No deployment of force/picket/patrol after occurrence of stray
incidents prior to the mass killing.

= Failure in collection of information about the preparations of people of
upper caste intending to attack dalits.

= There was a criticism that police had suggested dalits to flee away from
the village to save their lives as upper caste people had a plan to
attack them.

= No action even after receipt of information about preparations to
attack.

= Delay in initiating action even after the incident, finally led to
acquittal.

Case Study of Communal Violence of Dec-1990

Hyderabad has a history of Hindu-Muslim violence, and last such
violence had taken place in 1984.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Case Study of Communal Violence of Dec-1990

Hyderabad has a history of Hindu-Muslim violence, and last such
violence had taken place in 1984.

The roots of the 1990 riots lay in the Ayodhya dispute, which is
centered on Uttar Pradesh, a different state. Hindu activists have long
alleged that the Babri mosque in Ayodhya was constructed after Muslims
demolished a temple at the site. In November 1990, when a crowd of Hindu
activists tried to demolish the mosque, the police fired on them resulting in
several deaths. Since then, tension between Hyderabadi Hindus and
Muslims had been rising. The riots began with the killing of Sardar, a
Muslim auto-rickshaw driver, by two Hindus. The death was actually the

48 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
result of a land dispute unrelated to religious matters. But in light of the
Babri mosque controversy, people saw it as a Hindu-Muslim conflict. In
response to Sardar's killing, Muslims killed four Hindus in different parts of
the walled city. Bharatiya Janata Party workers then attacked Majid Khan,
an influential Muslim leader in the Sabzi Mandi area. As false rumors about
his death spread, Muslim mobs came out in open, followed by the Hindu
mobs, and the violence spread. The violence lasted for ten weeks, and
resulted in 200 to 300 deaths; thousands were injured. Not a single rioter
was arrested.

Chief Minister Marri Chenna Reddy, a member of the Indian National
Congress, claimed that the riot had been engineered by his rivals in his own
party, who wanted to oust him.

Failures on Part of Police
= Complete failure of intelligence.

= No anticipation of non communal incident turning into communal
violence.

= No preventive actions.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
= No anticipation of non communal incident turning into communal
violence.

= No preventive actions.

= No steps taken to reduce the tensions in the city after Babri masjid
incident.

= No action to counter the spreading of false rumors.
= Lack of preparedness.
= No contingency plan available and implemented.

= Inaction in controlling riots even though they went on for more than
ten weeks.

= No action against culprits despite the death toll of 200-300 people.

4. AGRARIAN UNREST

Case Study of Basheerbagh Firing Incident in connection with agitation on
Electricity Tariff Hike -2000

Reasons for Agitations:

Farmers may incur heavy losses, due to the policies of Government. Ex:
Increase in the prices of fertilizers. Failure of Government in providing MSP
(Minimum Support Price) to agriculture products. Short fall in supply of
electricity, Water, Seeds and fertilizes, Supply of spurious seeds, pesticides,
international agreements, incurring loss due to natural calamities, getting
loss, due to fall in prices of agricultural products, disputes between farmers,
about distribution of water from canals, rivers, cheating farmers by the
business community, are the reasons for agitations by farmers.

Case Study of Basheerbagh Firing Incident -2000

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In the month of May -2000, Andhra Pradesh Electricity Regulatory
Commission notified increase in power tariffs. The allegation was that the
increase in power tariffs was due to the interference of World Bank.
Congress and the nine party left alliance started strong protests across the
state against the government’s decision. All sections of society i.e. farmers,
domestic consumers, traders and industrialists were the worst affected.
Finally the nine left party alliance including extremist ideology based New
Democracy and Congress Party proposed rally in Hyderabad. The permission
sought by these parties was initially refused on 25.08.2000 apprehending
involvement of extremists, disorder and likely hood of violence. The
permission for the rally was accorded on 27.08.2000 imposing conditions of
confining the venue of rally at Indira Park. The assembly sessions were going
on at the time. The left parties were having the intention to defy the

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
confining the venue of rally at Indira Park. The assembly sessions were going
on at the time. The left parties were having the intention to defy the
prohibitory orders and proceed towards Legislative Assembly. The Congress
party was intending to organize protest at Babu Jagjeevanram statue near
L.B.stadium.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Police made elaborate bandobust arrangements by deploying additional
force with riot gear equipment, women police and mounted police. All routes
leading to Assembly were fortified with barricading and concertina coil
fencing. The participants of nine Left Party Alliance numbering about
25,000-30,000 assembled at Indira Park on 28.08.2000. Some Congress
Party workers also joined them The rally started from Indira Park in the
mood of aggression and marched towards Assembly defying the prohibitory
orders. Some unsocial elements and extremists took part in the rally in the
guise of left party workers carrying stones, lathies and weapons with a
preparedness to attack police. The rally crossed Basheerbagh fly over and

50 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
reached the strong barricading erected by police. A strong police force with
riot gear equipment was stationed at Basheerbagh cross roads.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The rallyists attacked the police by pelting stones, beating with lathies
and tried to break the fencing. Police initially fired tear gas shells and
resorted to lathi charge to disperse the mob. The mob continued to be very
violent injuring scores of police personnel. Even after using mounted police,
the mob did not recede and continued the violent activities such as attacks
on police, burning of vehicles and attempt to march towards assembly.
Police initially opened fire with rubber bullets and finally with bullets. Two
agitators died in the firing and one succumbed to the injuries in lathi charge.
One police constable received firearm injury, suspected to have been fired by
the extremists mingled in the rally .About 120 police personnel received
injuries in the incident. Finally police were able to foil the violent attempt to
disrupt the functioning of the highest democratic institution in the state.
Police action in this incident is justified as proportionate to the level of

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Police action in this incident is justified as proportionate to the level of
violence.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
kek
