metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf

page_content:
5. STUDENT UNIONS AND CAMPUS VIOLENCE

Campus Violence

743. The breaches of peace occasioned by students’ violence both in
and out of the campus require special attention of the police. There are
many reasons and occasions when violence breaks out. These may be
broadly classified as internal and external:-

The internal causes relate to purely problems of students relating to
fees, mess facilities, curriculum, examinations, copying, ill treatment by
authorities, scholarships, group rivalries, student elections, ragging,
misconduct of staff members or assault by outsiders on a student, teacher
etc. Some problems may be genuine, some may not be.

The external causes relate to political, social or other causes in which
students or groups of them involve themselves either within the campus or
outside. Like all other situations, information collection is the most
important duty of police and an essential requirement.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The SHO by personal contacts with students and teachers should keep
himself abreast of the affairs involving the groups in the colleges or
Universities in his jurisdiction. The guidelines for dealing with students’
violence or incidents are:

A. Keep the Head of the Institution informed of visits to the campus as far
as possible. Only in an emergency where a violent incident has occurred
or about to take place and where it is not possible to inform, the police
may rush to the spot making arrangements for information to the Head
of the Institution or other responsible person of the Institution.

B. Do not express opinion as to the right or wrong of the cause or person,
but only act according to law.

C. Conduct parlays with rival groups to avoid imminent clash if possible.

D. Take assistance of student leaders or neutral teachers to persuade
against violence.

E. Make prompt arrest if a serious incident like grievous hurt or homicide
has taken place.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
E. Make prompt arrest if a serious incident like grievous hurt or homicide
has taken place.

While proceeding to deal with an outbreak of violence, strong police
force must be taken. All policemen should be doubly briefed on restraint
and conduct.

G. Use of force should be avoided as far as possible, unless there is no
other way to stop further violence.

52 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
H. While making arrests avoid doing so in the full view of the students,
unless it is in the course of dealing with an unlawful assembly.

I. Assist University/College authorities in ensuring that only genuine and
legitimate inmates stay in hostels. Universities and College authorities
should be prevailed by higher police officers in this regard.

J. Collect full and accurate information regarding gangsterism, eve-teasing
or assaults on teachers and act as per relevant law.

Do not act on hearsay or on unverified information.

L. Take all steps as in any other case for investigation of an offence
occurring in the campus or hostels.

M. Assist the University/College authorities for keeping order during
examinations or other activities.

N. Visit the campus during normal times so that it becomes easier to
handle situations when they occur.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
N. Visit the campus during normal times so that it becomes easier to
handle situations when they occur.

O. Always remember that the students, being young and emotional are
prone to flash actions and impulsive and hasty acts. Handling of
situations should be done with proper understanding of the
issues/situation.

P. Where group clashes are likely there should be no hesitation to initiate
action under section 107 CrPC/ 126 BNSS, 2023 and to obtain bonds.

Case Study of OU Campus Unrest/ Violence

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Case Study of OU Campus Unrest/ Violence

Osmania University was established in 1918. It is the seventh oldest in
the country and third oldest in South India, first in the princely state of
Hyderabad. University witnessed many group clashes among different
student unions and organizations. Even killings of opponent student group
leaders were part of the clashes particularly between ABVP and RSU. Many
student leaders lost their lives in the group clashes. OU campus had been
centre of attraction for many agitations and student movements. University
has contributed many political leaders to the society. OU campus is the
nerve centre for Pro - Telangana agitation between years 2009 to 2014.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The leader of the TRS party Sri. K. Chandrasekhar Rao announced a
fast unto death on 29.11.2009, demanding introduction of bill for separate
Telangana state. All student organizations/unions organized protest
programmes in OU campus .The arrest of KCR and self immolation of
Srikantha chary at LB Nagar fuelled the sentiments of students in OU
campus making them unite. All the students started aggressive protest in
OU campus and attempted to come out of campus to spread their protest. In
turn sizable police force was deployed to stop them from coming out of the
campus. Initially police resorted to severe lathi charge on the students for

53 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
dispersal .All news channels transmitted the scenes of use of excessive force
by police which ignited the sentiments of all sections of people leading to the
spread of protest all over Telangana.

Since then protests, stone pelting on police, attempt to take out rallies
outside the campus became day to day affair in the campus. From police
side, deployment of additional force, strong barricading and posting of riot
control vehicles became part of regular duties. Many Pro - Telangana protest
programmes were organized in the campus and some led to students
clashing with police by hurling stones, damaging and burning of police
vehicles. In the later stages, police maintained restraint and used to answer
stone pelting with lobbing of tear gas shells. Use of lathies was secondary
choice for controlling students.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Students had been furious and violent responding to the various calls
given by political JAC and TRS party such as Bandh call, Million March,
Chalo Assembly and Sakala Janula Sammelana etc,. Tensions escalated and
violence reached a peak in the incidents of self sacrifices of students. A
senior officer from Telangana region was entrusted the duty of dealing the
agitation in OU campus which reduced the feeling of enmity between police
and students to a little extent. Despite coming under intense stone attacks
from students, police used to quell them by firing tear gas shells and
resorting to required level of lathi charge, without opening fire in most of the
occasions. Finally the unrest came to an end & after the Telangana Bill was
passed in the Parliament, jubilant atmosphere prevailed.

page_content:
6. TELANGANA AGITATION & VIOLENCE DISCUSSION/ FILM

(Case study of violence on Tankbund during Telangana Agitation)

Condemning the lathi charge on women protesters and RTC workers
during the ‘Chalo Tank Bund’ the Telangana State Road Transport
Corporation (TSRTC) JAC announced that they would stage protests in front
of the residences of MLAs and MLCs.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The early 2011 Telangana protests refers to a chain of events that took
place during the early months of 2011, after the Srikrishna Committee
report was submitted to government of India. These protests were part
of Telangana movement. The Telangana political JAC declared the launch of
a non-cooperation movement throughout Telangana, including the state
capital at Hyderabad, starting on 17 February. The plan was to request
government employees not to work; people not to pay taxes or utility bills;
people not to buy tickets while using public transport; to organise rallies; to
block traffic on highways; and other measures. On 24 February, Telangana
political JAC organised Egypt-like mass protests in Hyderabad beginning 10
March. Projections for number of people to be mobilised varied from one to
five million. Protesters raised slogans of 'Jai Telangana’, sang pro-Telangana
songs, and played games. Protesters included activists of various political

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
songs, and played games. Protesters included activists of various political
parties, students, government employees, lawyers, doctors, teachers,
journalists, writers and cultural artists. 48 people were arrested for
vandalizing the statues.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Non-cooperation movement

The Telangana political JAC declared the launch of a non-cooperation
movement throughout Telangana, including the state capital at Hyderabad,
starting on 17 February. Around 200,000 government employees in
Telangana participated by boycotting work and holding rallies or playing
sports at the workplace while TRS MLAs travelled ticket-less in local trains.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The Telangana JAC called for the shutdown of all services, except
emergency services, in Telangana on 22 and 23 February. Educational
institutions, private and public offices, commercial establishments,
industrial areas, private and public transport, and outpatient services in
hospitals were closed as unions, organisations, and individuals actively
participated in the strike by having rallies, hunger strikes, community meals
on roads, and other protests. The strike was mostly peaceful barring a few
incidents. The state government continued to lose Rs 8 billion per day in
revenue due to the non-cooperation movement.

55 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Protests in Assembly

Legislative Assembly proceedings were disrupted by Telangana
legislators on the first day of the budget session, as the Governor's address
to the assembly did not include the Telangana issue. 22 MLAs and six MLCs
from Congress boycotted the first day of the Assembly session. On 18
February five MLAs were suspended for unruly behaviour and for
manhandling the governor. Deputy Speaker N Manohar termed this as
shameful for democracy.

Incidents of violence

In the first two days of the non-cooperation movement, the government
lost 315 billion in revenue. On 19 February, Telangana protesters burnt
down five buses and set fire to police tents in and around the Osmania
University campus. On 21 February a march of Telangana students and
lawyers to Assembly turned violent when they were blocked by security
forces. Telangana cable operators blocked out TV news channels owned by
Seema-Andhra industrialists citing their alleged 'anti-Telangana' stand.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Pro-Telangana students and lawyers ransacked the MMTS station on
Necklace road and the reservation office was set afire. A car showroom was
ransacked at Habsiguda and an attempt was made to burn a train at Jamia
Osmania station. Three buses of a private engineering college parked near
Habsiguda were set ablaze by suspected Telangana activists on 24
February. On 2 March, Pro-Telangana activists disrupted a film shooting by
burning down the set.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Telangana universities, especially Osmania University, had a heavy
police presence since December 2009. Telangana political JAC
chairman Professor Kodandaram alleged that police from Seema-Andhra
region were very brutal towards Telangana students. The refusal of the police
to allow students to organise rallies provoked pitched battles between police
and students, and arrests of students, which leads to more protests. Police
behaviour was criticised by human rights activists and by the High
Court. On 21 February students hurled stones at the security personnel at
Adikmet and Nizam College hostel at Basheerbagh area, forcing the police to
use tear gas shells to disperse them. Policemen reported that Osmania
University students protesting for Telangana have used petrol bombs to
target police personnel on duty at the campus.

Protests in Parliament

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Protests in Parliament

Telangana MPs belonging to the ruling party protested in Parliament on
22 February for Telangana not being mentioned in the government
agenda. They threatened to immolate themselves on Parliament premises if
Telangana state is not created On 23 February, proceedings in Lok

56 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Sabha were paralysed when opposition parties joined Telangana MPs in
protests. Twelve Telangana MLAs were suspended on 23 February; On 24
February, fifteen members of the ruling party from Telangana joined the
protests. "Asaduddin Owaisi, president of All India Majlis-e-Ittehadul
Muslimeen party, remarked that his party will not allow or want Hyderabad
to be made a Union Territory. He said that if Telangana is created, then
Hyderabad has to be a part of it. On 3 March Parliament proceedings were
disrupted whole day due to protests by Telangana MPs with the support of
BJP members.

Strike call-off

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Strike call-off

On 3 March Telangana employees said they will not call off their
agitation unless there is a firm assurance from the centre on formation of
separate Telangana state. The next day, the state government gave a written
assurance to employees to press the centre for deletion of clause 14 (f) from
the Presidential Order, removing free zone status to Hyderabad, and set up
judicial commission to ensure strict enforcement of GO 610. The state gave
assurances that employees will be permitted a delegation to the Prime
Minister to request the division of the state. Congress legislators from
Telangana ended their boycott on 14 March, citing their responsibility to see
the state budget passed.

MILLION MARCH - 2011

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
MILLION MARCH - 2011

Preparations: On 24 February, TJAC announced that it will organise Egypt-
like mass protests in Hyderabad beginning 10 March.! Projections for
number of people to be mobilised varied from one to five million. Some
parents' associations and teachers appealed to the JAC to postpone the
protest as the gathering of a "million" people might cause inconvenience to
the students appearing for the ongoing Class X, XII and CBSE exams. Three
days before the proposed march, the JAC chairman said that the march
would not be on the scale on which it was originally planned, which included
"laying siege" to Hyderabad, and the march would start in the afternoon
instead of morning, in view of final exams in the morning.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Police issued prohibitory orders to prevent gatherings and set up check-
posts at the district borders of Hyderabad, as no permission had been
granted for the march. Police took a large number of activists into preventive
custody including the Osmania University JAC president and put up
barricades in all nine Telangana districts and on the outskirts of Hyderabad
to prevent activists joining the protest.

Day of the demonstration

On the day of the march, over 12,000 personnel from police and central
paramilitary forces were deployed in the city. Traffic was diverted by police

57 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
and no one was allowed to areas close to Tank Bund, the Secretariat, Raj
Bhavan, and the Assembly. Students clashed with police when they were
locked inside the Osmania University to prevent them from taking out a rally
to Tank Bund. Police fired teargas shells to control the students. Several pro-
Telangana leaders, including 50 MLAs belonging to the BJP, CPI, Muslim
League, TRS, TDP, and Jamat-e-Islami parties and JAC convenor
Kodandaram, were arrested as soon as they started their rally. The police
arrested people coming to Tank Bund area until 1 pm. After 1pm the
activists started reaching Tank Bund in groups from different directions and
outnumbered the police. The protestors uprooted barricades put up on both
ends of the road, and by 3 pm thousands of protestors were gathered and
they raised slogans of ‘Jai Telangana’, sang pro-Telangana songs, and played
games. Protestors included activists of various political parties, students,

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
games. Protestors included activists of various political parties, students,
government employees, lawyers, doctors, teachers, journalists, writers and
cultural artists.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Destruction of statues

Pro-Telangana activists damaged 16 statues representing Telugu
culture and language on Tank Bund. They threw some of the remnants into
the lake. The agitators used iron rods and ropes from the barricades put up
by the police to uproot the statues. The agitators did not touch the statues of
Nizam VI Mir Mahaboob Ali Khan. These statues were installed by state
government in 1986.

The protests were reportedly designed to deepen linguistic and political
divides between pro- and anti-Telangana groups. The police say the attack
was pre-planned. Telangana proponents alleged that the Coastal-
Andhra/Rayalaseema ruling class installed those statues intentionally
ignoring prominent personalities from Telangana. Agitators set a police
vehicle on fire and attacked a vehicle and crew of a TV channels

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
7. ANGANWADI WORKERS & CHALO ASSEMBLY DISCUSSION
(1999-2000)

In the erstwhile united Andhra Pradesh Government, more than one
lakh Anganwadi workers and helpers across the State participated in the
“Chalo Assembly” protest activities for the implementation of their long
pending major demands which includes:-

> The recognition of Anganwadi workers and helpers as Government
Employees.

> They should be covered under the Minimum Wage Act.
> Monthly wage.
> Retirement benefits etc.

The Anganwadi workers were protesting against the anti people/anti
workers policy of the Government, carrying red banners & raising slogans
against the State and Central Government. They organized a huge a rally
from RTC Cross Road en route to Indira Park which was stopped by police.
This compelled women workers to squat in the middle of the road at RTC
cross road, Hyderabad.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
This rashta roko by the agitators forced the traffic into a stand still for
about one and half hour. Such was the collective fury of this massive
gathering of women agitators. This programme was foiled by the police on
the same day and they arrested the leaders.

The repression against Anganwadi workers and their arrests also rocked
the two houses of A.P. Legislature. The opposition parties condemned the
high handed attitude of the ruling State Government (TDP) towards the
women workers. As such, the Assembly proceedings were also stalled.

Sensing that, this agitation is in full throttle, the State Government
negotiated with the agitating Anganwadi workers & Helpers union and
assured that their grievances will be addressed at the earliest.

59 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
8. POLITICAL VIOLENCE AND RIOTS

Measures to deal with factions in villages

738-1. the frequent occurrence of factious rioting in certain areas of the
state is a definite indication of the challenges, which the police have to face
in controlling factious crime effectively. Attention should be paid to factious
villages and all steps appropriate should be taken to prevent factious crimes.
Impartiality, integrity, promptness and firmness are the most essential
qualities required of a police officer in dealing with factions and investigation
of factious cases.

2.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2.

When a faction comes to notice for the first time, efforts should be made
to nip it in the bud. Conciliatory methods may be successful at times. If
the faction persists, there should be no hesitation in setting the relevant
law in motion. All officers from the SP downwards should visit factious
villages and send a message to the factionist that the police would mean
business in dealing with any disturbance or crime and follow up with
necessary measures. The factious villages shall be covered effectively
during day by beat area police officers. Informants should be cultivated
and information network built up. Arrests u/s 151 Cr.P.C. should be
made where ever necessary.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Even a minor incident resulting from a faction should not be ignored. Section
160 IPC/ 194(1) BNS and sections 106 and 107 of Cr.P.C./ 124, 126, BNSS
shall be used. In all cases under section 107 and 126 BNSS of the Code the
Magistrate should be requested to take interim bonds from the parties. In some
cases, it may be necessary to take action under clauses (e) and (f) of section 110
of Cr.P.C/ 129 BNSS against important faction leaders and rowdies. All the
above cases a person who is capable of ensuring good conduct should be
insisted as surety. If police officers furnish all background material on the
sureties it will enable the Magistrate to decide whether to accept the surety or
not and jail the persons if sureties are not to his satisfaction.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Even after the parties to a faction have been bound over there should be no
relaxation of vigilance in regard to that faction, as instances are not wanting in
which even after action has been taken under the security sections of the Code
of Criminal Procedure, factious parties have committed overt acts and indulged
in sudden outbursts of violence. Every such incident, however small including
the attempts should be taken cognizance of. The offenders should not only be
charged for the specific offence but also action taken for forfeiture of their
bonds. If, in spite of all these measures, factionists continue to be active, the
Superintendent of Police should have no hesitation to come up with proposals
for quartering additional Police in the area.

60 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
5. All incidents arising out of a faction should be recorded in the general diary
forthwith. An exhaustive note about the faction and its leaders and members
with their antecedents, as well as information collected from time to time by
Constables of the beat should be made in Part IV of the concerned Station
Crime History by the SHO. Rowdy sheets should also be opened for those who
are persistent factionists.

Case Study: Violence after assassination of Vangaveeti Mohanaranga in
1988

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Case Study: Violence after assassination of Vangaveeti Mohanaranga in
1988

Vangaveeti Ranga entered public life after his brother Vangaveeti Radha
Krishna was killed following group factions with Chalasani Venkata Rathnam
group. His political career began in 1981when he ran in the municipal election and
the Congress Party withdrew its official candidate in his favor. In 1979 , Devineni
Gandhi was murdered by Ranga followers. With this , Ranga took over the control of
Vijayawada. In the year 1983, with the entry of Telugu Desam Party (TDP), Devineni
family sensed an opportunity and joined TDP. Deveneni won Kankipadu
constituency and became MLA. With this Ranga also stepped into politics and
joined congress.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In the 1985, mid-term elections, congress nominated Vangaveeti Mohana
Rana. Ruling government with their influence, registered few criminal cases against
Ranga and got him arrested. Ranga started his campaign from jail. He won the
elections and became MLA from North Vijayawada. He became the voice of Kapu
community. On December 26, 1988 during the early hours of morning, thugs in
four private buses came in Ayyaswamy mala dress and attacked Ranga and his
followers with bombs and swords, knives and sickles. Vangaveeti Mohan ranga was
killed brutally on that day. The killing of Ranga sparked unprecedented caste
rioting in entire coastal region leading to scores of deaths and destruction of
property worth billions of rupees. Kamma community businesses were looted and
burnt. The violence in the city was so huge that curfew was imposed in Vijayawada
for around 2 months.

shiek

page_content:
9. PREPARATION OF BANDOBUST SCHEMES

Establishment of Control Room and Temporary Police Station
Preparation of Diagrams and Schemes for Major Events like

1. Fairs and Festivals (PSO 305-1).

2. Religious festivals and processions.

3. Large scale agitations.

The Duties of Bandobust:

Bandh-o-bust is an Urdu word. The protective arrangements made in
connection with an issue or event is called bandobust arrangement. The
process is called, Bandobust. Bandobust arrangements will be made, in
connection with the visit of VIPs, public meetings, Dharnas, Bandhs , Rastha
rokos and festivals, by drawing Police forces from other places, for security
arrangements, if necessary. In each and every bandobust, senior Officers will
prepare the scheme. It is called bandobust scheme. The British Police have
aptly analyzed it and prepared a model, which is called as IIMAC — Model.
This model can be followed for all types of bandobust duties. It consists of 5
aspects:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
1. Information and | Means of collection of information (secret and
Intelligence open) about the incident and its details.
2. Intention The aim and role of Police.
3. Method Method of arrangements.
4. Administration How to divide Police into different teams.
5. Communication Co-ordination between Police teams and method of
communication between the teams

Information and Intelligence: Police must have knowledge about the
following aspects:

1. Type of meeting which is going to take place, i.e., Political or Religious
or some other type.

2. Particulars of the leaders participating in the meeting.
Date, time and place of the meeting.

4. The success of Police entirely depends upon the collection of
Intelligence. Antecedents and background of the leaders who are
participating in the meeting have to be ascertained.

5. Ascertain the reason and background of the meeting, including the local
or any other special issues involved.

62 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
6. Whether there is any scope for clashes? If so, who are the parties to the
clash? Are they any beneficiaries?

7. At the time of meeting, a watch should be kept on unsocial elements
and their activities.

8. How public are being gathered? Whether they are coming voluntarily
are they being transported in vehicles?

9. Role of various persons, like local contractors, arrack producers and
business people, behind the gathering of the public. Particulars of
vehicles used for the purpose of transportation.

0. Whether any arrack is being distributed.

1.Whether the public are being gathered with any false promises or
coercion and other threats.

2.There are so many occasions, wherein the village elders are threatened
for collection of people, by extremists and sympathizers, for their
meetings.

3. Whether any special arrangements are made, in connection with the
arrival of political leaders.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. Whether any special arrangements are made, in connection with the
arrival of political leaders.

4.Collection of information, about the threat or danger to the VIPs
whoever attend the meeting, from their political rivals.

5. Surveillance has to be kept on the antisocial elements and the activities
of opposition parties.

6.If there is information about such anti social elements, proper
preventive measures must be taken.

Intention:

The aim of Police is to see that the permitted meetings should be
conducted peacefully.

2. Political leaders have to be provided with security as per threat
erception report.

P

3. The aim of Police is to see that no violence takes place. In case it goes
out of control and violence has prevailed Police should be ready to use
necessary force to curb the violence.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
4. When it is decided to use force and the situation warrants, it must be
decided in advance to use force to the minimum extent. Ex:- The
farmers are agitating against Govt. Opening of fire against the farmers
may be get a bad name to Govt. As far as possible, it should be avoided.

5. Opening fire can be done in unavoidable circumstances to protect the
lives of innocent public and to protect their properties and for self
defense.

63 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Method:

1.

As per the information available, co-operation of other departments is
required. To get co-operation from other departments, necessary steps
should be taken.

[he entire place of bandobust has to be divided into sectors. Depending
upon the necessity and its importance, the force has to be deployed.

[The armed forces have to be drawn, as per the quantum of expected
gathering and it has to be decided before hand, in which sectors they
have to be posted.

Administration:

1.

9.

[The requirement of force (rank wise) for bandobust including the places
irom which it is going to be drawn, must be noted down.

n case of important bandobust duties, the rehearsals have to be
conducted, once or twice, as required, so that, every member of the
force will be acquainted with their duties.

When there is no scope for rehearsals, the senior Officers will assemble
the Officers and men and explain about the duties of each and every
person to perform their duties and the plan.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
[he principle of right man at the right place has to be followed and
active Officers have to be posted in the most important sectors, in view
of the security angle.

[he plan for fuel to the vehicles has to be prepared for the movement of
forces well in advance.

[The required advances, TA & DA, has to be drawn and provided.
Boarding and lodging facilities, have to be provided for the

[The food packets have to be supplied to the place of posting for the
Officers and men who are posted for important sectors and where there
is no possibility of leaving the place of bandobust.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Co-ordination with other departments has to be maintained. For
example: The R & B department will make required arrangements at the
place of meeting, the sound Engineers, Telephone Engineers, Medical
Officers, Sanitary Officers Water Supply Officers etc. If the meeting is
conducted during night time, the electricity department will make
arrangements for uninterrupted power supply and also alternate
generator facility.

All kinds of co-operation is required from the organizers of the meeting.

10. If there are volunteers on behalf of organizers, their duties must be

decided and allotted.

64 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Communications:

1. Chain of command is important in Police department. The chain of
command means whose orders are to be obeyed and who has to follow
the orders from whom. That should to be decided, beforehand itself.

2. Who is the sector in-charge Officer? Who are the Officers nominated in
the sector? That has to be clearly defined in the duty chart.

3. What type of communication facilities are to be provided to other
departments, what types of communication sets are to be provided, and
up to what rank Officers have to be provided, with communication sets,
has to be decided.

4. Who is the in-charge Officer, at the place of meeting and what type of
communication equipment should be there in between the I/C Officer
and section in-charge Officers, has to be decided (Cell No. VHF set) etc.

The section Officer has to communicate briefly and precisely.

The telephone numbers of other departments to be provided to the
persons deployed for bandobust duty.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The telephone numbers of other departments to be provided to the
persons deployed for bandobust duty.

7. Ambulance has to be provided.

Maintenance of large gathering or huge crowd in a peaceful manner is a
challenge to Police department. The situation has to be assessed well in
advance, proper arrangements are required for conducting peaceful
bandobust, without giving scope for occurrence of any untoward incidents.
The image of Police will be boosted in public and also organizers, if we are
able to conduct bandobust, without any incidents.

ESTABLISHMENT OF CONTROL ROOM AND TEMPORORY POLICE
STATION

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
ESTABLISHMENT OF CONTROL ROOM AND TEMPORORY POLICE
STATION

If there is large gathering, in a fair, a temporary Police station has to be
established. The temporary Police Station has to be established at a
reasonable distance, from the place of fair, preferably in a temporary
building, shed or a tent. General diary, FIR book and other essential records,
have to be made available in the PS to facilitate the registration and
investigation of cases. Cognizable cases reported during the festival will be
registered at the temporary police station and investigated.

Bandobust in Fairs and Festivals (S.O. No. 305,307):

In each Police station, a list has to be prepared about,
“+ fairs,

* festivals and

“ Shandy days, in its jurisdiction.

65 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The required bandobust arrangements have to be made from Police
station strength and the circle if necessary, during the above fairs and
festivals. If large number of civil Police force and A.R force is required, it
must be drawn by making a requisition to the SP of the district. The entire
force drawn for bandobust must be assembled in the Headquarters of Police
station and the staff must be briefed about their duties, either by CI or SI.

At the place of fair, the organizers may erect shamiyanas and tents for
the public. Prevention of any damage and other loss due to unexpected fire
accident is the responsibility of Police. Multiple exits must be arranged for
the speedier exit of public, from the place of accident. The men drafted for
fair bandobust, should not take their families along with them.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The men drafted for bandobust, should not move unnecessarily, in the
fair, when they are not on duty. The accommodation for the bandobust duty
personnel has to be arranged far away, from busy public movement. The
Constable, who is posted on bandobust duty, must be issued a duty ticket
with clear instructions of the duty to be performed. Police have to co-operate
with sanitary department to maintain cleanliness.

The important duty of Police is to see that there should not be any
obstructions to organizers, and to prevent crime and maintenance of law and
order. They have to keep watch over the movement of criminals. They have to
trace the missing children, if any and hand them over to their parents. If
contagious diseases like cholera and chicken pox break out, immediately it
has to be brought to the notice of Health Department.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Police has to caution public not to drink contaminated water, under
such circumstances. The queue has to be maintained at the temples, at the
time of attending pooja in temple. The duty duration of the bandobust PCs,
should not be more than 6 hours.

Police Officers have to keep a watch and should not allow gambling in
the fairs and any organizer of such gambling should be dealt firmly, by
taking stern action against him. In fairs, if there is customary chariot
procession, Police should insist on the temple authorities to get a fitness
certificate of the chariot, from R&B engineering authorities. Police are
competent to stop such procession, if the certificate is not obtained by the
temple authorities.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The duty of Police is to see, that there should not be any harm to
public. If accidentally, fire breaks out, Police shall immediately rush to the
spot and take necessary steps, to put off the fire and call for the fire services,
to stop further spreading of fire. Whenever such accident takes place, there
will be a panic among the public and taking advantage of the situation, there
is every possibility of thefts Police should take necessary steps, to prevent
such offences. If the public or cattle are caught in fire, Police have to take
immediate steps to rescue them.

66 | Page

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
10. COMMUNAL RIOTS AND RIOT CONTROL SCHEME

“ Riot control measures.

“Riot control equipment (display of riot control equipment-demo).
“* Patrolling and picketing.

“Confidence building in public.

“ Media management during unrest.

RIOT CONTROL MEASURES

Implement the riot or contingency scheme in the affected area.
Magistrate to issue prohibitory orders under section 144 Cr.P.C/ Sec 163
BNSS In case the situation warrants curfew should be declared. Sector wise
division of effected area should be made each under a Gazetted police officer
and Magistrate for intensive supervision of police duties and patrolling for
handling of law and order. Any trouble should be promptly identified and
tackled. Requisition should be made for additional police reserves .In case it
is difficult to handle the disturbance curfew should be clamped and roads
cleared of crowds. Arrests of anti-social elements, searches for illicit
weapons, and other objectionable material should be carried out.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Licenses of arms issued to undesirable persons should be suspended,
Arms and ammunition shops should be sealed and guarded, stocks of acid
and explosive materials shops of fireworks manufacturers should also be
sealed. Duties should also be detailed at hospitals and mortuaries in order
to maintain law and order and also to prevent emotionally surcharged
crowds from taking away the body of a deceased person. Suitable residents
of a locality may be appointed as special police officers under section 17 of
the Police Act. Action can also be taken to have the riot affected locality or
town declared as a disturbed area under section 15 of the Police Act.

Special squads should be set to work for quick investigation of riot
cases bringing the guilty to book. Peace committees should be established
and dialogue between the communities opened to bridge the gulf. All places
of worship must be immediately places under watch/ guard to prevent
mischief.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Rumors should be promptly controlled and proper follow up measures
should be taken if found true. Contradiction of false rumors and stern action
against rumor mongers to be taken. Watch on railway station, bus stands,
dharmashalas & hotels should be maintained for movement of suspicious
characters. All points of ingress in the affected area should be guarded and

67 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
suspicious characters checked for objectionable articles and weapons.
Superior officers should be kept informed about developments.

RIOT CONTROL EQUIPMENT

For protection, officers performing riot control will often wear protective
helmets and carry riot shields. These are designed to protect the wearer from
hurled objects such as bottles and bricks. The gear is worn by riot control
officers to protect their body with no vulnerable spots to exploit.

For example, the helmets worn by riot control officers have an
additional outward-extending part that protects the back of the neck from
assault. To provide even greater protection, the protective equipment often
provides ballistic protection. If tear gas or other riot control agents are to be
used, gas masks may be needed. The initial choice of tactics determines the
type of offensive equipment used.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The base choice is between lethal (e.g. shotgun) and less-than-lethal
weaponry (e.g. tear gas, pepper spray, plastic bullets, batons, and other in
capacitants). The decision is based on the perceived level of threat and the
existing laws. In many countries it is illegal to use lethal force to control riots
in all but the most extreme circumstances. Special riot hand weapons
include the wooden or rubber baton; the Indian lathi, a long cane with a
blunt metal tip. Vehicle-mounted water cannons may serve to augment
personal weapons. Some water cannons let police add dye to mark rioters or
tear gas to help disperse the crowds.

PATROLLING AND PICKETING

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
PATROLLING AND PICKETING

The patrolling party in communally sensitive and disturbed areas, must
prevent offences and maintain law and order in that area. During patrolling,
a watch should be kept, over the movements of suspicious persons,
strangers and checking of vehicles should be done to unearth unlicensed
weapons, country made bombs and other deadly weapons. In the patrolling
area, if there is any information about commission of on offence, the
information has to be passed on to higher Officers and see that no offence
takes place.

If any incident takes place and information is received for Police
presence, the patrolling party should rush to the spot immediately. The
patrolling party has to keep a watch and conduct surprise checks on the
houses of ex-convicts, anti social elements and if any slightest suspicion
arises, they should be taken into custody and necessary enquiries must be
caused. The patrolling party must inform the control room about their
location promptly.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content: 68 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Pickets posted at communally sensitive areas have to keep close watch
over the persons encouraging communal hatred feelings and also on
communal leaders. They have to create confidence in the minds of the
public. Prior information has to be collected. They have to prevent offences.
Information collected, has to be passed on to superior Officers and they
should act as per the instructions of Officers.

One should not leave the picket and proceed on his personel work. They
have to maintain good relations with Mytri members, peace committee
members and voluntary organizations. Any incident, even trivial in nature,
should not be neglected. Pickets posted at Temples, Mosques have to take
precautions to see that the feelings of public are not hurt. They must see
that the prayers and poojas are performed as per custom. Care should be
taken to see, that anti social elements, rowdies, goondas, communal
offenders, do not take shelter in temples and mosques.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
CONFIDENCE BUILDING IN PUBLIC
After dispersal of riotous mob measures should be taken for building
confidence among victims and common public. Post pickets by reducing the

man power. Sec. 144 Cr.P.C./ 163 BNSS may be continued. Curfew may be
relaxed to assess the situation.

MEDIA MANAGEMENT DURING UNREST

Media plays an important role during unrest. The potential of media can
be tapped in maintaining public order. Sometimes the media also plays a
role in spreading prejudices, as it is needless to say that they are more
interested in sensational news than sensible constructive news. Press plays
an extremely important role in the context of a law and order problem. It can

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
be effectively used to counteract rumors, make the public aware of the
preparedness of the administration and creating an environment of
communal harmony. However, at times a sensational news item can
seriously jeopardize communal peace. It is in this context that news items
need to be carefully scrutinized, for which a lot of administrative tact is
required in dealing with the media.

Briefing & Debriefing:

Briefing should set the style and tone for an operation. Briefing provides
the information needed to direct deployed resources. This information is also
used for debriefing personnel in order to obtain further relevant, available
information.

Briefing and Debriefing process and can be broken down into five key
elements:

1) Briefing of intelligence.

2) Individual and team tasking.
3) Delivery methods

4) Debriefing.

5) Results.

Briefing and Debriefing Process:

Briefing:

All police personnel should be thoroughly briefed on the purpose of an
operation prior to participating in it. The time available for briefing will vary,
depending on the circumstances. Briefings can also be used to inform
personnel about how to respond to the media.

This should be tailored to the needs of the team. For example, a team
returning to work after rest days should be provided with facilities to self
brief on historical issues, but a formal briefing on current and relevant
tasking for that day is required.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The operation order is a document that links basic information
regarding an event or incident with the structure of the police response and ,
primarily, the operational resource requirement. The purpose of an operation
order is to focus and coordinate the police response towards specific
objectives, by describing how resources are to be deployed.

Any staff coming into contact with the media or members of the public
should be given general guidance about the information that they should or
should not release. A generic media aide memoire may be distributed to all
personnel as general guidance.

Briefing objectives:

Personal briefing styles vary, but there are certain key objectives to meet in a
briefing. These are to

75 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
1. Ensure that the team has assimilated the relevant information
contained within the briefing (this can be checked by conducting
random knowledge checks to confirm understanding.)

2. Ensure that individual members of staff understand their responsibility
for the allocated task.

3. Confirm there are sufficient resources to conduct the required tasks
(this includes situations where staff may self brief, for example at
remote stations).

Debriefing:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Debriefing:

The purpose of debriefing is to identify good practices and areas for
improvement which could include organizational learning. Pressure on
operational staff can lead to debriefing being overlooked. This should not be
allowed to happen as effective debriefing will ultimately minimize
bureaucracy and streamline current procedures. The debriefing process
should have a structure and begin in the early stage of an operation so that
those personnel deployed in the initial phases can provide information for
later phases of the police response. A full record of the debriefing should be
retained for audit and disclosure purposes.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Effective debriefing leads to many positive results for an organization.
The use of the national briefing model in forces ensures a consistent flow of
communication between different departments, and from managers to patrol
and neighborhood teams. This means that more staff has a_ better
understanding of their role in the organization and how they can contribute
to performance. It also allows more information to be collected, which in turn
assists future decision making.

Debriefing process should be incorporated into the daily business of
policing. When this happens, there are significant outcomes for the
organization as a whole and there are more outputs from operational teams
in respect of intelligence arrests and public reassurance.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The evaluation of the outcomes and processes of operations enhances
corporate knowledge and develops the expertise of staff involved. This is
particularly relevant in an emergency or during major incidents when several
organizations would be involved. A greater understanding of policing issues
leads to improved effectiveness of future operations.

Following the conclusion of an emergency and or major incident, a
report should be produced detailing the experiences gained by those
organizations involved in it. This benefits the wider policing community,
emergency services and other organizations.

76 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The strategic coordinating group determines how a multi agency report
will be compiled and circulated.

Dissemination

Information and other products from the debriefing process should be
circulated at local and force level so that they are accessible to all personnel.
This ensures that organizational memory systems can grow and develop.

Debriefing structure

Staff debriefing is likely to be one of the most productive areas for
capturing information. A debriefing must be tailored to the needs of the team
in question, but the structure, purpose and focus of the debriefing should be
consistent. Although a debriefing should ideally occur at the end of a shift,
this may not always be possible. A hot debrief should always be conducted
at the end of a police operation.

Contingency planning:

The contingency planning section is made up of a small team of police
officers and police staff and is based at force Head Quarters.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The contingency planning section is made up of a small team of police
officers and police staff and is based at force Head Quarters.

The main aim of the section is to prepare and maintain emergency
management procedures by police in the event of a major incident or
disaster.

A contingency plan should be a simple, concise and flexible document
that is easily understood and can be revised and updated as circumstances
change. It should be subject to the same decision making rationale as any
other police action.

The contingency plan should be accessible to those who require it
before, during and after an incident. Forces should consider testing their
contingency and emergency plans through training and exercising, as
appropriate.

Identifying contingencies:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Identifying contingencies:

A contingency plan is a process that prepares an organization to
respond effectively and coherently to an unplanned event. A contingency
plan can be also used as an alternative action plan if the original plan fails
to generate the desired result.

Development of a contingency plan is a team task, with involvement of
staff from each functional area of a business. The plan must have support
from the top down.

What are typical events which require contingency planning?

= Natural disasters such as fire, flood etc.

77 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
= Loss of data/IT security breach.
= Fraud.

= PR disaster.

= Loss of key personnel.

= Identify potential risks.

= Prioritize these by assessing what impact they might have on the
operation of the organization.

Steps in developing a contingence plan:

= Identify sources of outside assistance such as the emergency services.
nvolve relevant outside agencies in your plan.

= Identify key milestones along the way to becoming fully operational and
ways in which each will be achieved.

= Test your plan. Run simulations to ensure your plan has the desired
result. This will also include staff training to ensure their readiness.

= Monitor the plan. Regularly review the plan to ensure it includes new
potential risks.

78 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
12. USE OF FORCE

“ Dispersal of unlawful assembly.

“Crowd control / mob operation.

“Use of excessive force and lethal weapons (lathi charge / opening of fire)
“ RPD ( PSO 740-3) — Cr.P.C sec 129-131(Sec 148 —- 150 BNSS)
DIPERSAL OF UNLAWFUL ASSEMBLY

An unlawful assembly may be ordered to disperse by a Magistrate or
officer-in-charge of a Police Station and when so ordered, it is the duty of the
members of the unlawful assembly to disperse. If they do not, force may be
used to disperse them. Any Police Officer may without warrant arrest the
members of an unlawful assembly and thus disperse the assembly. The
Police must secure the presence of an Executive Magistrate where a breach
of the peace necessitating the use of force is anticipated. The Executive
Magistrate on information or requisition by police should be present at the
spot.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
When a Magistrate is present at the spot he should be in complete
charge of the situation. He has the necessary legal powers to order any
Police Officer to assist him in handling the situation. The senior most Police
Officer present is bound to assist the Magistrate by mobilizing the available
Police Force and utilizing them as best as possible.

If the crowd becomes defiant and the use of teargas, lathi charge or
both fail in their purpose, use of firearms may be resorted to. The Magistrate
is responsible for taking a decision as to when an unlawful assembly has to
be dispersed by force and also the kind of force to be used. After such a
decision is taken, the officer-in-charge of the Police is solely responsible for
deciding the manner and amount of force to be used keeping in mind the
principle that no more force and no longer than necessary should be used.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
When a Police party is formed for dispersing an unlawful assembly, it
should be numbered and split off into two or more sections, If the size of the
party and the time available admit of it. Each section should be under the
charge of an officer.

CROWD CONTROL / MOB OPERATION

Some of the methods which can be used to reduce the excitement and
tension in a crowd to bring it down to a manageable stage are mentioned
below.

Persuasive appeals: - persuasive appeals to a crowd should be made in
such a manner as to make the crowd feel that the police are on its side. The

79 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
technique of successful persuasion depends on the characteristics of the
crowd being dealt with;

1.

A police officer must be aware of the local sentiments and the traits
which can sway a crowd.

The police officer should be able to judge the changing mood of the
crowd and adapt his strategy to control it accordingly.

Humorous and witty remarks at an early stage of crowd collection often
break down the mob’s hostility.

Assistance of persons of acknowledged Influence to talk to the leaders
will help.

Police officers must, therefore, cultivate good relations with public
personages and local influential persons.

f use of force is contemplated to disperse the crowd, remember that the
leaders are not always in the front of the crowd.

Attitude of the Police: Calm and Silent

1.

aos

n successful crowd control, much depends upon the emotional stability
of the leader of the police and his ability to stay calm even under the
gravest of circumstances.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
f an officer has built up a reputation of being fair and firm he can often
succeed in persuading an excited crowd to disperse.

Getting agitated persons to sit down and write their complaint diverts
their mind from violent action and brings down their agitation.

Try to avoid negotiations on the road

Moving the excited persons to the safer confine of the P.S. breaks down
the hostility in the mob.

An Unexpected distraction

This can often help to save a situation. For instance, an accident

occurring at the time crowd formation can result in it’s;

Making a crowd wait: Sometimes excitement in a mob can be reduced just
by keeping it waiting for a prolonged time. This will make the hangers on to
leave the scene.

Use of Public address system and Movie/Video Camera

1.

Use powerful public address systems at the outer edge of the crowd,
directing the people to break up and go home.

neutralize the influence of the crowd excitement.

Use of a video camera recording helps in identification in case of trouble
and provides evidence against crowd.

OPENING OF FIRE)

1)

2)

3)

Exercise an extreme caution and discretion regarding the extent and the
line of fire.

The officer in command of the party will bring the men to the loading
position and give the order to load the ammunition.

Officers commanding Police parties will, ensure that the fullest warning
is given to the mob in a clear and distinct manner before any order is
given to use tear gas or lathies or fire arms.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
a) Firing should be from a distance sufficient to obviate the risk of
being rushed on and to enable strict fire control.

b) Riot flags and display banners should be taken when District
Reserves are called out in apprehension of disturbances and before
firing or any other means of dispersal is resorted

In the event of the Police party being forced to open fire, effectual means
to be ensured.

Whenever firing is resorted to, directions and warnings to the mobs
should be announced through loudspeakers or hand megaphones.

The fire order of each section will be given, by the regular word of
command by the officer-in-charge of the party.

Firing should be well controlled and officers ordering firing should
always indicate the number of rounds that should be fired at any
particular time.

Such firing should be resorted with blank ammunition only for
dispersing violent mobs.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf

page_content:
Such firing should be resorted with blank ammunition only for
dispersing violent mobs.

Firing with live ammunition should be directed against the most
threatening part of the crowd, the aim being kept low.

10) Firing should cease the moment it is no longer necessary.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf

page_content:
11)The Police used for dispersing mobs should wear the prescribed
uniforms and steel helmets.

RPD (PSO 740-3) - Cr.P.C sec 129-131/ Sec 148 - 150 BNSS

The right of private defense can be exercised to protect the life and
property of public or to protect oneself. This right can be exercised by using
force as much as is necessary and as long as it is necessary. This right
extends even to the causing of death in certain cases as laid down in section
100 IPC/ Sec 38, BNS as against body and in section 103 IPC/ Sec 41, BNS
as against property.

The police should exercise this right cautiously. Any amount of
exceeding the right may make them liable for penal action as per law.
Therefore, the police officers must make a judicious use of this right, only in
dire need to save the life and property, when occasion arises as shown in
sections 100 and 103 IPC/ Sec 38, 41, BNS.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Sec 129 Cr.P.C/ Sec 148, BNSS Dispersal of assembly by use of civil
force. (1) Any executive Magistrate or officer in charge of a police station, not
below the rank of a sub-inspector may command any unlawful assembly, or
any assembly of five or more persons likely to cause a disturbance of the
public peace, to disperse; (2) If, upon being so commanded, any such
assembly does not disperse, or if, it conducts itself in such a manner as to
shows a determination not to disperse, the commanding person may proceed
to disperse such assembly by force, if necessary can arrest and confine the
persons.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Sec 130 Cr.P.C/ Sec 149 BNSS Use of armed forces to disperse
assembly. (1) If any such assembly cannot be dispersed, the Executive
Magistrate who is present may cause it to be dispersed by the armed forces.
(2) Such Magistrate/ District magistrate may require any officer in command
of any group of persons belonging to the armed forces to disperse the
assembly with the help of the armed forces under his command, and to
arrest and confine such persons forming part of it as the Magistrate may
direct, or as it may be necessary to arrest and confine in order to disperse
the assembly or to have them punished according to law. (3) Every such
officer in so doing shall use as little force as possible.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Sec 131 Cr.P.C/ Sec 150 BNSS Power of certain armed force officers to
disperse assembly. When the public security is endangered by assembly
and no Executive Magistrate can be communicated with, any commissioned
or gazetted officer of the armed forces may disperse such assembly with the
help of the armed forces under his command, and may arrest and confine
any persons forming part of it, in order to disperse such assembly.

Sec 100 IPC/ Sec 150 BNS When the right of private defence of the
body extends to causing death.--The right of private defence of the body
extends, under the restrictions mentioned in the last preceding section, to
the voluntary causing of death or of any other harm to the assailant, if the
offence which occasions the exercise of the right be of any of the descriptions
hereinafter enumerated, namely:-

1. Assault as may reasonably cause the apprehension that death will
otherwise be the consequence of such assault;

2. Assault as may reasonably cause the apprehension that grievous hurt
will be the consequence of assault

An assault with the intention of committing rape;
An assault with the intention of gratifying unnatural lust;

An assault with the intention of kidnapping or abducting;

anu Fw

An assault with the intention of wrongfully confining a person, which
may reasonably cause him to apprehend that he will be unable to have
recourse to the public authorities for his release.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
7. An act of throwing or administrating acid or an attempt to throw or
administer acid which may reasonably cause the apprehension that
grievous hurt will otherwise be the consequence of such act.

Sec 103 IPC/ Sec 41 BNS When the right of private defense of property
extends to causing death: The right of private defense of property extends,
to the voluntary causing of death or of any other harm to the wrong-doer, if
the offence, the committing of which, or the attempting to commit which,
occasions the exercise of the right, be an offence of any of the descriptions
hereinafter enumerated, namely:-

1. Robbery;
2. House-breaking by night;

3. Mischief by fire committed on any building, tent or vessel, human
dwelling, a place for the custody of property;

4. Theft, mischief, or house-trespass.

page_content:
13. INVESTIGATION OF CASES

ey

“ Rioting
“Police Firing
RIOTING
Riots

A riot is a form of civil disorder by disorganized groups lashing out in a
sudden and intense rash of violence against authority, property or people.
While individuals may attempt to lead or control a riot, riots are thought to

be typically chaotic and exhibit herd behaviour, and are usually generated
by civil unrest.

What does IPC/BNS say:
Sec. 146/ Sec 191(1) Rioting:

Whenever force or violence is used by an unlawful assembly, or by any
member thereof, in prosecution of the common object of such assembly,
every member of such assembly is guilty of the offence of rioting.
Sec 147 of IPC/ Sec 191(2) BNS: Punishment for rioting.

Whoever is guilty of rioting shall be punished with imprisonment of
either description for a term which may extend to two years, or with fine, or
with both.

Sec 148/ Sec 191(3) BNS Rioting, armed with deadly weapon

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Sec 148/ Sec 191(3) BNS Rioting, armed with deadly weapon

Whoever is guilty of rioting, being armed with a deadly weapon or with
anything which, used as a weapon of offence, is likely to cause death, shall
be punished with imprisonment of either description for a term which may
extend to five years, or with fine, or with both.

Riots often occur in reaction to a perceived grievance or out of dissent.
Riots occur due to poor working or living conditions, conflicts between ethnic
groups, or religions, the outcome of a sporting event or frustration with legal
channels through which to air grievances.

Riots typically involve vandalism and the destruction of private and
public property. The specific property to be targeted varies depending on the
riot and the inclinations of those involved. Targets can include shops, cars,
restaurants, state-owned institutions, RTC buses and religious buildings.

Types of Riots

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Types of Riots

e A police riot is unlawful use of force by police against public,
commonly where police attack a group of peaceful civilians and/or
provoke peaceful civilians into violence.

84 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Student riots are riots precipitated by students, often in higher
education, such as a college/university. Student riots are often
political in nature, although student riots can occur as a result of
peaceful demonstration opposed by the Police. Students may
constitute an active political force like OUJAC, and student riots may
occur in the context of wider political or social grievances.

In a caste riot, riots occur when a mob of a majority caste commit
crime against lower caste people.

In a religious riot, the rioting mob targets people and properties of a
specific religion, or those believed to belong to that religion.

A prison riot is a type of large scale disorder by a group of prisoners
against the prison administrators, prison officers, or other groups of
prisoners, often to express a grievance, in an attempt to force change
or an attempt to escape the prison.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Urban riots occur due to conditions such as discrimination, poverty,
high unemployment, poor schools, poor healthcare, housing
inadequacy and police brutality and bias. Urban riots are closely
associated with police riots. In India, for instance, caste riots are
limited to rural areas while religious riots occur in cities.

Sports riots can be sparked by the losing or winning of team. When a
team loses badly, the general public react in a violent way attacking
the houses of the team members, destroying public property etc.

Food riots are caused by harvest failures, incompetent food storage,
hoarding of fertilisers etc. When the farmers become desperate in
such conditions, they attack shops, farms, homes, or government
buildings.

How to Control Riots

Riots are typically dealt with by the police. Tactics and weapons used

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
How to Control Riots

Riots are typically dealt with by the police. Tactics and weapons used

can include water cannons, plastic bullets, rubber bullets, pepper spray,
Lathi charge, dogs, Mounted Police, barbed wire etc. Also many states have
RAPID ACTION FORCES to control riots, who carry all the above weapons.

Preparations:
1. Talk to the Local Leaders and the Union Leaders who are leading the
crowd.
2. Prepare a bandoubust scheme in advance with sector wise force
required.
3. Arrange for barbed wire before hand and erect the same at strategic

points.

the force.

Have riot gear, helmets, stone guards, lathis, teargas, rubber bullets
etc ready.

Keep women police officers ready to arrest women protestors.

Keep vans/ambulances and other vehicles ready to transfer the
protestors to distance places.

Keep communication ready to inform the superiors of developments.

Keep back up force ready at safe distance to reach the scene in case of
emergencies.

Follow all the rules and regulations regarding the crowd control/
advice, persuasion & warning.

Give sufficient warning before taking a final decision.
Once riot starts, use only required force not more than that.

If the riots continue for long, use the services of local leaders to calm
the public and _ request not to believe rumours.

Implement 144 sec/ 189(4) BNS strictly.

Use the Media/Press to condemn rumours and instill confidence in
general public.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf

Implement 144 sec/ 189(4) BNS strictly.

Use the Media/Press to condemn rumours and instill confidence in
general public.

Identify trouble mongers and arrest them without delay.
Post pickets at important points.

Arrange for foot and mobile patrols at all sensitive points.
Organize searches and seize dangerous weapons.

The force should not be removed till the situation is completely under
control.

GUIDE LINES OF SUPREME COURT OF INDIA IN CRIMINAL APPEAL NO.1255
OF 1999

1.
2.

Receipt of any intelligence or tip off shall be reduced into writing.

Pursuant to the tip off, encounter takes place and fire arm is used by
the police party and a result of that, death occurs. An FIR shall be
registered and forwarded to court without any delay.

An independent investigation should be taken by the CID or police
team of another police station under the leadership of a higher rank
officer.

The investigation team shall seek :
a) Colour photographs of the victims for identification.

b) Statements of the witnesses including police personnel involved to
identify the scene.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
b) Statements of the witnesses including police personnel involved to
identify the scene.

c) Photo or the video of the scene and physical evidence to determine
cause, manner, location.

d) Lifting of finger prints of the deceased or any other finger prints to
be sent for chemical analysis.

e) Post mortem by two doctors including head of a District Hospital
under video graph.

f) Collection and preservation of any evidence of weapons and test
for gunshot residue and trace metal.

Magisterial enquiry under 176 Cr.P.C/ Sec 196 BNSS and report must

be sent to judicial magistrate under section 190 Cr.P.C/ Sec 210
BNSS.

Information of the incident must be sent to NHRC/SHRC without any
delay.

No delay in sending FIR, diary entries, panchanama, sketch etc., to
the court.

After full investigation in to the incident report under section 173
Cr.P.C/ Sec. 193 BNSS must be sent to competent court.

police firing must be sent to NHRC by the DG.

Placing the officer under suspension and prompt disciplinary action
against such officer where the conclusion of investigation amounts to
offence under IPC/ BNS.

Grant of compensation to the dependent of the victim of such case u/s
357-A of Cr.P.C/ 396 BNSS.

The police officer(s) concerned must surrender his/her weapons for
forensic and ballistic analysis.

Intimation about the incident must also be sent to the police officer’s
family and should the family need services of a lawyer/counseling,
same must be offered.

No out of turn promotions or instant gallantry rewards without
completion of investigation.

Family of the victim can make a complaint to the Sessions Judge
having jurisdiction in case of abuse, impartiality or lack of
independent investigation.

the commission of Enquiry Act, 1952.

“* Commissions are normally appointed to enquire into any definite matter
of public importance.

“* Commissions may consist of one or more members.
“> It is time bound for a fact finding.

“It has the powers of civil court for the purpose of summoning, receiving
evidence on affidavit, and production of a document.

“It has power to regulate its own procedures.

Commissions are generally to enquire into--

= Causes and course of disturbances

= To ascertain the facts regarding the entire series of incidents
= Measures taken to prevents and deal with such disturbances
= To assess the responsibility of various officers

= To justify the quantum of force used.

”

Commission may not be entrusted the job of identifying culprits.
Important Findings of the Commission of Enquiries

CRPF men assaulted the bus driver of Transport Corporation following
the collision between CRPF van and bus. The students from nearby
colleges’ pelted stones, one CRPF constable fired three rounds killing a
teacher who was standing inside the iron gate of the school.

”

CRPF constable tried to defend that he was dragged and the rifle was
also being snatched.

”

The commission did not believe the version for two reasons.

1. Two of the shots bored holes in the wall of the school building almost
in a vertical line which gave credence to the version of the students
that he took a proper aim towards the people inside the school
building and then fired.

2. If he was being dragged, as alleged, he would not have got sufficient
time to load his rifle and fire.
