Election Offences and Representation of Peoples Act,

1951 & Amendment Act,2013

(Corrupt Practices and Electoral Offences)

Chapter-I - Corrupt Practices

123. Corrupt practices.— The following shall be deemed to be corrupt
practices for the purposes of this Act:—

"Bribery", that is to say—

(A)

(B)

Any gift, offer or promise by a candidate or his agent or by any other
person with the consent of a candidate or his election agent of any
gratification, to any person whomsoever, with the object, directly or
indirectly of inducing—

a) a person to stand or not to stand as, or to withdraw or not to

withdraw from being a candidate at an election, or;

b) An elector to vote or refrain from voting at an election, or as a
reward to—

(ij) A person for having so stood or not stood, or for having
withdrawn or not having withdrawn his candidature; or

(ii) An elector for having voted or refrained from voting;

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
(ii) An elector for having voted or refrained from voting;

The receipt of, or agreement to receive, any gratification, whether as a
motive or a reward—

a) by a person for standing or not standing as, or for withdrawing or
not withdrawing] from being, a candidate; or

b) by any person whomsoever for himself or any other person for voting
or refraining from voting, or inducing or attempting to induce any
elector to vote or refrain from voting, or any candidate to withdraw
or not to withdraw his candidature.

“ Undue influence”, that is to say, any direct or indirect interference or
attempt to interfere on the part of the candidate or his agent, or of any other
person with the consent of the candidate or his election agent, with the free
exercise of any electoral right.

Provided that—

(a) Without prejudice to the generality of the provisions of this clause
any such person as is referred to therein who—

candidate or elector;

2. Model Code of Conduct

A. General Conduct:

1) No party or candidate shall include in any activity which may aggravate
existing differences or create mutual hatred or cause tension between
different castes and communities, religious or linguistic.

99 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2)

3)

4)

5)

6)

7)

Criticism of other political parties, when made, shall be confined to their
policies and programme, past record and work. Parties and Candidates
shall refrain from criticism of all aspects of private life, not connected
with the public activities of the leaders or workers of other parties.
Criticism of other parties or their workers based on unverified allegations
or distortion shall be avoided.

There shall be no appeal to caste or communal feelings for securing
votes. Mosques, Churches, Temples or other places of worship shall not
be used as forum for election propaganda.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
All parties and candidates shall avoid scrupulously all activities which
are “corrupt practices” and offences under the election law, such as
bribing of voters, intimidation of voters, impersonation of voters,
canvassing within 100 meters of polling stations, holding public meetings
during the period of 48 hours ending with the hour fixed for the close of
the poll, and the transport and conveyance of voters to and from polling
station.

The right of every individual for peaceful and undisturbed home-life shall
be respected, however much the political parties or candidates may
resent his political opinions or activities. Organizing demonstrations or
picketing before the houses of individuals by way of protesting against
their opinions or activities shall not be resorted to under any
circumstances.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
No political party or candidate shall permit its or his followers to make
use of any individual’s land, building, compound wall etc., without his
permission for erecting flag-staffs, suspending banners, pasting notices,
writing slogans etc.

Political parties and candidates shal
create obstructions in or break up

ensure that their supporters do not
meetings and processions organized

by other parties. Workers or sympathizers of one political party shall not

create disturbances at public mee

tings organized by another political

party by putting questions orally or in writing or by distributing leaflets

of their own party. Processions shal!

not be taken out by one party along

places at which meetings are held by another party. Posters issued by
one party shall not be removed by workers of another party.

B. Meetings

1)

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
places at which meetings are held by another party. Posters issued by
one party shall not be removed by workers of another party.

B. Meetings

1)

The party or candidate shall inform the local police authorities of the
venue and time any proposed meeting well in time so as to enable the
police to make necessary arrangements for controlling traffic and
maintaining peace and order.

100 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2)

3)

4)

A Party or candidate shall ascertain in advance if there is any restrictive
or prohibitory order in force in the place proposed for the meeting if such
orders exist, they shall be followed strictly. If any exemption is required
from such orders, it shall be applied for and obtained well in time.

If permission or license is to be obtained for the use of loudspeakers or
any other facility in connection with any proposed meeting, the party or
candidate shall apply to the authority concerned well in advance and
obtain such permission or license.

Organizers of a meeting shall invariably seek the assistance of the police
on duty for dealing with persons disturbing a meeting or otherwise
attempting to create disorder. Organizers themselves shall not take
action against such persons.

C. Procession

1)

2)

3)

4)

5)

6)

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
C. Procession

1)

2)

3)

4)

5)

6)

A Party or candidate organizing a procession shall decide before hand the
time and place of the starting of the procession, the route to be followed
and the time and place at which the procession will terminate. There
shall ordinary be on deviation from the programme.

The organizers shall give advance intimation to the local police
authorities of the programme so as to enable the letter to make necessary
arrangement.

The organizers shall ascertain if any restrictive orders are in force in the
localities through which the procession has to pass, and shall comply
with the restrictions unless exempted specially by the competent
authority. Any traffic regulations or restrictions shall also be carefully
adhered to.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The organizers shall take steps in advance to arrange for passage of the
procession so that there is no block or hindrance to traffic. If the
procession is very long, it shall be organized in segments of suitable
lengths, so that at convenient intervals, especially at points where the
procession has to pass road junctions, the passage of held up traffic
could be allowed by stages thus avoiding heavy traffic congestion.

Processions shall be so regulated as to keep as much to the right of the
road as possible and the direction and advice of the police on duty shall
be strictly complied with.

If two or more political parties or candidates propose to take processions
over the same route or parts thereof at about the same time, the
organizers shall establish contact well in advance and decide upon the
measures to be taken to see that the processions do not clash or cause
hindrance to traffic. The assistance of the local police shall be availed of

101 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
or arriving at a satisfactory arrangement. For this purpose the parties
shall contact the police at the earliest opportunity.

7) The political parties or candidates shall exercise control to the maximum
extent possible in the matter of precisionists carrying articles which may
be put to misuse by undesirable elements especially in moments of
excitement.

8) The carrying of effigies purporting to represent member of other political
parties or their leaders, burning such effigies in public and such other
corms demonstration shall not be countenanced by any political party or
candidate.

D. Polling Day
All Political parties and candidates shall —

1) Co-operate with the officers on election duty to ensure peaceful and
orderly polling and complete freedom to the voters to exercise their
franchise without being subjected to any annoyance or obstruction.

2) Supply to their authorized workers suitable badges or identity cards.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2) Supply to their authorized workers suitable badges or identity cards.

3) agree that the identity slip supplied by them to voters hall be on plain
(white) paper and shall not contain any symbol, name of the candidate or
the name of the party;

4) Refrain from serving or distributing liquor on polling day and during the
forty eight hours preceding it

5) Not allow unnecessary crowd to be collected near the camps set up by
the political parties and candidates near the polling booths so as to avoid
Confrontation and tension among workers and sympathizers of the
parties and the candidate.

6) Ensure that the candidate’s camps shall be simple .The shall not display
any posters, flags, symbols or any other propaganda material. No eatable
shall be served or crowd allowed at the camps and

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
7) Co-operate with the authorities in complying with the restrictions to be
imposed on the plying of vehicles on the polling day and obtain permits
for them which should be displayed prominently on those vehicles.

E. Polling Booth

Excepting the voters, no one without a valid pass from the Election
Commission shall enter the polling booths.

102 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
F. Observers

i)

(ii)

(iii)

(iv)

(v)

(vi)

[he Election Commission is appointing Observers. If the candidates or their
agents have any specific complaint or problem regarding the conduct of
elections they may bring the same to the notice of the Observer.

E. Party in Power

[he party in power whether at the Centre or in the State or States
concerned, shall ensure that no cause is given for any complaint that it has
used its official position for the purposes of its election campaign and in
particular —

a) The Ministers shall not combine their official visit with electioneering
work and shall not also make use of official machinery or personnel
during the electioneering work.

b) Government transport including official air-crafts, vehicles,
machinery and personnel shall not be used for furtherance of the
interest of the party in power;

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Public places such as maidans etc., for holding election meetings, and
use of helipads for air-flights in connection with elections shall not be
monopolized by itself. Other parties and candidates shall be allowed the
use of such places and facilities on the same terms and conditions on
which they are used by the party in power;

Rest houses, dark bungalows or other Government accommodation shall
not be monopolized by the party in power or its candidates and such
accommodation shall be allowed to be used by other parties and
candidates in a fair manner but no party or candidate shall use or be
allowed to use such accommodation (including premises appertaining

thereto) as a campaign office or for holding any public meeting for the
purposes of election propaganda;

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
thereto) as a campaign office or for holding any public meeting for the
purposes of election propaganda;

Issue of advertisement at the cost of public exchequer in the newspapers
and other media and the misuse of official mass media during the
election period for partisan coverage of political news and publicity
regarding achievements with a view to furthering the prospects of the
party in power shall be scrupulously avoided.

Ministers and other authorities shall not sanction grants/payments out
of discretionary funds from the time elections are announced by the
Commission; and

From the time elections are announced by Commission, Ministers and
other authorities shall not;

a) Announce any financial grants in any form or promises thereof; or

103 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
b) (Except civil servants) lay foundation stones etc. of projects or
schemes of any kind; or

c) Make any promise of construction of roads, provision of drinking
water facilities etc.; or

d) Make any ad-hoc appointments in Government, Public Undertakings
etc. which may have the effect of influencing the voters in favor of the
party in power.

Note: The Commission shall announce the date of any election which
shall be a date ordinarily not more than three weeks prior to the date
on which the notification is likely to be issued in respect of such
elections.

(vii)Ministers of Central or State Government shall not enter any polling
station or place of counting except in their capacity as a candidate or
voter or authorize agent.

3. Code of Conduct for Police

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
As per section 28-A of Representation Of Peoples Act 1951.Any police
officer designated for the time being by the state government for the conduct
of any election, shall be deemed to be on deputation to the Election
Commission for the period commencing on and from the date of the
notification calling for such election and ending with the date of declaration
of the results of such election. And accordingly, such officers shall, during
the period, be subject to the control, superintendence and discipline of the
Election Commission of India. Government servant should not canvass for
any party or, carry any symbol on his vehicle or, influence voters by any
action. The police force should maintain a strictly impartial attitude and
their action also should convey this impartiality. While granting or with
holding permission for an election meeting at a public place, no
discrimination should be made between political parties All complaints

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
holding permission for an election meeting at a public place, no
discrimination should be made between political parties All complaints
related to election should be expeditiously and impartially handled.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
kek

page_content:
1. BANDOBUST PLAN

1. CATEGORIZATION OF POLLING BOOTHS AND ROUTES

Election Commission's Letter No.464/INST/2008-EPS Dated: 24th

October, 2008 deals with identification of critical polling stations and
measures to be taken to ensure free and fair elections

Criteria to be followed by the DEO/ RO are as under:-

A.

An analysis of the polling station wise number of voters with EPIC and
without EPIC (non EPIC voters) shall be made. The polling stations shall
be sorted in descending order in terms of number of non-EPIC voters .

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The Commission had recently directed a survey of missing voters under
two categories i.e. missing voters with family links and missing voters
without family links. Out of these two categories the existence of missing
voters without family links offers a scope for misuse and malpractice.
The number of voters coming under the second category shall be
analyzed polling station wise and the Polling Station with large number
of such voters shall be marked. For this purpose the total number of
such missing voters without family linkage shall be divided by the total
number of polling stations to work out the assembly constituency
average. voters.

During the election the DEOs/ROs may be asked to do a vulnerability
mapping of hamlets/villages/electoral segments vulnerable for threat
and intimidation. Polling stations identified as having vulnerable pockets
shall be listed.

. The polling station wise election results available in Form 20 with

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
. The polling station wise election results available in Form 20 with

reference to the past general election shall be analyzed. All such Polling
Station where percentage of poll recorded is more than 75% and where
more than 75% of votes have been recorded in favor of one candidate
shall be identified as critical polling station.

The polling stations that went for re-poll during the previous election due
to reported electoral malpractices; the polling stations that witnessed any
sort of electoral violence shall also be identified.

The DEOs and ROs shall factor all the above inputs while finally
identifying the critical polling stations for additional measures. The EC
Observers shall be consulted while finalizing the list of critical polling
stations .

The response protocol to be followed with reference to the vulnerable
villages/ hamlets/electoral segments has been detailed vide

105 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Commission's letter 464/ INST/2007-PLN-I Dated 12th October, 2007.
These instructions shall be implemented without fail.

H. With reference to polling stations identified as critical polling stations on
account of other indicators listed above one or all of the following
measures shall be put in place.

a) The presence of CPF to safe guard the polling station.

b) Digital camera or video camera shall be positioned in the polling
station. The procedure for deploying such cameras has been given
vide letter No.447/2007/PLN-IV, dated 17.01.2007.

c) The Presiding Officer shall be specially briefed to ensure that the
EPIC/ approved identification document, if any are properly verified

2. PREPARATION OF ELECTION BANDOBUST PLAN AND DRAWING OF FORCE

Election Commission's Letter No.464/INST/2007-PLN-I Dated: 12th

October, 2007 deals with measures to ensure free and fair elections-
Prevention of intimidation to the voters of vulnerable sections of electorate-
Mapping of vulnerability criteria

1.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf

page_content:
1.

An exercise to identify the villages/ hamlets/habitats and segments of
electorate vulnerable to any threat, intimidation or interference with the
free exercise of electoral right shall be taken up polling station wise. The
sector officers for their respective polling stations shall do this exercise
by visiting the catchment area of the polling stations. The local (SHO)
and local civil authorities such as BDO / Tehsildar shall also be
consulted and their inputs taken into account before finalizing the list.
They should identify the source of such threat/ intimidation and identify
the names of persons who are likely to spearhead such offence of undue
influence. While doing this exercise they shall take into account the past
incidents, and current apprehensions.

[hey shall identify some point of contact within the habitat/ community
vulnerable for such undue influence so that information related to such
developments can be tracked constantly.

[The Returning Officer of the Assembly Constituency should compile all
such information and finalize the vulnerability mapping for the entire
constituency, polling station wise in a format prescribed.

[he DEO and SP shall initiate all preventive measures to ensure that
such intimidation/ obstruction do not really happen on the poll day.
They shall initiate confidence-building measures to bolster the voters'
confidence about the arrangements for free and fair poll. They shal
undertake tours to such locations and meet the communities and explain
the arrangements made for the free and fair poll.

The DEO/ RO shall interact with the candidates and representatives o
political parties to gather regular feedback. The District Intelligence shal
give regular feedback on the subject to the DEO through SP.

Upon the arrival of the Observers the DEO/ RO shall hand over the
etails of the polling station wise vulnerability mapping for the relevant
Assembly Constituency. The Observer will also visit such locations an:
interact with the voters and constantly monitor the developments.

The DEO and Superintendent of Police of the District should hold a joint
review on the subject and finalize a focused action plan to deal with the
potential threats and intimidation points identified. The action plan may
include, binding over the identified trouble mongers under appropriate
sections of the law, preventive detention if required, forcing their

good behavior, placement of police pickets, regular confidence building
visits etc. It has to be ensured that all such measures are undertaken in
absolutely non-partisan manner without fear or favor towards any
particular party.

The Zonal/and sector arrangements to monitor the events on the poll day
shall take such pre-identified vulnerable locations into account for
effective tracking. If the normal sector route map does not cover the
vulnerable locations special arrangements shall be made for this
purpose. The Sector officers shall make regular visits to those villages
and hamlets in advance and collect information and keep the senior

locations in the vicinity, to be pressed into service for action on the day of

security plan.

If any complaint is received or information gathered from any sources
about obstruction/threat to any voter/voters the same shall be enquired
into by the local administration without any delay.

The police patrolling parties should keep track of the vulnerable locations
and keep the control room informed. Wherever necessary police pickets
shall be established to ensure free access to all voters to cast their votes
without fear.

After the completion of induction training, troops should be deployed at
the specified forward location 7 day prior to the polling date, so that area
domination, flag marches and reconnaissance of polling booths is conducted
as a pre-poll deployment and confidence building measure. This will also
facilitate timely and smooth deployment of troops at polling booths before
the day of poll.

booths and others less critical can be covered by area security approach
through patrolling and area domination. Deployment of forces has to be done
on the basis of area vulnerability, security equipment available with the force
and prior experience in LWE area.

videographer and 3 to 4 armed police personnel.

= They are to be provided with a dedicated vehicle, mobile phone, a video
camera and necessary Panchnama documents required for seizure of
cash or goods.

(b) QRTs (Quick Response/Reaction Teams):

= In the aftermath of the recent terror attacks, every Police Force has
constituted an elite “Quick Response/Reaction Team” (QRT)
comprising commandos, specially and rigorously trained i.e. at par
with the National Security Guards (NSG) and armed with sophisticated
weapons, specially designed vehicles and ultra-modern communication

4. Implementation of Model code of conduct:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf

page_content:
4. Implementation of Model code of conduct:

"The Model Code of Conduct is crucial to ensure a level playing field
among various contenders in the poll fray." Election Commission of India's
Model Code of Conductis a set of guidelines issued by the Election
Commission of India for conduct of political parties and candidates
during elections mainly with respect to speeches, polling day, polling booths,
election manifestos, processions and general conduct. These set of norms
has been evolved with the consensus of political parties who have consented
to abide by the principles embodied in the said code in its letter and spirit.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The Model Code of Conduct comes into force immediately on announcement
of the election schedule by the commission for ensuring free and fair
elections. Much of it is designed to avert communal clashes and corrupt
practices. For example, politicians should not make hate speeches, putting
one community against another or make promises about new projects that
may sway a voter.

Main points:
The main points of the code are:

(i) Government bodies are not to participate in any recruitment process
during the electoral process.

(ii) The contesting candidates and their campaigners must respect the home
life of their rivals and should not disturb them by holding road shows or
demonstrations in front of their houses.

117 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
iii) The election campaign rallies and road shows must not hinder the road
traffic.

iv) Candidates are asked to refrain from distributing liquor to voters. It is a
widely known fact in India that during election campaigning, liquor may
be distributed to the voters.

(v) The election code in force hinders the government or running party
leaders from launching new welfare programmes like construction of
roads, provision of drinking water facilities etc. or any ribbon-cutting
ceremonies.

vi) The code instructs that public spaces like meeting grounds, helipads,
government guest houses and bungalows should be equally shared
among the contesting candidates.

These public spaces should not be monopolized by a few candidates.
5. Bound Overs/ Depositing of Arms:

Guard against the removal of ballot papers boxes and prevent any
damages by anti social elements.

Do not accept any favor and be impartial.
Do not be friendly or hostile with candidate or party worker.

Ensure that the voters are able to practice their right without any
obstacle.

The political party/ candidate booths are out of 200 m radius of PS and

The party/ candidate booth representatives do not pressurize or
influence voters to vote for or against any particular party.

No election campaigning takes place in a 100m radius of polling station.

No person provided with security, carries his security with in 100m
radius of polling station.

page_content:
MODULE - V
DISASTER MANAGEMENT

1. DEFINITION OF DISASTER MANAGEMENT

The United Nations defines a disaster as a serious disruption of the
functioning of a community or a society. Disasters involve widespread
human, material, economic or environmental impacts, which exceed the
ability of the affected community or society to cope using its own resources.

A disaster is a sudden, calamitous event that seriously disrupts the
functioning of a community or society and causes human, material, and
economic or environmental losses that exceed the community’s or society’s
ability to cope using its own resources. Though often caused by nature,
disasters can have human origins.

(VULNERABILITY+ HAZARD) / CAPACITY = DISASTER
A disaster occurs when a hazard impacts on vulnerable people.

The combination of hazards, vulnerability and inability to reduce the
potential negative consequences of risk results in disaster.

TYPES OF DISASTERS
1. NATURAL DISASTERS

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
TYPES OF DISASTERS
1. NATURAL DISASTERS

Natural disasters are naturally occurring physical phenomena caused
either by rapid or slow onset events which can be geophysical (earthquakes,
landslides, tsunamis and volcanic activity), hydrological (avalanches and flo
ods), climate related (extreme temperatures, drought and _ wildfires),
metrological (cyclones and storms/ wave surges) or biological (disease
epidemics and insect/ animal plagues). Technological or man-made hazards
(conflicts, famine, displaced populations, industrial accidents and transport
accidents) are events that are caused by humans and occur in (or) close to
human settlements. This can include environmental degradation, pollution
and accidents.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
There are a range of challenges, such as climate change, unplanned-
urbanization, under-development/poverty as well as the threat of
pandemics, that will shape humanitarian assistance in the future. These
aggravating factors will result in increased frequency, complexity and
severity of disasters.

Disaster Management can be defined as the organization and management
of resources and responsibilities for dealing with all humanitarian aspects of

132 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
emergencies, in particular, preparedness and response in order to lessen the
impact of disasters.

Disaster Prevention: These are activities designed to provide permanent
protection from disasters. Not all disasters, particularly natural disasters,
can be prevented, but the risk of loss of life and injury can be mitigated with
good evacuation plans, environmental planning and design standards.

Disaster Preparedness: Preparedness is the main way to reducing the
impact of disasters. Community-based preparedness and management
should be high.

Disaster Relief: This is a coordinated multi-agency response to reduce the
impact of a disaster and its long-term results. Relief activities include
rescue, relocation, providing food and water, preventing disease and
disability, repairing vital services such as telecommunications and transport,
providing temporary shelter and emergency health care.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Disaster Recovery: Once emergency needs have been met and the initial
crisis is over, the people affected and the communities that support them are
still vulnerable. Recovery activities include rebuilding infrastructure, health
care and rehabilitation. These should blend with development activities,
such as building human resources for health and developing policies and
practices to avoid similar situations in future.

Disaster management is linked with sustainable development,
particularly in relation to vulnerable people such as those with disabilities,
elderly people, children and other marginalized groups.

Types of Disasters:
Floods:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Types of Disasters:
Floods:

India is highly vulnerable to floods. Floods are a recurrent phenomenon,
which cause huge loss of life and damage to livelihood systems, property,
infrastructure and public utilities. It is a cause for concern that flood related
damages show an increasing trend. This can be attributed to many
reasons including a steep increase in population, rapid urbanization,
growing developmental and economic activities in flood plains coupled with
global warming.

Floods have occurred in areas, which were earlier not considered flood
prone. Eighty per-cent of the precipitation in India takes place in the
monsoon months from June to September. The rivers bring heavy sediment
load from catchments. These, coupled with inadequate carrying capacity of
rivers are responsible for causing floods, drainage congestion and erosion of
river-banks. It is a fact that some of the rivers causing damage in India

133 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
originate in neighboring countries; adding another complex dimension to the
problem.

Cyclones:

Cyclones are caused by atmospheric disturbances around a low-
pressure area distinguished by swift and often destructive air circulation.
Cyclones are usually accompanied by violent storms and bad weather. The
air circulates inward in an anticlockwise direction in the Northern
hemisphere and clockwise in the Southern hemisphere. Cyclones are
classified as: (i) extra tropical cyclones (also called temperate cyclones); and

ii) tropical cyclones. The word Cyclone is derived from the Greek word
Cyclones meaning the coils of a snake. It was coined by Henry Paddington
because the tropical storms in the Bay of Bengal and the Arabian Sea appear
ike coiled serpents of the sea.
Cyclone Category Wind Speed in Km/h Damage Capacity

o1 120-150 Minimal

02 150-180 Moderate

03 180-210 Extensive

04 210-250 Extreme

05 250 and above Catastrophic
Earthquakes

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
o1 120-150 Minimal

02 150-180 Moderate

03 180-210 Extensive

04 210-250 Extreme

05 250 and above Catastrophic
Earthquakes

An earthquake is a phenomenon that occurs without warning and
involves violent shaking of the ground and everything over it. It results from
the release of accumulated stress of the moving lithospheric or crustal
plates. The earth's crust is divided into seven major plates, that are about 50
miles thick, which move slowly and continuously over the earth's interior
and several minor plates. Earthquakes are tectonic in origin; that is the
moving plates are responsible for the occurrence of violent shakes. The
occurrence of an earthquake in a populated area may cause numerous
casualties and injuries as well as extensive damage to property.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Biological disasters are of organic origin or conveyed by biological vectors,
including exposure to pathogenic micro-organisms, toxins and bioactive
substances that may cause loss of life, injury, illness or other health
impacts, property damage, loss of livelihoods and services, social and
economic disruption, or environmental damage. Examples of biological
disasters include outbreaks of epidemic diseases, plant or animal contagion,
insect or other animal plagues and infestation. Biological disasters may be in
the form of:-

134 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Epidemic affecting a disproportionately large number of individuals within a
population, community, or region at the same time, examples being Cholera,
Plague, Japanese Encephalitis (JE)/Acute Encephalitis Syndrome (AES)

Pandemic is an epidemic that spreads across a large region, that is, a
continent, or even worldwide of existing, emerging or reemerging diseases
and pestilences, example being Influenza H1N1 (Swine Flu), Covid-19 etc.

ROLE OF POLICE:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
ROLE OF POLICE:

During the above circumstances, if information is received by Police, it
should be immediately conveyed to the relevant departments and superior
Officers. If possible, make efforts to take necessary steps, along with the staff
of other departments. Police must take part in the activities, like providing
vehicles, to send patients to the hospitals and working jointly with the
medical staff, in giving treatment. Necessary patrolling must be arranged, to
prevent property offences in the affected area and it should be continued,
until normal conditions are restored. Rapport must be maintained with the
residents of the affected area and information should be given to superior
Officers and other departmental Officers.

2. MAN MADE DISASTERS
Fire Accidents:-
About Fire:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. MAN MADE DISASTERS
Fire Accidents:-
About Fire:

Fire is a very good servant, but, a very bad master, as long as fire is
under our control, it serves a lot of useful purposes for us, but, once it goes
out of our control, it can create a lot of destruction.

However, despite the presence of fire safety measures, the occurrence of
accidents is often times inevitable.

Thus, the occurrence of fire related accidents is oftentimes inevitable —
in spite of all the safety precautions.

The most common causes of fire are:
1. Electrical;
2. Pantry area;
3. Smoking;

Conditions needed for a fire: For a fire to take shape and continue, the
following three conditions should be met:

1. A combustible material
2. A specific temperature at which the above material would burn

3. Some fuel (mostly oxygen) to aid the burning.

135 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
vy When petrol/ gasoline is burning, the petrol/ gasoline is the
combustible material, and, the atmosphere provides the fuel (oxygen).

v¥ The presence of the above 3 elements together is called the “fire
triangle”.

vY Each of these 3 elements have to be present to start a fire, and for the
fire to continue.

¥ Sometime, one element may aid in producing the other element.

Y It’s important to know about the fires because fire extinguishers are
classified and marked based on the fire on which they would be
effective.

Y So, in case of a fire, you first want to know the class of fire, so that you
can use the right extinguishers.

v If you use the wrong extinguisher(s), the result could be fatal also in
some cases.

v In best case situation, there would be no injury etc, but, you could still
loose precious time — in performing an activity which is useless.

Duties of Police: (Order No. 308 of APPM Part-I Vol-I):

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Duties of Police: (Order No. 308 of APPM Part-I Vol-I):

A Police Officer must inform to the nearest fire station, about the fire
accident, either by telephone or through a special messenger if necessary,
with details of accident and the seriousness of the fire accident. Later, he
must inform to the available staff in Police station and proceed to the spot,
along with them, to help the victims. Even, if Police Officer is not on duty, he
must rush up to the place of fire accident, after knowing about it and take
up relief activities, to the possible extent.

According to section 81 IPC/ Sec 19 BNS:

A Police Officer engaged in extinguishing the fire, will have some special
powers. This section protects, in the case of damage to any article, in the

process of extinguishing fire. This power is available to the Officers, above
the rank of PCs.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
process of extinguishing fire. This power is available to the Officers, above
the rank of PCs.

a. The SHO is empowered to remove any person, who causes obstruction, in
the process of extinguishing the fire. He is also empowered, to close any
way, which leads to aggravation of any problem. He will prevent loss of
life and property.

b. The SHO and the staff working under his instructions, must keep in
mind, that they cause minimum damage to the property, in the process
of entry into the place, removing the obstacles and extinguishing the fire.

c. The SHO is empowered to close the taps and pipelines, to secure more
quantity of water, to put off the fire.

136 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
d. In the place where fire extinguishers are not available, all possible
assistance must be given to the fire service staff, when they are available,
for extinguishing the fire.

Extinguishing the fire during fire accidents is the responsibility of fire
service. Maintenance of law and order must be looked after by Police, during
fire accidents. After reaching the place of fire accident, Police should not
involve in the process of putting off fire on their own accord. Necessary
assistance may be given on the request of the senior Fire Officer. Police must
clear the way to avoid any obstructions to the fire brigade, in discharging of
their duties.

Road Accidents: (Order No. 331(1) of APPM Part-I Vol-I):

Every year, as many as 8,000 people are losing their lives in road
accidents, in the State and more than 20,000 are seriously injured.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Every year, as many as 8,000 people are losing their lives in road
accidents, in the State and more than 20,000 are seriously injured.

Reasons for Road Accidents: Over speed, negligence, rash driving, in
drunken condition, driving in sleepy mood, bad condition of the vehicle, bad
condition of the road.

Role of Police:

1. Must proceed to the place of accident immediately, after receiving the
information.

2. The injured must be shifted to hospital for treatment. If the vehicles are
not available, with Police, shifting must be done, either through public
vehicles or with the assistance of others.

3. Place of accident must be photographed (must carry camera while
starting from the P.S).

Place of incident must be cordoned to preserve the physical evidences.

Alternate way must be indicated, to avoid interruption, to the free flow
of traffic and traffic must be diverted.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Alternate way must be indicated, to avoid interruption, to the free flow
of traffic and traffic must be diverted.

6. If it is a hit and run case, the information should be given to the
surrounding Police stations, through VHF set. These cases normally
occur on highways. Some persons will drive the vehicles at a high
speed, hit the pedestrians and escape, without informing the Police.
Such people, can be arrested by passing on the information through
wireless set.

7. The fleeing away vehicle must be chased by public or private vehicle
and taken into custody. Information must be given to all the patrolling
vehicles, about the fleeing vehicle.

8. Police Officer must note down all the available evidences and names of
witnesses. If it is possible, witnesses must be enquired there itself.

137 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
10.

11.

The place of accident must be properly guarded, immediately after the
incident, to prevent theft of valuables, from the deceased.

Physical evidences, like skid marks, paint marks, blood stains and
crushed vehicles, must be preserved. They must be removed from the
road, after the completion of Panchanama to restore the traffic.

If the address of the deceased persons are not known, proper
investigation must be conducted to identify the dead bodies.

Train Accidents (Order No. 331(2) of APPM Part-I Vol-I) :

Train accidents may take place on the rails and near the areas of

Railway station surroundings. So, they will come under the jurisdiction of

Railway Police.

Basing on the seriousness of the accidents, the local Police must take

immediate steps. The Railway Police will take care about the passengers who

are not injured in the rail accident.

Duties of local Police during train collusions:

1.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
are not injured in the rail accident.

Duties of local Police during train collusions:

1.

Information about the train accident must be given to the SP, SRP,
district Collector, concerned Mandal Revenue Officer and district head
quarter’s hospital authorities.

First aid must be given to the injured and they should be sent to the
nearest hospital for treatment. People who are injured in the accidents
must be brought out carefully. Otherwise, there is scope of causalities.

Senior Police Officer must reach the place of accident, estimate the
seriousness of the situation and take steps for making required
arrangements, with the assistance of the other concerned Officers. EX:
Provision of ambulance, food, water etc.

The dead bodies must be properly preserved and handed over to the
relatives, after the completion of investigation.

Necessary assistance must be given to the railway and revenue
authorities.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Necessary assistance must be given to the railway and revenue
authorities.

Relief activities must be taken up with the assistance of surrounding
village Officers.

Security must be provided to the properties of railway department and
passengers. Guard must be provided during night time.

Rehabilitation activities by voluntary organizations must _ be
coordinated.

Place of incident must be preserved, until the investigation by railway
authorities is started.

138 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
10. The staff present at the place of accident, must have patience and work
sincerely, to save the lives of the people, involved in the accidents.

11. Any act which degrades the prestige of Police should be avoided.
Air Craft crashes:

If aircraft accident occurred in the fields or villages, the Village Revenue
Officer must inform the nearest Magistrate, Police station or out-post, in
writing, through a report. The Village Revenue Officer must give information
to the nearest Government Medical Officer, if there are any casualties.

The report sent by the Village Revenue Officer (VRO), must contain the
following information:

1. Country of Origin of the aircraft and its registration particulars.

. Name and address of the owner, operator and the hirer.

. Date and time of the accident.

. Starting point of the aircraft, and destination.

. Land marks, by which the place of accident can be easily identified.

. Number of passengers.

NO wu f WN

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
. Land marks, by which the place of accident can be easily identified.

. Number of passengers.

NO wu f WN

. Number of deaths and seriously injured persons, with their names and
other particulars.

8. Details of the accident and damages caused (to the extent of the
knowledge of V.R.O).

If the staff of air craft died are incapacitated, to send a report about the
accident. Police Officer who gets the first information on the basis of V.R.O
report or the V.R.O himself must send the message to the Director General
of Civil Aviation, through the quickest means.

Copy of the message should be sent to Inspector of Police of that area.
The message must contain all the above mentioned particulars. Same
information must be given to the District Magistrate and the Officer in
Charge of the nearest Police station. The message should be sent within 24
hours of the accident.

Duties of Police Officer:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Duties of Police Officer:

The information about the accident must be given to the nearest airport
authorities or civil aviation authorities.

The First Information Report must be sent to the district Magistrate and
SP, as early as possible. If any of the crew members, involved in the accident
desire to speak with their concerned Officers, all the available
communication channels must be provided to them. Security should be

139 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
provided to the wreckage of the aircraft, till the arrival of air port authorities.
Steps should be taken, to save the lives of persons in danger and valuable
equipment. If it is necessary to move the aircraft, it should be reduced to
writing and sent to the concerned officials.

In the places where it is not possible for other organizations to provide
security, to the aircraft request must be made to the local Police, by Army or
Navy and required security should be provided.

The Village Revenue Officer is also responsible to preserve the damaged
parts of the air craft.

For preservation of evidence, Police must take the following steps:
1. Preservation of wreckage of the aircraft.
2. Collection of documents required for investigation.

3. Collection of land marks, photos of the wreckage and drawing rough
sketch of the scene of accident.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. Collection of land marks, photos of the wreckage and drawing rough
sketch of the scene of accident.

4. Collection of the wreckage placed at a distance from the place of accident
and their preservation. Drawing rough sketch by showing the disposition
of wreckage, with the names and addresses of the persons, who identified
the wreckage.

5. Collection of names and addresses of eye witnesses, recording of the
extra ordinary circumstances of the accident, should be done, if
necessary. The information collected and the assistance of other
Government departments must be placed at the disposal of civil aviation
authorities, on their arrival to the place of accident.

6. The Magistrate or Police Officer will take necessary steps in case of the
persons who died in the accident.

7. If the pilot is dead, the body must be handed over to the aviation
authorities, after postmortem and necessary assistance must be given.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
7. If the pilot is dead, the body must be handed over to the aviation
authorities, after postmortem and necessary assistance must be given.

8. Police must extend all cooperation and assistance to the civil aviation
investigating authorities.

The fire tenders may go up to a distance of 30 KMs from head quarters.
During emergencies, they may go beyond 30 KM, with the permission of the
Officers in charge of the fire station. If there is any loss of human life or
property up to Rs. 50,000 or more, the information should be given to SDPO
and SP. If two or more Police stations are located near the place of accident,
the senior Police Officer present on the spot, must alert the staff of his Police
station and Armed Police force. They must proceed to the spot and control
the crowd and extinguish the fire. Police should to keep the crowd away from
the place. Efforts should be made, to get the required quantity of water from

140 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
other sources. Sentries must be posted, to avoid theft of property and to
protect the people involved in the fire accident. Some Police force must be
kept a side, to be ready for any duty with an in charge Officer.

Industrial Gas Leakages

Chemical disasters may be traumatic in their impact.

The elements which are at highest risks due to chemical disaster
primarily include the industrial plant, its employees & workers, the
residents of nearby settlements, adjacent buildings, occupants and
surrounding community.

Status of Chemical Disaster Risk in India -India has witnessed the
world’s worst chemical (industrial) disaster “Bhopal Gas Tragedy” in the
year 1984.

Only in last decade, 130 significant chemical accidents were reported in
India, which resulted in 259 deaths and 563 injured.

There are about 1861 Major Accident Hazard (MAH) units, spread across
301 districts and 25 states & 3 Union Territories, in all zones of the
country.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Besides, there are thousands of registered and hazardous factories
(below MAH criteria) and un-organized sectors dealing with numerous
range of hazardous material posing serious and complex levels of
disaster risks.

Legal frame work to address chemical risk:

The following regulations covering the safety in transportation, liability,
insurance and compensations have been enacted.

onan PF WN

Explosives Act 1884.

Factories Act 1948.

Environment Protection Act 1986.
Public Liability Insurance Act 1991.
Petroleum Act 1934.

Insecticides Act 1968.

Motor Vehicles Act 1988.

Disaster Management Act 2005.

e¢ Government of India has further reinforced the legal framework on
chemical safety and management of chemical accidents by enacting
new rules such as MSIHC Rules, EPPR Rules, SMPV Rules, CMV

141 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Rules, Gas Cylinder Rules, Hazardous Waste Rules, and Dock Workers
Rules and by way of amendments to them.

e CIFS (Chief Inspectorate of Factories) to strengthen chemical safety in
India.

Bomb Explosions - Caution

Only bomb disposal personnel should handle any suspected devices that
are located. Take no further action until the devices have been identified
and rendered safe.

Beware of secondary devices! The scene may contain secondary explosive
devices designed specifically to kill or maim public safety responders.

Do not touch any suspicious items. If a suspected secondary device is
located, immediately evacuate the area and contact bomb disposal
personnel.

Exercise Scene Safety Principle.

Conduct a Preliminary Evaluation of the Scene.

Procedure: Upon arrival at the scene, first responders should:

1.

Establish a command post/ implement an incident command system
(i.e. a point of contact and line of communication and authority for
other public safety personnel).

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Request emergency services from bomb technicians, fire-fighters, EMS
personnel, and law enforcement officers.

Identify scene hazards, such as structural collapse, blood borne
pathogens, hazardous chemicals, and secondary explosive devices.

Identify witnesses, victims and evidence.

Preserve potentially transient physical evidence (e.g. evidence present
on victims, evidence that may be compromised by weather conditions).

Administer Lifesaving Efforts.

1.

First responders’ primary responsibility is to rescue living victims and
provide treatment for life — threatening injuries.

Evacuate ambulatory victims.

Leave fatalities and their surroundings undisturbed. Removal of
fatalities will await authorization.

Establish Security and Control Principle.

1.

Set up a security perimeter.

142 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. Restrict access into and out of the scene through the security perimeter
(e.g. control media, bystanders, and nonessential personnel).

3. Initiate documentation of the scene as soon as conditions permit (e.g.
taking notes, identifying witnesses, videotaping/ photographing by
standers).

4. Establish an entry and exit path for personnel.

Establish staging areas to ensure that emergency vehicles have access
into the area.

Attempt to locate the seat(s) of the explosion(s).

Record overall views of the scene (e.g. wide angle, aerial, 360-degree) to
especially relate items within and to the scene and surrounding area. (A
combination of still photography, videotaping, and other techniques is
most effective).

8. Consider muting the audio portion of any video recording unless there
is narration.

9. Attempt to determine the method of bomb delivery.

0. Document blast effects (e.g. structural damage, bent signs, thermal
Effects, fragmentation etc.)

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
0. Document blast effects (e.g. structural damage, bent signs, thermal
Effects, fragmentation etc.)

1. Ensuring that victims are examined for bomb component fragments.
Autopsies should include full-body x-rays.

2. Discuss post scene issues (e.g. forensic testing, insurance inquiries,
Interview results, criminal histories).

3. Communicate and document post scene responsibilities.

4. The investigator should ensure communication of known scene-related
health and safety issues at the time of release.
BUILDING COLLAPSE:

The Thane Building Collapse in 2013 is a typical incident which
brought to light the issue of illegal housing and unsafe building construction
practices.

The building was under illegal construction with no occupancy
certificates issued to its residents.

Preliminary probe indicated that the laying of foundation of another
building in the vicinity triggered the collapse.

143 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Reasons for Building Collapse:
Structural failures:

Size, shape, or the choice of material, is that the structure is not strong
and tough enough to support the load. Structural instability due to
geometry, design or choice of material.

Manufacturing errors:

Improper selection of materials, incorrect sizing, improper heat treating,
failing to adhere to the design, or shoddy workmanship.

Guide lines:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Improper selection of materials, incorrect sizing, improper heat treating,
failing to adhere to the design, or shoddy workmanship.

Guide lines:

The need to construct buildings with structural integrity has been well
acknowledged across the world. Structural integrity has been defined by Dr
Steve Roberts (University of Oxford) as the science and technology of the
margin between safety and disaster. Structural integrity is the ability of a
structure or a component to withstand a designed service load, resisting
structural failure due to fracture, deformation, or fatigue. Structural
integrity is a concept often used in engineering, to produce items (including
construction of buildings) that will not only function adequately for their
designed purposes, but also to function for a desired service life.

144 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. INCIDENT MANAGEMENT

Preparedness, Setting Up of Control room/ Alerts to Public:

e A control room should be set up first and foremost to collect and
disseminate information and to facilitate real-time, effective and
coordinated response to crises/ disaster situations.

e To accelerate information flow.

e To collect and collate information from multiple sources and sift them for
onward transmission.

e To provide for simultaneous vertical as well as horizontal flow of
information.

e To prevent information loss.
e To archive vital information.
Evacuation from low-lying areas/ from scene of disaster

Alarms must be heard by everyone at Crisis Centre. A disorganized
evacuation can result in confusion, injury, and property damage.

e Acclear chain of command and designation of the person authorized to
order an evacuation or shutdown. May designate an “evacuation warden”
to assist others in an evacuation and to account for personnel;

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e Specific evacuation procedures, including routes and exits.
Setting up of relief camps and distribution of essential commodities:

Relief Camp: The following steps shall be taken for setting up relief camps
in the affected areas,

1. Adequate numbers of buildings or open space shall be identified where
relief camps can be set up during emergency.

2. The use of premises of educational institutions for setting up relief camps
shall be discouraged.

3. One member of the Incident Command Team of the district trained in
running and management of relief camps will be deputed for
management of relief camps.

4. The requirements for operation of relief camps shall be worked out in
detail in advance.

5. Agencies to supply the necessary stores will be identified in the pre
disaster phase.

6. The temporary relief camps will have adequate provision of drinking
water and bathing, sanitation and essential health-care facilities.

145 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Adequate security arrangements shall be made by local police.

Adequate lighting arrangements shall be made in the Camp Area
including at water points, toilets and other common areas.

9. Wherever feasible, special task forces from amongst the disaster affected
families will be set up to explore the possibility of provision of food
through community kitchens, provision of education through the
restoration of schools and anganwadis.

10. Efficient governance systems like entitlement cards, identification cards,
bank accounts for cash transfers etc shall be developed.

e Sanitation services are crucial to prevent an outbreak of epidemics in
post disaster phase.

e Therefore a constant monitoring of any such possibilities will be
necessary. It should be ensured that disaster-affected households have
access to sufficient hygiene measures. Soap, detergents, sanitary
napkins and other sanitary items should be made available to ensure
personal hygiene, health, dignity and well-being.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e In the relief camps, toilets should be sited, designed, constructed and
maintained in such a way as to be comfortable, hygienic and safe to
use.

Basic Relief Kit: Dry/ cooked food, water, basic medicines (to be specified
by Ministry of Health), torches with spare batteries, hygiene items, plastic
sheets 10’x10’.

Other Relief Kits: Sleeping bag, dry food including, biscuits, high energy
bars, ready to cook meals, water, torch with spare batteries, candle and
match box, medicines, ORS packs first aid kit, facial masks, gloves,
mosquito repellents hand sanitizers, daily hygiene items (soap, toothpaste,
toothbrush), essential clothing, rope, umbrella/ rain coat, polythene sheet
10’x10’, Office kits;

Equipments for setting up basic office at the disaster site:

Traffic control and regulation:
All safety procedures must remain in place until the incident is

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
terminated (all personnel, hazards, emergency and related vehicles and
equipment are removed from the roadway) or until a designated agency
arrives on scene, provides a complete traffic control set-up and/or assumes
responsibility for the incident.

COMPONENT AREAS OF AN EMERGENCY TRAFFIC CONTROL ZONE: A well designed
emergency traffic control zone should reflect five distinct component areas.
These areas are described below in the order in which drivers will encounter.

ADVANCE WARNING AREA: The Advance Warning Area should alert motorists
that there is a traffic situation or difficulty ahead which will require some
action on his/her part.

APPROACH AREA: The Approach area should identify the nature of the
equipment or vehicle that he/ she is about to encounter and allow them to

analyze the situation.

TRANSITION AREA: The Transition Area should provide an indication as to the
expected action to be taken by the motorist. This will help them decide on a

149 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
course of action and execute safe driving techniques prior to entering the
Activity Area.

destroying evidence.) Cones can be used to close off the Buffer Zone to
vehicular traffic by placing them along the skip line (centre line) The skip
line is the dashed line or solid yellow (or white) centerline of the roadway
that is used to separate the highway into driving lanes.

Accident to IAF Aircraft

The event of an accident of an IAF aircraft away from its airfield, information
in this regard is communicated to the nearest Indian Air Force for -

A. Timely rescue;

B. Investigation to determine the cause of accident before any of the
material evidence is destroyed or tampered with;

C. Assessment of damage to Civil/ Government property if any; and
D. Salvage operations.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
C. Assessment of damage to Civil/ Government property if any; and
D. Salvage operations.

Any delay in the communication of this message that is likely to occur as a
result of conflict in the area of jurisdiction i.e., the place of accident as
related to the nearest Air Force Authority, should be avoided. For this
purpose various Police Authorities at all levels should keep themselves
informed of the location of the nearest Air Force Authority.

Investigation: To help proper investigation into the cause of the accident,
the Police authority to whom such an occurrence has been reported, should
also prepare a list of eye witnesses to the accident.

In cases of accidents due to train collision at manned or unmanned level
crossings, the casualties are likely to be high. Immediate action for
conduct of inquests and postmortem examination should therefore be
taken. While in general it is necessary to conduct inquests for all the
dead bodies, by utilizing as many officers of the rank of HC or above as
possible; the postmortem may be done only if necessary. As the cause of
death is apparent, a certificate from the Medical Officer is adequate. The
Railway Police should be informed promptly and clearance for restoration
of rail and road traffic should be done with least possible delay.

A report in Form 49/ 49-A should be prepared. These two should be
forwarded in original without delay to the SP.

4. ‘The vehicle to which the accident was attributed’ means:

A. where only one vehicle was concerned, that vehicle, and
B. Where more than one vehicle was concerned, the vehicle to which the
accident can be attributed prima facie.

page_content:
MODULE - VI
TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT

1. TRAFFIC, FACTORS AND NEED

A. TRAFFIC:

Traffic means:- Road and Road users - The movement of vehicles, ships,
persons, etc., in an area or along a route. Traffic regulation and management
means, control of the movement of vehicles and pedestrians on streets.

Increase in the number of vehicles plying on the roads - that is to say -
increase in population, vehicles for passengers/ transport/ goods etc. are
contributing factors/ reasons for traffic congestion.

B. FACTORS AFFECTING THE TRAFFIC

1. Vehicles: The road should cater to the needs of existing and anticipated
number of vehicles. Mine haul road systems accommodate a wide variety of
sizes and types of vehicles, from smallest jeeps to wheel loaders, haul trucks,
road graders, bull dozers etc. some standard physical dimensions for the
vehicles have been recommended.

Vehicle dimensions

e The vehicular dimensions which can affect the road and traffic.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Vehicle dimensions

e The vehicular dimensions which can affect the road and traffic.

e The width of vehicle affects the width of lanes, shoulders and parking
facility. The capacity of the road will also decrease if the width exceeds
the design values.

e The height of the vehicle affects the clearance height of structures like
over-bridges, under-bridges and electric and other service lines and also
placing of signs and signals.

e The length of the vehicle which affects the extra width of pavement,
minimum turning radius, safe overtaking distance, capacity and the
parking facility.

e The rear overhang control is mainly important when the vehicle takes a
right/ left turn from a stationary point.

e The ground clearance of vehicle comes into picture while designing
ramps.

e Visibility should be clear even in bad weather conditions like fog and
rain;

e Equally important is the side and rear visibility.

157 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. Road factors:

Road surface

Lighting: Illumination is used to illuminate the physical features of the
road way and to aid in driving.

Roughness: The surface should not be too smooth to assist skidding nor
should it be too rough to increase rolling resistance.

Night visibility: Since most accidents occur at night because of reduced
visibility, the traffic designer must strive to improve nighttime visibility.

Geometric aspects: The roadway elements such as pavement slope,
gradient, right of way etc affect transportation in various ways. Central
portion of the pavement is slightly raised and is sloped to either side so
as to prevent the collection of water on the road surface geometric
features of highways like the lane width, lane widening on curves,
minimum curb and corner radius, clearance heights etc are other
aspects.

3. Human (Road users) factors affecting traffic:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3. Human (Road users) factors affecting traffic:

Road users are drivers, passengers, and pedestrians etc., They form the
most complex element of the traffic system.

Human performance varies from individual to individual.

We should deal with a variety of road user characteristics. For example, a
traffic signal timed to permit an average pedestrian to cross the street
safely may cause a severe hazard to an elderly person.

[he elderly persons, the children, the handicapped, the slow and speedy,
and the good and bad drivers.

[The human characteristics like ability to react to a situation, vision and
hearing, and other physical and psychological factors vary from person to
person and depend on age, fatigue, nature of stimuli, presence of drugs/
alcohol etc...

[The design considerations should safely and efficiently accommodate
these factors.

C. NEED FOR TRAFFIC REGULATION AND MANAGEMENT:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
[The design considerations should safely and efficiently accommodate
these factors.

C. NEED FOR TRAFFIC REGULATION AND MANAGEMENT:

[o avoid danger to persons or traffic
To prevent damage to the road or to buildings nearby
To facilitate the passage of traffic

lo prevent use by unsuitable traffic

To preserve the character of a road

158 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e To preserve or improve amenities of the area through which the road
runs.

Road traffic safety refers to the methods and measures used to prevent road
users from being killed or seriously injured. Road users include pedestrians,
cyclists, motorists and vehicle passengers. Best-practices in modern road
safety strategies focus on preventing serious injury and death from vehicle
crashes despite human fallibility whereas previous road safety paradigms
assumed compliance with traffic regulations. Safe road design is now about
implementing a Safe System approach: The basic strategy of a Safe System
approach is to minimize the loss in the event of a crash. This will vary from
one crash scenario to another, depending upon the level of protection offered
to the road users involved.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
According to Section 31 of Police Act, 1861, road safety is one of the
duties of Police. Transport Department plays an important role in the
management of traffic, by issuing Driving Licenses, Registration Certificates
and Permits. Police and Transport Departments, will book cases against
persons who violate traffic rules.

Traffic Jam: A situation in which the moving vehicles on the road will not be
in a position to move further and are held up on the road, due to some
irregularity, is called traffic Jam.

A situation in which, the moving vehicles on the road, will slow down,
due to road problem and cannot proceed at normal speed, is called traffic
congestion.

D. REASONS FOR TRAFFIC JAM -
e Rapid growth of population.

e Concentration of more number of people in cities, due to the availability
of more employment and business opportunities.

e Narrow roads.

e Improper implementation of available laws.

e Lack of funds, for modernization of roads.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e Narrow roads.

e Improper implementation of available laws.

e Lack of funds, for modernization of roads.

e Encroachment of foot paths by petty vendors.

e Lack of proper understanding about traffic rules, among the public.
e Lack of traffic lights and other control devises.

e Absence of institutions with experts, to solve traffic problems.

e Enormous increase in number of vehicles.

e Stoppage of vehicle on the road, due to mechanical defect.

e Irregular parking of vehicles.

159 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Violation of traffic signals.

Political and other rallies, dharnas, Rastha Rokos etc.
Overflowing of water on the roads, due to heavy rains.
Falling of poles and trees across the roads.

Collapse of bridges.

Movement of more vehicles at a time, on the narrow roads.

Stoppage of traffic, during VVIPs visits.

E. ROAD SAFETY-

TEN GOLDEN RULES FOR ROAD SAFETY

1.

10.

Stop or Slow Down: Allow pedestrians to cross first at uncontrolled zebra
crossings.

Buckle Up: So that your family and you are safe in the car. Seat Belts
reduce the chances of death of a car occupant in accidents.

Obey Traffic Rules and Signs: To prevent road accidents.

Obey Speed Limits: For your own safety and that of others. In residential
areas and market places, ideal speed should be 20kmph and the
maximum speed limit is 30kmph.

Keep Vehicle Fit: To prevent breakdown and accidents on road.

Never Use Mobile While Driving: To avoid distractions that largely leads to
accidents.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Never Use Mobile While Driving: To avoid distractions that largely leads to
accidents.

Wear Helmet: To protect your head while riding a two wheeler. A good
quality helmet reduces the chances of severe head injury.

Never Drive Dangerously: To ensure your own safety and that of other
road users.

Be Courteous: Share the road with all and be considerate. Never rage on
the road.

Never Mix Drinking and Driving: Be Responsible. Don’t drink while
driving.

Road accidents are an outcome of the interplay of various factors, some

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Never Mix Drinking and Driving: Be Responsible. Don’t drink while
driving.

Road accidents are an outcome of the interplay of various factors, some

of which are the length of road network, vehicle population, human
population and adherence/ enforcement of road safety regulations etc. Road
accident causes injuries, fatalities, disabilities and hospitalization with
severe socio economic costs across the country. The United Nations has
rightly proclaimed 2011-20 as the Decade of Action on Road Safety. India is
also signatory to Brasilia Declaration and is committed to reduce the
number of road accidents and fatalities by 50 per cent by 2020.

2. TRAFFIC LAWS AND RULES

THE MOTOR VEHICLES ACT, 1988
ACT No. 59 OF 1988

MV ACT extends to whole of India. It came into force on 1 July 1989. No. of
Chapters — 14, No. of Sections —- 217

The Act deals with —

Construction equipment and maintenance; Standards for components
and parts; Anti-pollution control devices; Issue of testing fitness certificates;
Registration marks

¢ CARRIAGE A carriage is a contrivance which is used for carrying or
transporting, especially along or over a solid surface. i.e. wagon, cart
truck, vehicle or other means of conveying goods, or passengers by land.

e The term Automobile includes all vehicles propelled by other than
muscular power, except rail road and railway cars and motor vehicles,
running only on rails and tracks, and road rollers.

e “Contract carriage” means a motor vehicle which carries a passenger or
passengers for hire or reward and is engaged under a contract,

9. Rules for drivers in respect of motor cab stands: - The drivers at
motor cab stands shall observe the following rules:-

i) The drivers of the first two motor cabs on the stand shall stay beside
their cabs and ready to be hired at once by any person provided that a
hirer wishing to hire any particular cab on the stand may do so;

ii) All motor cabs on the stand shall move up as vacancies occur.

iii) No motor cab engaged for some future time shall remain on the stand
unless the driver is willing to accept any intermediate hiring that may
be offered;

iv) No disabled motor cab shall remain on the stand unless such
disablement is temporary and can be and is remedied at once;

(v) every motor cab other than motor cab referred to in Clause (iv) which is
on the stand shall be ready to be hired and when its turn comes the
driver shall accept the first offer of hire which may be made to him
irrespective of the length of the journey for which such offer is made;
