distance of not less than one foot from the motor cab immediately in
front of it and where the stand is by the side of a kerb paralled to and
not more than one foot from the kerb; and

(vii) No driver shall keep a flag fitted to the taxi meter down or shall set it in
motion, before the motor cab is hired in its turn.

3. E.E.Es IN TRAFFIC MANAGEMENT

Traffic involves three ‘E’s:
e Engineering.

e Education.

e Enforcement.

Police Department, Transport Department and Engineering Section of
the R & B Department, should work together to prevent road accidents. The
engineering department deals with the maintenance of roads, margins,
pavements, traffic signals and other precautionary items on highways.
Proper education on traffic rules and regulations, are the joint responsibility
of Police and transport departments. Creating proper understanding on the
traffic rules and regulations among the road users is the main aim of this
branch.

1. Engineering:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
1. Engineering:

Junction is a place, where two different roads will join. Regulation of
traffic, at a junction is a complicated act. Heavy rush of vehicles in different
directions, may cause obstructions, in regulation of traffic, at the junctions,
resulting in road accidents or traffic jams. They may cause serious
dislocation to free flow of traffic. A road to a definite direction from junction
is known as a leg. In developed countries, 50 per cent of the road accidents
are occurring at the junctions. So, less number of road Junctions, will
enhance road safety. Scientific way of road designing, reducing the number
of road junctions, may dilute the traffic density at one place.

Aim of scientific designing of road junctions

“ Drivers will be able to identify the routes, after arriving at the junctions.

“ Separate ways for pedestrians must be provided.

“> Sufficient place for parking vehicles.

“ Signs on the road to enable drivers to identify their route.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
“> Sufficient place for parking vehicles.

“ Signs on the road to enable drivers to identify their route.

“> Required warning signals installation, to control over speeding.

equipment required to regulate traffic. Earmarking places for Police points,
to give directions to vehicles.

Junctions:

Junctions can be divided as 3 legs junction and more than 4 legs
junctions. Traffic can be effectively regulated at the junctions, by forming
roundabouts. These roundabouts, may be in the shape of circle, rectangle or
squares.

page_content: Blind Spot:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
A blind spot in a vehicle is an area around the vehicle that cannot be
directly observed by the driver while at the controls, under existing
circumstances. Blind spots exist in a wide range of vehicles: cars, trucks,
motorboats, sailboats and aircraft. Other types of transport have no blind
spots at all, such as bicycles, motorcycles and horses. Proper adjustment of
mirrors and use of other technical solutions can eliminate or alleviate vehicle
blind spots. In transport, driver visibility is the maximum distance at which
the driver of a vehicle can see and identify prominent objects around the
vehicle. Visibility is primarily determined by weather conditions and by
a vehicle's design. The parts of a vehicle that influence visibility include
the windshield, the dashboard and the pillars. Blind spots may occur in the
front of the driver when the A-pillar (also called the windshield pillar), side-
view mirror, and interior rear-view mirror block a driver's view of the road.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
view mirror, and interior rear-view mirror block a driver's view of the road.
Behind the driver, there are additional pillars, headrests, passengers, and
cargo that may reduce visibility.

hould be able to act positively to ensure free flow of traffic in the event

Vv

° In a democratic country, cooperation of public is essential, to conduct
and organize any type of activity. If the Transport and Police Departments
go on implementing the laws, without educating the people about the traffic
rules and regulations, people will not cooperate and there may be criticism
against Police, leading to lawlessness. Lack of proper understanding about
the roads and highways, on the part of passengers and drivers, can be
mentioned as one of the reasons for traffic problems. Explaining the people
about the rules, regulations and system of traffic management, is the main
aim of traffic education. It will increase road safety. In our State, more
number of deaths is caused, due to road accidents, than killings by the
extremists and other suicide deaths.

169 | Page

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Making laws and their implementation alone, may not give good results.
People should be involved in the implementation, by suitably activating
them. They should be made to understand through traffic education. Most of
the road accidents are taking place, due to non observance of traffic rules.
Efforts must be made to explain the relevant rules and regulations, to the
public and making them follow the prescribed rules voluntarily.

Traffic signals, which are aimed for traffic regulation and road safety,
are divided into 3 types:

1. Mandatory signals.
2. Cautionary signals.
3. Informative signals.

1. Mandatory signals:- These signals must be strictly followed. Otherwise,
road accidents will become inevitable. Ex: - stop, give way, No entry, one
way, prohibited, H. vehicles prohibited, No right turn, no left turn, No U
turn, overtaking prohibited, No Horn, speed limit, No parking. These signals
are shown in circles:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. Cautionary signals: - These signals are shown in triangles. They warn,
about the danger if not followed.

e Turn to right or left, hair pin bend, reverse turn, Cross Roads, Road to
Left or Right, ,T“ Junction. Round about, speed breaker, dangerous
curve, unmanned railway crossing, ,Y“ Junction. Cycle cross,
Pedestrians, School Zone, Men at work, Railway cross Narrow (Manned)
Bridge.

3. Informative signals: - (Directory signals): These signals are installed, to
give information to passengers and vehicle drivers. Shown in squares. Ex: -
public telephone at 2 KM, petrol bunk at 1 KM, hospital, hotel etc. P
Indicates parking place, +Indicates first aid centre.

Traffic signals on the highways and junctions are very much useful for
traffic management and develop the understanding about the roads and
traffic, among the vehicle drivers.

page_content:
TELANAGANA STATE POLICE DEPARTMENT

STANDARD OPERATING PROCEDURE
FOR
TRAFFIC REGULATIONS ENFORCEMENT THROUGH CONTACT & NON-
CONTACT MODES,

1.0 PURPOSE

The traffic regulations enforcement objective of Telangana State Police
Department is to reduce traffic violations/ incidents and to facilitate the safe
& expeditious flow of vehicular/ pedestrian traffic through voluntary
compliance with traffic regulations.

2.0 POLICY

Telangana State Police seeks to promote traffic safety, uniform
enforcement of all traffic laws, & education of the public regarding traffic
safety, and recognising the multiple options officers have when enforcing
traffic laws.

3.0 TRAFFIC RULES ENFORCEMENT

Telangana State Police plans to further tighten its enforcement in the
coming years and ensure road discipline. The vehicles plying on roads have
increased and also have led to serious traffic congestion and thus often
provoke the people to break rules to reach their destinations on time.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
The primary objective of Police is not charging/ collecting fine; Police just
want the commuters become more aware about the laws and risk factors of
driving. This would make the commuters think twice before breaking traffic
rules.

It is imperative for police to focus mainly on three parameters viz
Engineering, Education & Enforcement for effective traffic management.

3.1 TRAFFIC ENGINEERING

Road Re-engineering process has to be conducted periodically at the
junctions and hotspots where traffic accidents/ deaths will occur frequently
by involving key stakeholders i.e govt. bodies such as Municipalities, State
Road Transport/ National & State Highways Authorities, HRDCL,
Authorities/ Cantonment Boards/ R & B/ Panchayathies etc. This aspect
aims to improve the existing transportation infrastructure and factor in

safety when designing new transportation infrastructure. Engineering
measures include:

i) Identifying and developing solutions for known road safety problem
areas.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
i) Identifying and developing solutions for known road safety problem
areas.

172 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
ii) Reviewing standards for road development and moving towards
a safety conscious road form, customized for local conditions.

iii) Conducting road safety audits

All the unit police officers need to have regular coordination with
engineering wings of all the above departments for taking up design/
modifications of junctions, providing road infrastructure etc as per their road
development plan and traffic management. Further, the supervisory officers
should ensure involvement of the above departments and execution of the
following activities for the safety of commuters and pedestrians: -

e Road Re-engineering - Development of junctions, marking U-turns, Free
eft turns for traffic management

e Installation of Traffic Signaling Systems for reducing waiting time at
junctions

e Provision of pedestrian signals for safety while crossing roads

e Arranging traffic signages and road markings

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
e Provision of pedestrian signals for safety while crossing roads

e Arranging traffic signages and road markings

e Road widening, shifting of bus bays if nearer to junctions and removing
illegal encroachments for free flow of traffic

e Marking parking places for vehicles
e Building footpaths and railings, for the safety of pedestrians
Traffic Accident Investigation

The necessary reliance on engineering, education, and enforcement
programs requires excellent traffic accident data. Therefore, these
requirements place a significant emphasis on thorough and accurate traffic
accident investigation. The unit police officers should encourage the proper
collection of traffic and accident investigation data for implementation of
prevention measures and remedial countermeasures.

3.2 TRAFFIC EDUCATION

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3.2 TRAFFIC EDUCATION

Telangana State Police stands committed to the concept of traffic
education as an important factor in prevention and law adherence. Whenever
appropriate, Telangana State Police will preface any new or concentrated
traffic enforcement with a period of public education or re-education.

Lack of knowledge among common people and lack of strict enforcement
of traffic rules are the main reasons behind the accelerating rate of traffic
accidents and deaths. There are so many rules and regulations which are not
known to a majority of public. People must be upgraded with knowledge about
traffic rules for their safety through conduct of periodical trainings, and
awareness programs. Since people are not aware of such rules, they tend to
break the law and become victims of frequent road accidents. Hence, police

173 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
need to be strict/firm in enforcing law and asking citizens to become more
aware about the laws and risk factors of driving.

Trainings and awareness programs have to be conducted to the main
targeted groups viz RTC Drivers, Auto Drivers, Taxi Drivers, Students &
Youth, School Children, Pedestrians, Drunken Drivers, Traffic violators, and
Parents of underage drivers at Traffic Training Institutions/ Schools/
Counselling Centres/ Community Centres etc by the police. Counselling
should be conducted for violators involved in endangerous to human life
violations like Drunken Driving, Talking on Cell phone while driving, Driving
without and for Parents/ guardians of underaged children etc.

3.3. TRAFFIC ENFORCEMENT

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3.3. TRAFFIC ENFORCEMENT

Enforcement under M.V Act against violations in order to discipline the
violators to ensure free flow of traffic and safety on Roads is a key role of the
Police and can work as an effective tool in achieving its objectives of reducing
the traffic violations and incidents. In order to make the enforcement effective,
Police Officers should take appropriate enforcement action to address traffic
rules violations, un-safe driving, pedestrian behaviour, focussing on areas
with a high traffic incidents as well as being responsive to neighbourhood and
citizen complaints.

In this context, the Government of Telangana has issued a G.O Ms. No.

metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

which introduced the system of penalty points for the Traffic Violations under

period of one year. If repeated after revoking by the same individual and
accrues more than 12 points, his DL will be placed under suspension
for 2 years and similarly for Third time - 3 Years suspension. The
penalty points are in addition to the fine imposed for respective

If the accrueded penalty points exceed 12, the enforcement officer
should collect original DL from the commuter and handover to RTA
Authorities for taking necessary action. In case the commuter is not
carrying original licence with him, the vehicle can be seized temporarily

Therefore, all commuters shall be aware of penalty point system to avoid

Further, Telangana State Police have initiated cashless contact and non-
contact enforcement through e-Challan System, a unique feature first of its
kind in the country, which brings in more transparency to the system.
Violator(s) have option to pay fine amount through defined payment gateways
using Net banking, Debit/ Credit cards, Mobile Apps, etc, which will be a
welcoming feature for both officers and public. Due to introduction of cashless
enforcement, Police shall not collect any fine amount directly from the
violators.

3.4 UNIFORM ENFORCEMENT POLICIES
3.4.1 Contact Enforcement

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
3.4 UNIFORM ENFORCEMENT POLICIES
3.4.1 Contact Enforcement

Sub-Inspectors of Police and above rank officers are only empowered to
conduct Contact Enforcement. On noticing traffic violation, the enforcement
officer in the field shall stop the violator and enter the violation& vehicle
details into e-Challan System through a digital device/ TAB. A “spot challan”
has to be printed through a blue tooth printer connected to TAB for issue to
violator. The violators has to pay fine amount at payment gateways within (7)
days from the date of issue of challan.

Further, Contact enforcement should be implemented in respect of
violations committed by inter-state vehicles drivers. Only in such cases,
Police Officer shall collect imposed fine amount directly from the violator(s)
and deposit the same in official account through designated Banks.

a) Checking Team Composition

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
a) Checking Team Composition

At the place of checkpoint, apart from the officer only 2 traffic persons
should be used as support staff. In routine checking, more than 2
supporting staff shall not be used.

b) Logistics

The SHO concerned has to ensure the deployment of the following items
at checkpoints

e TAB (SI and above),

e Blue Tooth Printer with Paper Rolls
e Body-worn Camera

e Display Banners about Enforcement
e Metal Barricades

e Plastics Barricades, if available

« Cones

Procedure for Enforcement Activity

For carrying out Citizen Friendly Enforcement, Telangana State Police

has introduced the following enforcement actions facilitating commuters in a

fair, impartial, courteous, transparent and professional manner —

Procedure for Vehicles Stopping

i)

ii)

iii)

iv)

A “STOP” is temporarily stopping of a commuter for enquiry, verification of
documents etc.

Officers conducting a stop shall be in neat turn out and their name plate &
police badge properly displayed to clearly identify themselves as Police
Officers.

Once the commuters’ vehicle is stopped, one of the traffic cops should
inform that vehicle checking is going on; hence, stopped.

Vehicles should be stopped at random. It should be ensured that only 2 to
3 vehicles to be handled at any point of time.

Procedure for Vehicle Checking Approach

i)

ii)

iii)

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Procedure for Vehicle Checking Approach

i)

ii)

iii)

Vehicle(s) checking should be done during non-peak hours only. In peak
hours all the officers and men shall focus on Smooth Regulation’. Peak
hours are usually between 9-O0AM to 11-00 AM and 5-00 PM to 7-00 PM
preferably as per local official hours. During these hours, if any violation
is noticed by the Enforcement Officers, photograph of violation may be
taken to upload in e-Challan System and issue challan to the
violator.The commuters need not be stopped during these hours to
dislocate the free flow of traffic.

Checking should be done on straight and wide roads only. Checking
should not be preferred on free left turnings, on narrow roads and nearer
to junctions.

When the checking is planned, specially designed “Vehicle checking
barricades” have to be placed at vehicle checking point.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
When the checking is planned, specially designed “Vehicle checking
barricades” have to be placed at vehicle checking point.

Vehicle checking should be made in the presence of the officer not below
the rank of Sub-Inspector. In the absence of Sub-Inspector of Police or
Inspector of Police no other officers/ men should stop vehicles to check
the violations.

177 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
v) The In-charge officer shall ensure that the process of vehicle checking
should not obstruct the free flow of traffic and lead to en-dangerous to
the citizens in any manner.

vi) In case, the vehicle checking is planned in late evenings, the checking
place should be under proper illumination.

Checking Team Functions

i) One traffic officer should stand near the stopper and clearly indicate a
commuter specifically, to take his vehicle to the left by giving hand
signal.

ii) Vehicle checking team should behave politely with commuters, and also
assist him while showing the documents. As far as possible, the in-
charge officer should only interact with commuters. In case, support
staff interacts with the citizens, it should be directly monitored by the
officer.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
iii) If any violation is found, the same should to be explained to the
commuter in brief, including the fine amount, before imposing it.
Whenever any commuter asks any information related to M.V. Act/ Rules
and procedures, the in-charge officer should patiently furnish the
required information.

iv) The checking team should accept electronic form of Certification of
Registration, Insurance, Driving Licence or any other document
demanded by police.

v) The staff should be courteous throughout checking with the citizens and
after completion of the process they should say “thank you” for
cooperation, in a pleasant manner in Telugu/ Hindi/ Urdu.

vi) Body-worn Cameras should be used invariably while conducting drive.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
vi) Body-worn Cameras should be used invariably while conducting drive.

vii) If any commuter argues irrationally, the same should be video graphed.
In any commuter prefers prolonged argument, the in-charge officer shall
immediately inform to the Inspector of Police & DSP over handheld set.
Whenever the in-charge officer feels necessary, he may take assistance of
extra force.

viii) Whenever pamphlets are supplied from the Headquarters to educate the
citizens, the same should be distributed to the citizens at these points.

ix) After concluding the checking, the “vehicle checking barricades” should
be removed from that checkpoint and put it in a safe place.

178 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Role of Checking Officer in exceptional circumstances

i)

ii)

iii)

iv)

If a commuter claimed any emergency which seem to be genuine, unless
there is a grave visible violation, he should be allowed to proceed without
any checking.

When a family is found in a vehicle, only in-charge officer should interact
with them and only relevant/ required information should be asked.
While dealing with women and senior citizens, in case of minor
violations, the same shall be explained to them, and they should be let
off by advising rectifying the same.

Apart from imposing fine for any violation, the in-charge officer should
also advise and guide the citizen about the procedure to rectify the same.

Violator(s) should pay the challan amount within (7) days from the date of
issue of challan at any of the designated payment gateways. If they fail to
pay the challan amount within stipulated period, appropriate legal
procedure need to be initiated as per law.

If a commuter had [3] or less challans pending on his/ her name, he/ she
should be informed about the details of pending challans and advised to
pay the challan(s) amount immediately in order to get their detained
vehicle released.

If pending challans are found [4] or more on commuter’s name, he/ she
should be noted as “Repeat Violator” and forced to pay the outstanding
challans amount for release of the vehicle and documents.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
If the pending challans are more than [14], the vehicle of the violator
should be detained and released only after conformity of payment of
pending fine amounts.

Even on clear indication, if any commuter does not stop the vehicle and
wilfully avoid police checking, the same should be photographed, and
upload the details u/s 132/ 177 of M.V. Act for generation of e-Challan.

Whenever the ownership details of the Registration Certificate are not
tallied with that of the respondent, thorough verification should be made
with extra care, before releasing the vehicle.

4.0. e-CHALLAN SYSTEM

In order to ensure enforcement of traffic regulations through both contact
and non-contact modes, Telangana State Police has initiated an IT enabled
integrated e-Challan System in all the Districts/ Commissionerates of Police
across the State for enabling timely enforcement of Motor Vehicles Act, 1988
and to make the citizens lot more responsive to traffic regulations adherence.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Traffic rules violations captured through the digital Cameras/
Surveillance CCTV Cameras/ Automatic Red Light Violation Detection
(ARLVD) System/ Laser Gun Cameras, Automatic Number Plate Recorder
(ANPR) etc have to be uploaded along with violation details toe-Challan
System at the concerned Police Station/ Surveillance Camera Monitoring
Centre (SCMC) or at e-Challan Centre. Accordingly, challans will be printed
and posted to the address of registered owner(s) of the vehicle(s) for payment
of fine amount at payment gateways.

Functional Features in e-Challan System

The enforcement officers can use the following functional features (9)
through TAB (a digital device) while in field during contact enforcement.

i) Drunken Driving

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
i) Drunken Driving

Press the “Drunken Driving” button for entering the information viz
Vehicle Number, Driver details (if not available, enter Name, Father’s Name
& Address), Phone Number, Adhaar Number, Breath Analyser Test Slip
Number, Place at alcohol consumed, Occupation of Driver etc..After entering
the information press “Submit” button for generation of e-Ticket. Print two
Breath Analyser Test slips and issue e-Ticket u/s 185 (a), 130/ 177 MV Act
along with Breath Analyser Test result slip duly signed by respondent. Also
take photograph through TAB while issuing e-Ticket to respondent. [See
detailed procedure mentioned in SOP for Drunk Driving]

ii) Spot Challan

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
ii) Spot Challan

Press the “Spot Challan” button to generate challans through TAB in
Online. In issuing Spot Challan, the entry of Driving Licence Number with
the combination of Date of Birth is mandatory in addition to other details.
Signature of the violator has to be taken on copy of challan before issue as
acceptance of truth. An SMS alert will be sent automatically to the violator
and registered owner of the vehicle as soon as the challan is issued.
Violator(s) can pay fine amount on the spot through payment gateways using
Net Banking, Debit/ Credit cards/ PayTM / other Mobile Apps etc. The

181 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
payment details will be updated online in e-Challan System for future
reference.

iii) Crane Lifting Enforcement (under 39(b) 41 C.P Act)

Press this button for booking cases under C.P. Act and generation of
challans for violations such as Parking Vehicles Wrongly, Encroachment of
Shop Keepers, Footpath vendors, etc. The vehicles and shop materials
(properly packed) should be lifted with cranes and moved to the concerned
Police Station, keeping a cone at that place with due message for violator to
contact the Police Station. After explaining the details of violation, a challan
need to be issued to the violator. Signature of the violator has also to be
taken on copy of challan as acceptance of truth. An SMS alert will be sent to
the violator as soon as the challan is issued. After conformityof payment of
challan amount, the vehicle or material lifted should be released/ handed
over to the respondent.

iv) Vehicle History

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
iv) Vehicle History

By pressing Vehicle History button, the field officer can view the
details of vehicle, owner and pending challans on vehicle under MV Act and
CP Act. This will facilitate field officer(s) in advising the violators for prompt
payment of pending challan(s) if any, in strict enforcement on repeat violator
sand prompt observation of traffic regulations to avoid untoward incidents. If
the violator clears the pending challan(s), the transaction details will be
updated at central server instantly.

Further, by entering Vehicle Registration Number, the field officers can
find whether the vehicle is a theft vehicle, sold out vehicle, vehicle involved
in crime, RTA Tax pending vehicle, Charge sheet issued vehicle, etc., In case
vehicle is involved in any crime, the system will automatically pop up a
message that it is a “Crime involved Vehicle” and the message will also be
printed clearly in Challan.

v) Vehicle Release

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
v) Vehicle Release

Once the pending challan payments are made, the transaction details
will be automatically updated at centrally located Server and displays
“Pending Challans are cleared” on TAB, for release of detained vehicle or any
other items.

vi) Duplicate challans print

Officers can print and issue duplicate challan(s) in respect of challans
issued earlier to the violators, on demand from the violator, in case they
missed earlier challan.

182 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
vii) Reports

The following reports can be generated on various parameters periodically
and as when required-

Abstract Reports - Day-wise/ Month-wise/ Year-wise abstract reports in
respect of number of challans generated and fines imposed by officers, fine
amounts collected through payment gateways etc

Officer’s Performance - This report explains challans generated with
Vehicle Registration Numbers, Fine Amount, Violation details, Date & Time of
Offence etc.

Offline Report - This report explains no. of challans generated in Offline
mode and amounts collected. However, the date of cases booked offline will
be updated periodically manually to central server.

Conclusion

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Conclusion

Traffic enforcement / services represent a collaborative effort across
many disciplines. While education, engineering, and law enforcement are
important, there are many more factors at work. Creating safer roads, safer
cars, and, most important, safer drivers will help prevent traffic-related
fatalities. Traffic services and management will remain a vital part of these
lifesaving efforts

kek

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
4. TRAFFIC REGULATION

Organization and functioning of traffic Police Station and Highway
Police Station

PSO. NO. 702-1. Traffic police station will be established in all
commissioner ate, cities and “A” grade municipalities and also in other
places depending on the necessity. The functions and responsibilities of a
Traffic and Highway police station shall be to achieve smooth and safe flow
of traffic by efficient regulation and enforcement of traffic laws and to ensure
prevention of accidents.

Efficient road traffic management should aim at striking just balance
between 3 essential factors.

i) Satisfactory circulation

ii) Absolute safety and

iii) Reasonable economics.

Basic duties of the traffic police personnel include:

A. Manning fixed traffic points in a town or city

Mobile patrol to regulate traffic and clearing the road
Enforcement of MV Act and Rules and other notifications.

Check and control of rash, dangerous and drunken driving.

apo 8

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Check and control of rash, dangerous and drunken driving.

apo 8

Patrolling of highways, enforcement of traffic discipline and maintenance
of order on the main roads

a

Removing obstructions to traffic flows and organizing traffic diversion
whenever necessary, both in the congested areas of the towns/city and
on the highways.

G. Prompt arrival at the scenes of road accidents, guarding the scene,
rendering first aid and shifting the injured to the hospital, intimating to
the L&O Police and assisting them in the investigation of the cases.

H. Communicating information to L&O police/DPS of any incident or
movement of criminals or suspected persons or property, which they
come to know.

I. Observation and apprehension of wanted persons or taking charge of
missing persons in course of their duties.

j. Setting up barricades to check vehicles on alert from the police
authorities.

185 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
K. Assisting L&O police or detective police in checking for suspicious
persons or properties.

L. Assistance to L&O police during large gatherings

M. Undertaking Traffic education

N. Maintenance of prescribed records

O. Upkeep and maintenance of all traffic equipment including traffic signals

P. Take special care of school children, old or blind or handicapped persons
and others at road crossings and peak traffic points.

Q. Monitoring of traffic on the highways on a full time basis.

R. Advise the local bodies and other authorities for erection of road signals,
road painting and other matters connected with traffic engineering

S. Coordination with local authorities, highway department, R&B
department, Panchayathra Department, Telephones, Water Works,
Drainage, Electricity, in the matter relating to free flow of traffic and its
regulation, occasioned by maintenance and repair works undertaken by
the concerned departments.

Traffic detachment:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Traffic detachment:

The traffic and highway police stations will be under the control of a
DSP in the districts. In large cities where number of traffic police stations is
more a Deputy Commissioner of Police heads the branch. Inspectors and
Reserve Inspectors will divide the police station into sectors allotted to the
SIs/ Inspectors. The staff of the Traffic PS is divided into sections and each
section kept in charge of a Head Constable. The traffic section shall work in
accordance with cycles of duty to be fixed with reference to the local

conditions. The location of the traffic and highway police station should be
determined on the extent of the highway to be patrolled and the regulation of
traffic in large towns. The locations should be determined on the basis of
nodal traffic points and the need for setting up of police check-posts during
emergencies.

Highway patrol mobile:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Highway patrol mobile:

Ahighway patrolis either a police unit created primarily for the
purpose of overseeing and enforcing traffic safety compliance on roads and
highways, or a detail within an existing local or regional police agency that is
primarily concerned with such duties. They are also referred to in many
countries as traffic police, although in other countries this term is more
commonly used to refer to foot officers on point duty who control traffic at
junctions.

Duties of highway patrols or traffic police may include the following:

186 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Accident investigation gathering evidence to determine the cause of a

roadway accident.

Commercial vehicle enforcement

Enforcing highway laws related to

An effective traffic control device must fulfill the following requirements.

Accident analysis and prevention

These data can be used for planning, monitoring and evaluating TLE
(Traffic Law Enforcement) in Hyderabad. These can help identify traffic
control and human factors that could lead to traffic non-compliance, and
help identify priorities for improving road safety. These data indicate a need
to enhance the safety and to assess the effectiveness of the current legal
action as deterrence to improve road safety. Recommendations to enhance
TLE within the given resources of the police are made.

page_content:
A. INVESTIGATION OF CASES, MOTOR ACCIDENT CLAIMS, THIRD
PARTY INSURANCE SOLATIUM SCHEME, FIRST AID

Standard Operating Procedure (SOP)of Investigation of Traffic
Accident (Road Accident, Automobile Crash) Cases

1.

INTRODUCTION

The rapid urbanization, often without planning and mass transportation
facilities have led to chaotic conditions in many cities, leading to massive
strain on every public facility organisation, particularly, the police
department.

While road travel is one of the essential activities, it has become
hazardous due to a variety of factors. The advent of fast-moving vehicles
with sophisticated technology and a comparatively better road
infrastructure have also increased the risk to lives as people take to rash
and negligent driving leading to accidents which pose threat to everyone,
particularly, the pedestrians.

Offences and under IPC/BNS:

Sections

279 IPC/ 281BNS: Driving or riding on a public way so rashly or
negligently as to endanger human life, etc.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Offences and under IPC/BNS:

Sections

279 IPC/ 281BNS: Driving or riding on a public way so rashly or
negligently as to endanger human life, etc.

336 IPC/ 125 BNS: Doing any act which endangers human life or the
personal safety of others

337 IPC/ 125 BNS: Causing hurt by an act which endangers human life,
etc.

338 IPC/ 125 BNS: Causing grievous hurt by an act which endangers
human life, etc.

304-A IPC/ 106 BNS: Causing death by rash or negligent act
Appropriate sections of Motor Vehicle Act shall also be added in the road
traffic accident case where ever required for example:

Sections of 134-A r/w 187 MV Act for accused driver of crime vehicle not
shifting injured to hospital for treatment, except under circumstances
mentioned in section.

134-B, R/W 187 MV Act for accused driver of crime vehicle not informing
matter to police.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. WHEN COMPLAINANT/ PETITIONER COMES TO POLICE STATION TO
LODGE COMPLAINT

Offer chair to the petitioner and make him comfortable which would repose
confidence in him.

a. If the Complainant comes to police station with a written
Complaint/ Petition

e Take petition, go through the contents
e Enquire with him about the contents to confirm the facts

b. If the Complainant comes to police station to report about the
offence orally -—

SHO by himself or through his subordinate shall reduce the oral statement
of complainant into writing, read over the statement to him, when he
confirms about the contents, take his signature.

c. Ifthe Complainant comes to Police Station with injuries

— if the nature of injuries requires immediate treatment, take the injured
person immediately to hospital after providing required first aid for
examination of wounds and treatment.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
— When the injured person is unable to give statement, take the complaint
rom eyewitness or from blood relatives for registering a case.

— If the complainant has nominal injuries, his statement may be reduced
in to writing for registering the case

d. In the instances mentioned above (A, B, & C)

— Make endorsement on the complaint mentioning

. Date and time of receipt of complaint/statement
. Crime number
. Section of law applied as per the contents of the petition

— Apply appropriate sections like—

*  134-A r/w 187 MV Act for accused driver of crime vehicle not shifting
injured to hospital for treatment, except under circumstances
mentioned in section

* 134-B, R/W 187 MV Act for accused driver of crime vehicle not
informing matter to police

— In case the vehicle driver is not having license, apply relevant sections of
MV Act.

191 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
— In case the vehicle is not having insurance apply relevant sections of MV
Act.

— Make an entry in the General Diary
— Register the case by issuing FIR
— Provide a copy of FIR free of cost to Complainant (Sec.154 (2) Cr.P.C.)
— Dispatch Original FIR to jurisdictional magistrate forthwith
— Inform about offence to all concerned officers
— 1.0. proceeds to Scene of crime with IO kit box & case file.
e. Soon after the receipt of the complaint -

— Depute two staff members to SOC with required material of guarding
scene (Cones and Scene cordoning tape/ Ribbon) to preserve the
evidence. Instruct them to control the curious on-lookers who may
disturb scene of offence and clear unauthorized persons.

3. INVESTIGATION PROCESS AT SCENE OF CRIME (SOC).

Secure the presence two respectable and independent mediators (Panchas) of
the locality.

A. Investigation activity like Survey and recording of Scene of offence.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
A. Investigation activity like Survey and recording of Scene of offence.

— Note the time of arrival at scene of offence for writing in case diary to be
written U/S 172 Cr. P. C./192 BNSS.

— Check the perimeter of scene to ensure that entire scene is secured.

— Note down the GPS Coordinates (Latitudes-longitudes) of the scene of
crime.

— Place compass to know magnetic north.

— Clear unauthorized persons from the scene.

— Prevent anyone from disturbing anything in the entire scene of offence.
— Identify and fix the permanent objects/ reference points.

— Reconstruct the scene with the help of eyewitnesses.

— Examine the scene of offence minutely in the presence of mediators by
following standard methods for identifying physical evidence/material
objects (M.Os.) like blood stains, traces of crime vehicle which are useful
for investigation.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
— Provide numbers to each identified material object (M.O.) so that they are
clearly visible in photographs and easy to describe in Observation Report
(Panchanama) and Sketch Map of Scene of Crime.

192 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
— Take measurements of the scene carefully with specific reference to
Permanent object/Reference point, material objects and other important
features of the crime scene.

B. Make careful note of following points in respect of dead body:
— Position of dead body
— Physical condition of dead body and clothing
— Location and nature of injuries on the body of deceased

— Ascertain whether position of dead body was altered after offence before
arrival of 1.0.

— Examine the body of deceased for physical evidence like loose hairs, fibre
etc.

— Move the dead body to shortest possible distance and place it on a mat.

— Examine the ground underneath the dead body and note specific points
if any.

— Examine entire dead body (back and front side) for additional physical
evidence that may become visible after movement.

C. Take digital photographs of scene on following lines:

a. Cover all the material objects and reference points (Longshot)

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
C. Take digital photographs of scene on following lines:

a. Cover all the material objects and reference points (Longshot)

b. Cover all the material objects identified including the dead body

c. Each material object shall be photographed separately (close shots)
D. Collecting Material objects (M.Os.)

— Collect Material Objects/physical evidence which are useful in the
investigation like blood stains and pack carefully without causing any
damage.

— Collect required physical evidence from the deceased person like clothing
etc.

— The process of packing, labelling, sealing and attestation by punch
witnesses should be completed at the scene of crime itself.

E. Preparing crime detail form (CDF) (IIF -II)
— Carefully fill up all the columns of crime detail form

— Panchanama and sketch map are useful for fixing the scene of offence,
reconstruction of scene of offence and other purposes.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
— Panchanama and sketch map are useful for fixing the scene of offence,
reconstruction of scene of offence and other purposes.

— Panchanama shall be prepared in the presence of mediators by
describing the complete details whatever is done at scene of offence
including the description and collection of physical evidence, property

193 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
recovered/seized for the purpose of investigation because they are the
witnesses who depose evidence about entire process done at scene of
crime in the court.

— The date and time of starting and closing shall be mentioned in
panchanama.

— After recording panchanama, the IO shall read over the contents to
mediators (panchas) and obtain their signatures when the panchas agree
the contents as true.

— it is better to prepare an observation report of scene of offence
(Panchanama) separately and attach same to CDF with reference to
column numbers 9.

F. Draw sketch map of Scene of Crime as per the specifications.

— With reference to column numbers 11 of CDF, it is better to prepare a
sketch map of scene of offence separately and attach it to CDF.

G. Conducting Inquest over the dead body and sending dead body for
conducting PME:

— Conduct inquest over the body of the deceased in the presence of
mediators by carefully filling each and every column of inquest proforma
accurately

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
— Describe general description of the dead body.
— Describe the direction, physical position, and condition of the dead body.

— Mention clothing (style, colour, unusual appearances such as tears,
holes, pockets turned inside — outside etc.) any stains.

— Describe location and appearance of wounds, bruises, foreign materials
etc.

— Note the details about the blood: (a) Location (b) Pattern (c) Direction of
flow (d) Degree of coagulation on body and clothes.

— Describe condition, location and extent of (1) Rigor mortis (2) hypostasis
and other body conditions.

— Every detail touching the apparent cause of the death shall be recorded.

— Exact measurements must be given, and vague expressions should be
avoided.

h. After completing writing of inquest report.

— Prepare requisition to medical officer for conducting postmortem
examination over the body of the deceased.

— Shift the dead body to mortuary for conducting postmortem examination.

194 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
— Dispatch the original inquest to jurisdictional magistrate.

Note: Public inconvenience with regard to traffic also should be kept in
mind by not compromising with the process of investigation. If there is
possibility of diverting the traffic in alternative routes, make such
arrangements.

4. FURTHER INVESTIGATION

— Recording of statements of all the witnesses like eyewitnesses, victims,
injured persons, u/s 161 Cr. P. C./ Sec 180 BNSS

— Write first Case Diary (Section 172 Cr. P. C./ sec 192 BNSS ( Record of
investigation) incorporating entire process by mentioning the timings and
places visited including the further plan of action.

— Forward the material objects to FS L for analysis.

— Explain the victim family about the benefits available under Motor
Vehicle Act i.e., for claiming compensation from Motor Accident Claims
Tribunal.

Arrest of accused persons

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Arrest of accused persons

The I.0. may arrest the accused person, if he is satisfied the conditions
mentioned in the section 41(1) (b)(ii) Cr. P. C)/ SEC 35 BNSS

— Arrest should be made by observing required formalities (Sections 41-B,
46, 50, Cr. P. C)/ Sec 36, 43, 47 BNSS)

— Intimate about the arrest to the friend or relative nominated by accused
as per section “50-A” Cr. P. C./ sec 48 BNSS and serve a notice
accordingly.

— Prepare arrest memo (IIF - III) by filling all columns carefully and
properly.

— The arrested persons should be produced before the Medical Officer with

a requisition for examination as required u/s 53 Cr. P. C./ sec 51 BNSS
and obtain medical examination report.

— Enter the details of accused persons in the records of police station such
as prisoner’s search register, sentry relief book and general diary.

— Interrogate accused persons and record his detailed interrogation report.
— Obtain photographs of accused
Remand of accused:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
— Interrogate accused persons and record his detailed interrogation report.
— Obtain photographs of accused
Remand of accused:

— Arrested person shall be produced before the Magistrate within 24 hours
of his arrest with a detailed remand case diary, explaining circumstances
leading to incident and linking the criminal to crime, evidence collected,
details of recovery if any.

195 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Examine crime vehicle and seize it under a seizure report and mention
about any damaged portion with reference to accident in report.

Obtain photographs of the crime vehicle covering the portion of damaged
part.

Examine all the documents of crime vehicle like Registration Certificate,
trip sheet/Statistical Return, insurance papers, driving license and
obtain the copies of the same for providing to the complainant/victim.

Original documents which are required for investigation may be seized.

Send requisition to Motor Vehicle Inspector for inspection of crime
vehicle.

Inspection of crime vehicle by Motor Vehicle Inspector.
Send information to RTO that the vehicle is involved in a criminal case.

Get the reports like wound certificates of injured persons, PME Report,
FSL report, motor vehicle inspector report etc.

5. REVIEW OF EVIDENCE BY I.0., FINALISATION OF INVESTIGATION,
PREPARING AND FILING CHARGE SHEET IN THE COURT

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
5. REVIEW OF EVIDENCE BY I.0., FINALISATION OF INVESTIGATION,
PREPARING AND FILING CHARGE SHEET IN THE COURT

After completion of the investigation charge sheet has to be prepared by

carefully filling all the columns of “FINAL REPORT FORM (IIF-V)”. The charge
sheet shall contain:

Facts of the case.

Investigation made, nature of direct and indirect evidence discovered.
Arrest of accused.

The details of recoveries.

Arrest and remand of accused.

Whether arrested accused are on bail or in custody.

Searches conducted and seizures made if any.

Sequence of events in chronological order.

Memorandum of evidence which contain the list of witnesses, and what
each witness is likely to say.

Prepare and serve Notice on the complainant U/S 173 (2) (ii) Cr. P. C./
sec 193 BNSS.

While filing charge sheet in the Court, see that all the required
documents, case property, MOs returned from FSL are deposited in the
Court.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
While filing charge sheet in the Court, see that all the required
documents, case property, MOs returned from FSL are deposited in the
Court.

Copies of the charge sheet in full shape should be furnished to the
Prosecuting Officer and accused persons.

196 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
B. PREVENTION OF ACCIDENTS AND REDUCING CASUALITIES
1. Investigation of cases

Police should be able to investigate a traffic accident, obtain witness
and victim information, determine the cause and fault of an accident and
properly fill out the FIR and Accident Report form explorers.

Initial Steps:

Prompt arrival at the scene of an accident is essential. Safety, however,
should be emphasized at all times. While in route, the officer should
maintain contact with the Dispatcher. They should also be on the lookout for
any suspicious or damaged vehicles fleeing the general area.

Arrival at the Scene: - While enroute to the scene, the officer should obtain
as much preliminary information as possible concerning the accident.

Whether or not additional support is proceeding to the scene, such as
wrecker, ambulance, or additional Police patrols By obtaining such
information prior to arrival at the scene, the investigators may more quickly

and efficiently respond to the needs o:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
and efficiently respond to the needs o:

Essential Elements of an Inves

the situation.

tigation: Preparing CDF FORM which

contains the essential investigative e

ements who, what, when, where, why,

and how the accident happened. It may be supported by scaled diagrams,
photographs and/or statements.

Recording the statements of victims and witnesses, shifting the crime
vehicle from the scene of offence, ensuring the free flow of regular traffic
should followS.

A requisition may send to motor vehicle inspector for inspection of
crime vehicle in case of fatal road accidents.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
A requisition may send to motor vehicle inspector for inspection of
crime vehicle in case of fatal road accidents.

Photography: Photographs are used to supplement an accident investigation
and the written report concerning the accident. When used in court
testimony, they help indicate and explain the chain of events. The
investigator should take as many pictures as necessary to properly depict
the accident. They should represent what is actually seen by the
investigator, thus scale; angles of view, color and light should be as nearly as
possible to what is seen.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Photographs must be taken as follows (minimum): Establishing
Photograph--This picture should show the scene, the approaches to the
scene, and a fixed object in the background to establish the scene. 360-
Degree Photographs--These pictures should show all four sides of the
accident scene that is, looking north, south, east and west. Damage
Photographs--Close-up pictures of damage to all vehicles and items struck
by vehicles involved, to include contact and induced damage.

197 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In order to give effective rights to the person injured or expired in an
accident, Fatal Accidents Act, 1885 was enacted in India. This Act provided a
procedure and a right of named legal heirs to claim compensation from the
person committing negligence. Because of increase in automation and
consequential losses of life and property in accident, it was considered that
to give relief to the victims of accident claims an effective law should be
brought in. To facilitate this, provisions have been inserted for compulsory
third party insurance and to provide a machinery of adjudication of claim in
Motor Vehicle Act by amending Act No.110 of 1956, by which Section 93 to
109 with reference to third party insurance and Section 110(A) to 110(F).

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Reference to creation of Motor Accident Claims Tribunal and procedure
for adjudication of claim has been provided. Initially the liability was
restricted to a particular sum but after 1982 the liability of the Insurance
Company has been made unlimited and even the defenses of the Insurance
Companies have been restricted so as to ensure payment of compensation to
third parties. Scheme of Chapter 10 and 11 of Motor Vehicle Act: Chapter
10 with Sections 140 to 144 provides for interim compensation on ‘No Fault’
Basis unlike to main claim petition, negligence is not required to be proved
and this compensation is not refundable even if negligence is not proved in
the main claim application. Under Chapter 10 for interim award insurer is
not even permitted to raise any defence relating to negligence of applicant or
permitted under Section 149 of Motor Vehicle Act. But, if ultimately it is held
that insurer is not liable to pay compensation victims can receive it from
owner.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Q According to this provision Rs. 50,000/- is to be given to the kith and kin
of the deceased and Rs. 25,000/- to the grievously injured victim. The
compensation under Section 140 is made payable if prima facie evidence
of following is available;

(1) Accident by the offending vehicle;
(2) Offending vehicle being insured;
(3) Death or grievous injuries have been caused.
Third party insurance: Policy that covers civil liabilities to third parties,

Q Arising from bodily injury, property damage, or other wrongs due to the
action or inaction of the insured.

Q It covers only civil liabilities and not criminal liabilities.
Definition. Liability insurance purchased by an insured (the first party)
Q From an insurer (the second party)

Q For protection against the claims of another (the third)party.

198 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Q The first party is responsible for its own damages or losses whether
caused by itself or the third party.

Q Third party insurance is the legal minimum.

QO This means you're covered if you have an accident causing damage or
injury to any other person, vehicle, animal or property.

Q It doesn't cover any other costs like repair to your own vehicle.

C. SOLATIUM FUND TO COMPENSATE ACCIDENT VICTIMS IN HIT AND
RUN CASES:

1.In Hit &Run cases, accident victims are eligible for compensation
through a special fund constituted in terms of section 163 of the Motor
vehicles Act, 1988 called “Solatium Fund”.

2. The amount of compensation is Rs 25,000/- in the event of death.
3. And Rs.12, 500/- for grievous injuries.

Press statement given by government of India in connection with
Solatium Fund:

PRESS INFORMATION BUREAU
GOVERNMENT OF INDIA

SOLATIUM FUND TO COMPENSATE ACCIDENT VICTIMS IN HIT AND RUN
CASES New Delhi, Sharavana 21,1933 August 12,2011.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
In “Hit & Run ”cases, accident victims are eligible for compensation
through a special fund constituted in terms of section 163 of the Motor
Vehicles Act,1988 called ‘Solatium Fund’. The amount of compensation is
25,000/- in the event of death and 12,500/- for grievous injuries. a portion
of the Gross written premium is contributed towards this fund every year by
both public and private insurers. However, in case the vehicle is without
insurance, the victim/dependents has the right to claim compensation from
the owner/driver of road transport & highways (MoRTH) has recommended
that the certificate of insurance should be co- terminus with the validity of
certificate of Registration of The Motor Vehicle. The MoRTH has informed
that there is no proposal to levy one time insurance premium from new
vehicles to compensate road accident victims. This information was given by
the Minister of state for Finance, Shri Namo Narain Meena in written reply to

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
the Minister of state for Finance, Shri Namo Narain Meena in written reply to
an Unstarred Question in Lok Sabha today.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
FIRST AID:

1. FIRSTAID: it means immediate treatment given to the victim, before
medical help is obtained.

199 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. THE AIMS:
a) To preserve life.
b) To promote recovery.
c) To prevent worsening of the causality‘s conditions.

Arrange transportation to hospital, (if necessary), (First aid attending
person should observe carefully, think clearly and act quickly. He
should be cool, calm and confident. He should not excite should give first
Aid methodically.)

Golden principles
1. Assessment.
2. Call for help.
3. First aid.
4. Transport.

v¥ Assessment - assessing the extent of severity of injured, conscious/
unconscious.(airway, breathing, circulation)

Y Dealing with unconscious patients — C.P.R Bleeding Fractures CARDIO
PULMONARY RESUSCITATION.

Y C.P.R. is a technique performed in emergency conditions unconscious
state, heart attacks, drowning. This technique involves alternate chest
compressions & artificial breathing at 100 compressions per minute.

Y Bleeding set in recovery position. Raise or elevate the limbs.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Y Bleeding set in recovery position. Raise or elevate the limbs.

¥ Don’t remove the blood clots, press, stop bleed (direct or indirect)
band aid & bandages no unwanted movement.

FIRST AID IMPORTANCE:

¥ Don’t remove the blood clots, press, stop bleed (direct or indirect)
band aid & bandages no unwanted movement.

FIRST AID IMPORTANCE:

First aid is the assistance given to any person suffering a sudden illness
or injury, with care provided to preserve life, prevent the condition from
worsening, and or promote recovery. It includes initial intervention in a
serious condition prior to professional medical help; being available, such as
performing Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) whilst awaiting an
ambulance as well as the complete treatment of minor conditions ,such as
applying a plaster to a cut. First aid is generally performed y the layperson,
with many people trained in providing basic levels of first aid, and others
willing to do so from acquired knowledge. The very first person on the scent
of an accident is unlikely to be a paramedic or a doctor one day it may be
you who has the opportunity to save a life, to help someone make a quick
recovery.

THE POLICE ACT, 1888
(3 of 1888)

An Act to amend the law relating to the Regulation of
Police whereas it is expedient to relax those provisions of Acts for the
regulation of police which restrict the employment of police-officers to be
presidency, province or place of the police-establishment of which they are
members; It is hereby enacted as follows: -

1. Title and extent. -
i. This Act may be called the Police Act, 1888.
ii. It extends to the whole of India.
2. Constitution of police-forces for special purposes. -

i. The Central Government may, by notification in the Official Gazette,
create a special police-district embracing parts of two or more States,
and extend every pail of the said district the powers and jurisdiction of
members of a police-force belonging to a State specified in the
notification.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
ii. The said Police Force shall have, within every part of any State or which
any part is included shall have the powers, duties, privileges and
liabilities which, they have in their own State.

iii. Any member of the police-force whom the Central Government shall
generally or specially empower to act ,subject to any orders shall
exercise within any State any part of which is included in the said
district deemed to be an officer-in-charge of a police-station discharging
the functions of such an officer within the limits of his station.

3. Employment of police-officers beyond the State to which they
belong. - a member of the police force of any State may discharge the
functions of a police-officer in any part of any other State and shall, while so
discharging such functions, be deemed to be a member of the police-force of
that part and be vested with the powers, functions and privileges and be
subject to the liabilities of a police-officer belonging to that police force.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
4. Consent of State Government in exercise of powers and
jurisdiction. - The police of one State to exercise powers and jurisdiction in
any area within another State, not being a railway area, without the consent
of the Government of that other State.

THE POLICE ACT, 1949
ACT NO.64 OF 1949

An Act to provide for the constitution of a general police-district
embracing two or more Contents.

1. Short title, extent and commencement
1. This Act may be called the Police Act, 1949

2. Constitution of general police-district embracing two or more Union
territories

STATEMENT OF OBJECTS AND REASONS

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Each of the centrally-administered areas has, at present, as separate
police force of its own. Ajmer-Merwara, the Andaman and Nicobar Islands,
Coorg and Himachal Pradesh, whose police is in the process of
reorganization, have separate cadres of both officers and men. Delhi has a
separate cadre of head-constables and constables but a common cadre for
Assistant Sub-Inspectors and higher ranks with the East Punjab. Owing to
the small areas and cadres, the police officers have perforce to continue in
the same station or area for a long time and this position is not very
satisfactory. To remedy this, it has become necessary to provide that Police
Officers of Centrally-administered areas are transferred from one area to
another according to the administrative exigencies by creating a common
pool of officers and men which the Chief Commissioners' Provinces may
draw upon, this reducing their dependence upon the Provincial

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
pool of officers and men which the Chief Commissioners' Provinces may
draw upon, this reducing their dependence upon the Provincial
Governments. This common pool of officers may also prove a useful source
of supply to the Central Intelligence Bureau in the recruitment of its staff in
the lower ranks. As the formation of a common cadre of officers for the police
force in Chief Commissioner’ Provinces involves the constitution of a single
police force in all those Provinces under a single Inspector General, this
object can be achieved only by enacting new legislation which may take the
form either of an amendment to the Indian Police Act of 1861 or of an
independent Act. The latter alternative is considered more suitable for the
purpose.

23. Regulating public assemblies and procession and granting licenses
relating to same:-

(1) As occasion requires, regulate the conduct of all assemblies and
procession on the public roads or in public .the public streets or
thorough fares and prescribe routes and the times for the passing of
such processions.

(2) If satisfied that the intention of certain person or any class of persons is
to collect people or take out a procession on any public road or public
street or; thoroughfare which would, if not cause breach of peace, may
require, proclamation, that such persons or class of person s intending
to so collect people or take out a procession shall obtain a license.

(3) Grant a license containing the names of persons to whom the
permission has been accorded and bearing the conditions subject to
which such assembly may be held or such procession may be taken out.

24. Powers assemblies and processions violating conditions of license:-

THE PREVENTION OF DAMAGE TO PUBLIC PROPERTY
ACT, 1984

Property: Central Government, State Government; Local bodies;
Corporations; Partly or fully setup by Govt. funds.

Sec.3. Mischief causing damage to public property.—

1) Whoever commits mischief by doing any act in respect of any public
property, other than public property of the nature referred to in sub-
section (2), shall be punished with imprisonment for a term which may
extend to five years and with fine.

2) Whoever commits mischief by doing any act in respect of any public
property being—

(a) any building, installation or other property used in connection with
the production, distribution or supply of water, light, power or
energy;

(b) any oil installations;

(c) any sewage works;

(d) any mine or factory;

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
(b) any oil installations;

(c) any sewage works;

(d) any mine or factory;

(e) any means of public transportation or of tele-communications, or any
building, installation or other property used in connection therewith,
shall be punished with rigorous imprisonment for a term which shall
not be less than six months, but which may extend to five years and
with fine:

Provided that the court may, for reasons to be recorded in its judgment,
award a sentence of imprisonment for a term of less than six months.

Sec.4. Mischief causing damage to public property by fire or explosive
substance.—Whoever commits an offence under sub-section (1) or sub-
section (2) of section 3 by fire or explosive substance shall be punished with
regorous imprisonment for a term which shall not be less than one year, but
which may extend to ten years and with fine:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
Note: Mischief shall have the same meaning as in sec.425 IPC. Public
property means any property whether immovable or movable which is owned
by or in the possession of, or under the control of central government or
state government or local authority.

page_content:
The Cigarettes and Other Tobacco Products (Prohibition of
Advertisement and Regulation of Trade and Commerce,
Production, Supply and Distribution) Act, 2003

1. Definitions.—In this Act, unless the context otherwise requires,—

(l) "public place" means any place to which the public have access, whether
as of right or not, and includes auditorium, hospital buildings, railway
waiting room, amusement centers, restaurants, public offices, court
buildings, educational institutions, libraries, public conveyances and the
like which are visited by general public but does not include any open
space;

(n) "Smoking", means smoking of tobacco in any form whether in the form
of cigarette, cigar, beedis or otherwise with the aid of a pipe, wrapper or
any other instruments;

(p) "Tobacco products" means the products specified in the Schedule.

2. Prohibition of smoking in a public place.—No person shall smoke in any
public place:

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2. Prohibition of smoking in a public place.—No person shall smoke in any
public place:

Provided that in a hotel having thirty rooms or a restaurant having
seating capacity of thirty persons or more and in the airports, a separate
provision for smoking area or space may be made.

12. Power of entry and search

13. Power to seize

14. Confiscation of package

21. Punishment for smoking in certain places.—

(1) Whoever contravenes the provisions of section 4 shall be punishable with
fine which may extend to two hundred rupees.

(2) An offence under this section shall be compoundable and shall be tried
summarily in accordance with the procedure provided for summary trials
in the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1973 (2 of 1974).

3. Prohibition of smoking in a public place:

1. The owner, proprietor, manager, and supervisor or in charge of the
affairs of a public place shall ensure that:

a. No person smokes in the public place (under his jurisdiction / implied).

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
a. No person smokes in the public place (under his jurisdiction / implied).

b. The board as specified in schedule II is displayed prominently at the
entrance of the public place, in case there are more than on floor, at each
floor including the staircase and entrance to the lift/s at each floor.

214 | Page

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
c. No ashtrays, matches, lighters or other things designed to facilitate
smoking are provided in the public place.

2. The owner, proprietor, manager, supervisor or in charge of the affairs of
a public place shall notify and cause to be displayed prominently the name
of the person(S) to whom a complaint may be made by a person(S) who
observe any person violating the provision of these Rules.

3. Ifthe owner, proprietor, manager, supervisor or the authorized officer of
a public place fails to act on report of such violations, the owner, proprietor,
manager, supervisor or the authorized officer shall be liable to pay fine
equivalent to the number of individual offences.

4. Hotels, restaurants and airports:

1) The owner, proprietor, manager, supervisor or in charge of the affairs of
a hotel having thirty or more rooms or restaurant having seating capacity
of thirty persons or more and the manager of the airport may provide for
a smoking area of space as defined in rule 2(e).

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
2) Smoking area or space shall not be established at the entrance or exit of
the hotel, restaurant and the airport and shall be distinctively marked as
“smoking area” in English and one Indian language, as applicable.

3) A smoking area or space shall be used only for the purpose of smoking
and no other service(s) shall be allowed.

4) The owner, proprietor, manager, supervisor or in charge of the affairs of
a hotel having thirty or more rooms may designate separate smoking
rooms in the manner prescribed as under:

a. All the rooms so designated shall form a separate section in the same
floor or wing, as the case may be. In cased of more than one floors/wings
the room shall be in one floor/wings the room shall be in one floor / wing
as the case may be.

b. All such rooms shall be distinctively marked as “smoking rooms” in
English and on e Indian language as applicable.

metadata.filename: Maintenance of Public Peace and Order (02.04.2024).pdf
metadata.subject: public_order_ethics

page_content:
b. All such rooms shall be distinctively marked as “smoking rooms” in
English and on e Indian language as applicable.

c. The smoke from such room shall be ventilated outside and does not
infiltrate / permeate into the non smoking areas of the hotel including
lobbies and the corridors.

5. Recovery of fine by authorized officers:

(1) The authorized officers mentioned in schedule (I) shall be competent to
act under and compound the offences committed in violation of section 4
of the act.
